# @poukai-inc/ui ŌĆö design system rules for LLM consumers

This file is the authoritative brand contract for `@poukai-inc/ui`. Hand-authored by `poukai-design`. Takes precedence over inline comments, story descriptions, or any generated output (including `dist/llms.txt`). Read before writing, reviewing, or modifying any component, token, or style in this repository.

Consumed via: `cat node_modules/@poukai-inc/ui/dist/llms-full.txt`

---

## Token semantics

All tokens live in `src/tokens/tokens.css` on `:root`. Owned exclusively by `poukai-design`. Engineers read tokens; they never modify the token file. If an implementation requires a value with no token, work stops until `poukai-design` adds it.

### Color

- `--bg: #fbfbfd` ŌĆö Page background (apple.com canvas). NEVER pure `#FFFFFF`; the off-white value gives elevation headroom above the page and allows clean dark-mode inversion. The only surfaces that should be `--bg` are the literal page canvas and elements that need to feel "on the page" (not elevated above it).
- `--bg-elevated: #ffffff` ŌĆö Front-most elevation layer. Reserved exclusively for popovers, sheets, modals, and dialogs. This is the ONLY surface where pure `#FFFFFF` is permitted ŌĆö it reads as "elevated above the page" precisely because the page is `#FBFBFD`. NEVER use `--bg-elevated` for page background or content sections.
- `--surface: #f5f5f7` ŌĆö Recessed elevation (one step below `--bg`). Used for code blocks, quote backgrounds, card fills (RoleCard). NEVER use as body text. NEVER use as a section divider. `--surface` recedes below the page canvas; `--bg-elevated` rises above it.
- `--surface-section: #f8f8fa` ŌĆö Page-section recessed background. Use for full-width alternating section bands on editorial pages only. Subtler than `--surface` (halfway between `--bg` and `--surface`). NEVER use for inline elements (code blocks, card fills) ŌĆö use `--surface` for those. NEVER stack adjacent to `--surface` on the same page. Maximum 5 alternating bands per page.
- `--fg: #1d1d1f` ŌĆö Primary text, wordmark fill, sigil stroke, and the `primary` button background (inverts: `--fg` fill, `--bg` text). The only color allowed for body paragraph text. NEVER use decoratively as a background wash.
- `--fg-muted: #6e6e73` ŌĆö Secondary text: captions, footer copy, eyebrow labels, lede paragraphs, hiredBy lines, Stat provenance. NEVER use for primary calls to action. NEVER use as a disabled-state substitute.
- `--hairline: #d2d2d7` ŌĆö Dividers, 1px rules, borders. Always applied as a border/border-top/border-bottom, NEVER as a background. Never render thicker than `--hairline-w: 1px`.
- `--hairline-soft: #e5e5ea` ŌĆö Quieter decorative separator. Sits between `--hairline` and `--surface` in the neutral ramp. Use where a hairline would be too assertive ŌĆö e.g. between adjacent tiles that already share a surface background. NEVER use for interactive borders or focus rings.
- `--accent: #0071e3` ŌĆö The brandŌĆÖs single accent. Roles: status dot fill, link underline-on-hover (the accent layer grows over the resting hairline underline), focus rings. NEVER use as a surface fill, body text color, or button background. It is a signal color: interactivity and focus only. `--accent` is NEVER visible in a resting state ŌĆö it appears only on hover (animated underline) and on focus-visible (ring).
- `--accent-glow: rgba(0, 113, 227, 0.18)` ŌĆö Transparent halo for the available status dot and `::selection`. NEVER use as a container fill or hover state. Never stack it.
- `--bg-warm-accent: #c0452c` ŌĆö Editorial warm-accent band background (full-bleed orange). Restraint rules in `meta/brand.md`: at most one warm band per page; never adjacent to another `--bg-warm-accent` band. NEVER use for inline elements or component fills.
- `--fg-on-warm: #fdf5f0` ŌĆö Primary text on warm bands. Warm-tinted near-white. The only text color permitted as primary copy over `--bg-warm-accent`. NEVER use over `--bg`.
- `--fg-on-warm-muted: #f5ddd0` ŌĆö Supporting text on warm bands. Use sparingly; see `meta/brand.md` for the contrast ceiling. NEVER use as primary copy.

**Status color tier ŌĆö product feedback only (NOT editorial brand).** Nine tokens for success/error/warning state surfaces (Banner tones, Form invalid borders, future Toast). Distinct from `--bg-warm-accent` (editorial brand orange) by hue: success is green, danger is true red, warning is amber/ochre.

- `--success: #248a3d` ŌĆö Apple systemGreen light. Success confirmations, positive feedback. WCAG AA on `--bg` (#FBFBFD): ~4.7:1. NEVER use as surface fill or non-confirmation indicator.
- `--bg-success: #f0faf3` ŌĆö Tinted page-tier surface (subtle green). Used by Banner success background. NEVER substitute for `--bg`.
- `--fg-on-success: #0d3d1e` ŌĆö Deep forest for text on `--bg-success` (~10.5:1). Use only on `--bg-success` surfaces.
- `--danger: #b3261e` ŌĆö True red. Used by Form invalid border (`Input`/`Textarea`/`Field` `[aria-invalid="true"]`), Banner danger left-rule, future destructive button variant. WCAG AA on `--bg` (#FBFBFD): ~7.0:1. NEVER use as surface fill, body text, or non-error indicator.
- `--bg-danger: #fef0f0` ŌĆö Tinted page-tier surface (subtle pink). Used by Banner danger background. NEVER use as primary page background; this is reserved for the danger-banner surface.
- `--fg-on-danger: #5c1310` ŌĆö Deep oxblood for text on `--bg-danger` (~9.1:1). Use only on `--bg-danger` surfaces.
- `--warning: #b46100` ŌĆö Amber/ochre. Used by Banner warning left-rule, future caution indicators. Distinct from `--bg-warm-accent` (#C0452C, brick-red editorial) ŌĆö different hue family. WCAG AA on `--bg` (#FBFBFD): ~4.6:1.
- `--bg-warning: #fff7e6` ŌĆö Tinted page-tier surface (light cream). Used by Banner warning background. NEVER substitute for `--bg-warm-accent`; the warm-accent band is editorial, this is product feedback.
- `--fg-on-warning: #4a2900` ŌĆö Deep brown for text on `--bg-warning` (~10.2:1). Use only on `--bg-warning` surfaces.

**Elevation rhythm.** Four steps, low to high: `--surface (#F5F5F7) < --surface-section (#F8F8FA) < --bg (#FBFBFD) < --bg-elevated (#FFFFFF)`. Use `--surface` for recessed inline elements (code blocks, quote blocks, card fills). Use `--surface-section` for full-width alternating section bands on editorial pages ŌĆö this is the only tier permitted as a section divider. Use `--bg` for the page canvas and page-level content. Use `--bg-elevated` for overlays that must read as front-most. Never skip steps. Never use `--surface` as a section divider.

**Never pure edges.** `--fg` is never `#000000`; `--bg` is never pure `#FFFFFF`. Both ends of the neutral ramp pull in slightly ŌĆö this gives elevation headroom on the page and ensures the palette inverts cleanly to dark mode without losing its restraint. Treat any spec or hardcoded value that implies a pure edge as a brand-level escalation.

### Dark mode

Dark mode is implemented as a `@media (prefers-color-scheme: dark)` block appended to `src/tokens/tokens.css`. It overrides every color token on `:root`. No component CSS changes ŌĆö every component that consumes `var(--token)` references gains dark mode automatically. Full rationale in `meta/design/dark-mode.md`.

**Philosophy.** Dark mode is not an inversion of the light palette. It is a parallel surface stack: backgrounds darken progressively toward black (the floor); foregrounds brighten toward near-white (never pure). The elevation rhythm inverts direction ŌĆö surfaces RISE through lighter grays as they elevate, rather than toward white. `html { color-scheme: light dark }` is declared so browser-native controls (scrollbars, form inputs, selection) match the token-driven scheme.

**Dark-mode token table.**

| Token | Dark value | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `--bg` | `#000000` | Page floor. Apple canonical dark-mode canvas. |
| `--bg-elevated` | `#1C1C1E` | Popovers, sheets, dialogs. Apple iOS `systemBackground` dark. |
| `--surface` | `#1C1C1E` | Recessed inline blocks. Same raw value as `--bg-elevated`; semantically distinct by context. |
| `--surface-section` | `#161618` | Section bands. Slightly lighter than `--bg`, preserving the recessed-band rhythm. |
| `--fg` | `#F5F5F7` | Primary text. Near-white (10/255 off pure). Contrast on `--bg`: **19.58:1** (AAA). |
| `--fg-muted` | `#86868B` | Secondary text. Apple `secondaryLabel` dark. Contrast on `--bg`: **6.10:1** (AA). |
| `--hairline` | `#2C2C2E` | Dividers. Apple `separator` dark value. |
| `--hairline-soft` | `#3a3a3c` | Quieter decorative separator in dark mode ŌĆö sits between `--hairline` and `--surface` in the dark neutral ramp. |
| `--accent` | `#0A84FF` | Links, focus rings. Apple `systemBlue` dark. Contrast on `--bg`: **5.86:1** (AA; Ōēź3:1 required for non-text). |
| `--accent-glow` | `rgba(10,132,255,0.24)` | Halo / selection. Higher opacity compensates for dark surface absorption. |
| `--bg-warm-accent` | `#8A2E1C` | Editorial band ŌĆö dimmed to ~55% luminance of light value ("deep ember"). |
| `--fg-on-warm` | `#FDF5F0` | Unchanged. Contrast on dark `--bg-warm-accent`: **7.75:1** (AAA). |
| `--fg-on-warm-muted` | `#D6B9A8` | Contrast on dark `--bg-warm-accent`: **~4.8:1** (AA). |
| `--danger` | `#FF453A` | Apple `systemRed` dark. |
| `--bg-danger` | `#1C0A0A` | Near-black, red undertone. |
| `--fg-on-danger` | `#FFB4B0` | Contrast on `--bg-danger`: **11.63:1** (AAA). |
| `--success` | `#30D158` | Apple `systemGreen` dark. Contrast on `--bg`: ~10.9:1 (AAA). |
| `--bg-success` | `#041A09` | Near-black, green undertone. |
| `--fg-on-success` | `#A8F0BE` | Soft mint. Contrast on `--bg-success`: ~13.1:1 (AAA). |
| `--warning` | `#FF9F0A` | Apple `systemOrange` dark. |
| `--bg-warning` | `#1C1408` | Near-black, amber undertone. |
| `--fg-on-warning` | `#FFD6A0` | Contrast on `--bg-warning`: **13.74:1** (AAA). |

**Contrast budget (all met).**

| Pair | Ratio | Threshold | Result |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--fg` on `--bg` | 19.58:1 | 7:1 (AAA body) | AAA |
| `--fg-muted` on `--bg` | 6.10:1 | 4.5:1 (AA normal) | AA |
| `--accent` on `--bg` | 5.86:1 | 3:1 (non-text) | AA normal |
| `--fg-on-warm` on `--bg-warm-accent` | 7.75:1 | 4.5:1 | AAA |
| `--fg-on-warm-muted` on `--bg-warm-accent` | ~4.8:1 | 4.5:1 | AA |
| `--fg-on-success` on `--bg-success` | ~13.1:1 | 4.5:1 | AAA |
| `--fg-on-danger` on `--bg-danger` | 11.63:1 | 4.5:1 | AAA |
| `--fg-on-warning` on `--bg-warning` | 13.74:1 | 4.5:1 | AAA |

**Component audit.** All shipped atoms, molecules, and organisms consume only `var(--token)` references and gain dark mode for free. One known exception: `Dialog.module.css` scrim (`rgb(0 0 0 / 0.4)`) is intentionally inline per its spec ŌĆö acceptable in dark mode but flagged for future opacity review.

### Typography

- `--font-serif: "Instrument Serif", Georgia, ŌĆ”` ŌĆö Editorial display face. Used for h1, h2, RoleCard titles, Principle titles, FailureMode titles, Stat numeral. NEVER use for UI chrome.
- `--font-sans: "Geist", -apple-system, ŌĆ”` ŌĆö Primary UI face. All body copy, navigation, buttons, captions, badges, eyebrows. Self-hosted in src/tokens/fonts/. NEVER substitute another sans-serif.
- `--font-mono: "Geist Mono", ui-monospace, ŌĆ”` ŌĆö Used for Stat source lines and RoleCard eyebrow labels. Encodes "reference" or "label" semantics.
- `--fs-tagline: clamp(2.25rem, 1.5rem + 3.5vw, 4.25rem)` (36-68px) ŌĆö The h1 display scale. Used exactly once per page for the Hero title.
- `--fs-tagline-intimate: clamp(2rem, 1.25rem + 2.5vw, 3.25rem)` (32-52px) ŌĆö Hero title at `size="intimate"`. Use only inside the Hero molecule. Never use directly on `h1` outside Hero context.
- `--fs-statement: clamp(1.75rem, 1.25rem + 2vw, 2.75rem)` (28-44px) ŌĆö Italic-serif editorial Statement block. Sits between `h2` and `--fs-tagline-intimate`. Used only by the `<Statement>` molecule; not a heading replacement and does not emit any heading element.
- `--fs-pull: clamp(1.25rem, 1rem + 1vw, 1.625rem)` (20-26px) ŌĆö Inline editorial pull-quote accent. Above `--fs-body`, below `--fs-statement`. Used only by the `<Pull>` molecule. Fills the gap between body and Statement; a distinct semantic rung with reuse potential for future inline callout components. No `size` variant ŌĆö one scale carries.
- `--fs-body: clamp(1.0625rem, 1rem + 0.3vw, 1.1875rem)` (17-19px) ŌĆö Primary reading size.
- `--fs-meta: 0.875rem` (14px) ŌĆö Secondary text: captions, nav labels, badge text, hiredBy lines.
- `--fs-micro: 0.75rem` (12px) ŌĆö Uppercase labels only. Always paired with letter-spacing: 0.04em and text-transform: uppercase.
- `--fs-stat: clamp(2.75rem, 2rem + 3vw, 4.5rem)` (44-72px) ŌĆö Stat numeral at size="md".
- `--fs-stat-large: clamp(3.5rem, 2.25rem + 5vw, 6rem)` (56-96px) ŌĆö Stat numeral at size="lg". One per section at most.
- `--tracking-stat: -0.015em` ŌĆö Negative letter-spacing for display numerals in Stat only.
- `--tracking-micro: 0.04em` ŌĆö Canonical tracking for micro/uppercase labels. Used by `.micro` global class, `Stat` source line, and `Hero` eyebrow (variant="no-title"). Always paired with `text-transform: uppercase` and `--fs-micro`.
- `--tracking-eyebrow: 0.06em` ŌĆö Canonical letter-spacing for all Eyebrow labels. Used by `<Eyebrow>`, `RoleCard` eyebrow, `FieldNote` label. At `--fs-meta` (14px) = 0.84px per letter ŌĆö enough to communicate uppercase structure without tipping airy.
- `--tracking-numeric: 0.08em` ŌĆö Wider tracking for sequential numeric markers. Used by `FailureMode` index (`.index`). Slightly wider than eyebrow tracking to visually separate reference labels from editorial labels.
- `--lh-body: 1.55` ŌĆö Canonical body line-height. Applied to global `body`, `RoleCard` body, `Statement` supporting, `FieldNote` body, `LinkCard` body, `FeatureCard` body, `TeamCard` bio, `Dialog` description and body. The default body reading rhythm.
- `--lh-body-relaxed: 1.6` ŌĆö Relaxed body line-height for longer-form editorial prose. Applied to `Principle` content and `FailureMode` body ŌĆö intentionally looser than `--lh-body` to give the longer editorial paragraphs more air. Do not use for UI chrome or short card copy.
- `--lh-meta: 1.2` ŌĆö Line-height for meta/eyebrow-scale text. First named line-height token in the system, scoped to the meta register. Matches the value already used on `h2`, `h3`, and `.numeral` throughout the token file.
- `--fs-card-title: clamp(1.5rem, 1.15rem + 1.2vw, 2rem)` (24-32px) ŌĆö Card-title rung. Shared by `<RoleCard>`, `<Principle>`, `<FailureMode>` titles. Always rendered in `--font-serif`, `font-weight: 400`. Do NOT use as a heading-level replacement; the title element semantics still come from the consuming page.

### Spacing (4px base grid)

- `--space-1`: 0.25rem (4px) ŌĆö Tight: icon-to-label gap
- `--space-2`: 0.5rem (8px) ŌĆö Inline gap: eyebrow-to-title
- `--space-3`: 0.75rem (12px) ŌĆö Badge dot-to-text, Stat column gap
- `--space-4`: 1rem (16px) ŌĆö Body paragraph margin, card internal rhythm
- `--space-6`: 1.5rem (24px) ŌĆö Hero status-to-title, nav gap, card padding tier
- `--space-8`: 2rem (32px) ŌĆö Hero title-to-lede, Principle padding
- `--space-10`: 2.5rem (40px) ŌĆö Editorial bump above `--space-8` (h1 desktop bottom margin, Hero CTA desktop top margin, FailureMode top padding)
- `--space-12`: 3rem (48px) ŌĆö Principle desktop padding, main content top
- `--space-16`: 4rem (64px) ŌĆö Large section gaps
- `--space-24`: 6rem (96px) ŌĆö Main content bottom padding
- `--space-32`: 8rem (128px) ŌĆö Maximum editorial breathing room

Other gaps in the scale (--space-5, --space-7, --space-9, --space-11, etc.) do not exist. NEVER use raw pixel values.

### Layout

- `--page-pad: clamp(1.5rem, 2vw + 1rem, 3rem)` ŌĆö Horizontal padding for SiteShell header/footer. The only token that governs the outer page edge.
- `--content-max: 64rem` (1024px) ŌĆö Maximum width of the main content column. NEVER exceed in the main column layout.
- `--hero-max: 38rem` (608px) ŌĆö Maximum width of the Hero text column. Applies at all widths. NEVER override with a wider value.
- `--hero-illustration-max: 25rem` (400px) ŌĆö Maximum width of the Hero illustration column when the `illustration` slot is active. Applies above the 720px breakpoint; the illustration column is hidden below that.

### Motion

- `--easing: cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1)` ŌĆö Expo-out for entrance animations.
- `--easing-link: cubic-bezier(0.2, 0, 0, 1)` ŌĆö Link underline grow animation.
- `--dur-press: 80ms` ŌĆö Tactile click/press feedback (button `:active` transform, immediate-response interactions). Deliberately shorter than `--dur-fast` so the press lands without lag.
- `--dur-fast: 180ms` ŌĆö Micro-interactions.
- `--dur-mid: 240ms` ŌĆö Standard transition.
- `--dur-slow: 600ms` ŌĆö Entrance animations.
- `--dur-pulse: 1800ms` ŌĆö `<StatusBadge status="available">` halo pulse (intentionally slow + meditative, paired with `ease-out`).

All motion is gated by `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` via a `:root !important` block. This block is the ONLY permitted use of `!important` in the codebase.

### Radii and rules

- `--radius-1: 2px` ŌĆö Focus rings only.
- `--radius-2: 4px` ŌĆö Button corners and the icon well in RoleCard.
- `--radius-3: 8px` ŌĆö RoleCard container.
- `--hairline-w: 1px` ŌĆö Universal border width. NEVER use 2px or more for decorative borders.

### Button height ladder

The `<Button>` height ladder is part of the brand contract. Four named rungs, hand-tuned, not interpolated.

- `--btn-h-sm: 32px` ŌĆö `size="sm"` min-height. Minor inline action.
- `--btn-h-compact: 40px` ŌĆö `size="compact"` min-height. Editorial CTA ŌĆö quieter than the page default. Apple's "Compact" rung.
- `--btn-h-md: 44px` ŌĆö `size="md"` min-height. Page-default CTA. The only rung that passes WCAG 2.5.5 AAA at threshold.
- `--btn-h-lg: 52px` ŌĆö `size="lg"` min-height. Loud CTA.

Padding and font-size stay hand-tuned per size (no separate padding-token scale). Consumed by `src/atoms/Button/Button.module.css`; available to any future primitive (form fields, segmented controls) that needs to align to the same height ladder.

### Icon size scale (4px base grid)

Four named rungs. Semantically distinct from `--space-*` ŌĆö icon dimensions are not rhythm values. Consumed by `<Icon>` atom via a `SIZE_MAP` lookup. NEVER use raw pixel values for icon sizing; reference these tokens.

- `--icon-xs: 12px` ŌĆö compact pill / dense label row (e.g. inside `<Tag>`, inside `<Eyebrow>`)
- `--icon-sm: 16px` ŌĆö default inline icon; pairs with `--fs-meta` (14px) text. The most common rung.
- `--icon-md: 20px` ŌĆö mid-weight; pairs with `--fs-body` (17ŌĆō19px) text or stands alone in a row
- `--icon-lg: 24px` ŌĆö large standalone; navigation rows, feature-card icon slots, prominent markers

Default `size` on `<Icon>` is `"sm"` (16px). Lucide's SVG grid is designed for 16px placement. No rung above 24px exists on the atom; display-scale icons at 32px+ are molecule-level layout decisions.

---

## Component recommended usages

### Wordmark
- Min height: 40px. SiteShell default is height={56}.
- Aspect ratio locked at ~4.08:1 (viewBox 0 0 1184 290). Set only `height`; width is auto.
- Do NOT render below height={40}. Do NOT add a second Wordmark inside SiteShell's children. Do NOT use as a watermark. Do NOT set label="" (it is the a11y name).
- Path geometry changes require Arian's approval (ADR-0011).

### StatusBadge
- Maximum 1 instance per page. Encodes consultant/service availability ŌĆö not system status.
- status="available" triggers pulse animation automatically. Do NOT add additional CSS animation.
- prefers-reduced-motion disables the pulse at the CSS level ŌĆö do NOT force-animate via JS or inline styles.
- Children: single short clause, 10 words or fewer.

### Button
- Maximum one variant="primary" per visual section.
- Labels always sentence-case, never ALL CAPS.
- Min heights: size="sm" 32px, size="compact" 40px, size="md" 44px, size="lg" 52px. Default is size="md".
- size="compact" is the editorial CTA ŌĆö quieter than md, louder than sm. WCAG 2.5.5 AAA tap target (44px) requires md or lg; sm and compact are AA tiers.
- Use asChild with an <a> element for link-styled buttons. Do NOT nest a <button> inside Button with asChild.

### IconButton
- Square, icon-only sibling of Button. Same variant union (primary / secondary / ghost) and same size ladder (sm 32 / compact 40 / md 44 / lg 52) driven by the shared --btn-h-* tokens.
- `aria-label` is REQUIRED at the type level ŌĆö IconButton without an accessible name is a TypeScript error. The label is mirrored into a VisuallyHidden child as belt-and-suspenders for assistive-tech variants that prefer inner text.
- Icon size resolves automatically per Button size: sm ŌåÆ 16px, compact / md ŌåÆ 20px, lg ŌåÆ 24px. No consumer override.
- Composes Button + Icon + VisuallyHidden. No `asChild` in v1 ŌĆö IconButton always renders a `<button>`. WCAG AAA tap-target posture same as Button: md and lg pass at 44px, sm and compact are AA only.
- Use for icon-only affordances where the glyph is unambiguous (close, copy, prev/next). For icon+label, use Button with a child Icon, not IconButton.

### Checkbox
- Wraps `@radix-ui/react-checkbox`. Owns ONLY the control + indicator ŌĆö label pairing, helper text, error messaging, and `htmlFor` wiring are the molecule layer's job.
- Fixed 16├Ś16px (`--icon-sm`). No `size` prop. Radius `--radius-1` (2px).
- States: unchecked (bg `--bg`, border `--hairline`) / checked (bg `--fg`, Lucide `Check` glyph) / indeterminate (bg `--fg`, Lucide `Minus` glyph). Hover (unchecked, pointer): border ŌåÆ `--fg-muted`. Disabled: opacity 0.5. `aria-invalid="true"`: border ŌåÆ `--danger`.
- `checked` prop is Radix's `CheckedState` (`boolean | "indeterminate"`). Supports controlled (`checked` + `onCheckedChange`) and uncontrolled (`defaultChecked`).
- Standalone usage fails WCAG 2.5.8 (24px) and 2.5.5 (44px) hit-target rules ŌĆö practical hit area is control + label, owned by the molecule. Consumers using bare Checkbox must extend the click target externally.
- Canonical state contract for tests is Radix's `data-state` attribute (`checked` / `unchecked` / `indeterminate`), not icon DOM presence.

### Switch
- Wraps `@radix-ui/react-switch`. Use when a setting has a persistent on/off state that takes effect immediately ŌĆö not for one-shot confirmations (use `<Button>`), not for single-item form selection (use `<Checkbox>`).
- Import: `import { Switch } from "@poukai-inc/ui"` or `import { Switch } from "@poukai-inc/ui/atoms/Switch"`.
- Track: 32├Ś18px pill. Thumb: 14px circle. Travel: `translateX(0) ŌåÆ translateX(14px)` via CSS transform on `[data-state="checked"]`. No `left` animation.
- Off: track bg `--surface`, border `--hairline`. On: track bg `--fg`, border transparent. Hover (unchecked, pointer): border ŌåÆ `--fg-muted`. Focus-visible: 2px `--accent` outline on track. Disabled: `opacity 0.5`. Reduced-motion: global `tokens.css` clamps transition ŌĆö state preserved, animation removed.
- Supports controlled (`checked` + `onCheckedChange`) and uncontrolled (`defaultChecked`). Forwards all native `<button>` props via `...rest`. `forwardRef` to root button.
- Label always external ŌĆö supply `<label htmlFor={id}>` or `aria-label`. Unlabelled Switch = a11y violation.
- Canonical state contract for tests: Radix `data-state` (`"checked"` / `"unchecked"`).
- Example:
  ```tsx
  // Uncontrolled with sibling label
  <label htmlFor="notifications">Enable notifications</label>
  <Switch id="notifications" defaultChecked />

  // Controlled
  <Switch checked={enabled} onCheckedChange={setEnabled} aria-label="Enable feature" />
  ```

### Heading
- Canonical heading atom. Owns the H1 ŌåÆ H6 type ramp. Decoupled `as` (rendered HTML element, drives the document outline) and `size` (visual rank, drives type styling). `size` defaults to whatever `as` is, so `<Heading>Foo</Heading>` renders an `<h2>` styled at h2 rank with no extra props.
- `as` (default `"h2"`) ŌĆö closed union of `"h1" | "h2" | "h3" | "h4" | "h5" | "h6"`. An explicit H1 must be opt-in (the default does not auto-promote to H1; one H1 per page is the consumer's responsibility).
- `size` (optional) ŌĆö independent of `as`. Use when document outline and visual rank diverge: `<Heading as="h1" size="h2">` keeps the page H1 semantics while sizing the text at h2 rank.
- Owns no margin. Consumer molecule owns spacing between Heading and adjacent siblings.
- Do NOT use Heading for non-heading editorial display (statements, taglines). Use `<Statement>` for editorial italic-serif blocks; use `<Hero>` for the page tagline.
- Do NOT pass arbitrary HTML elements via `as` ŌĆö the union is closed at the type level.

### Text
- Canonical paragraph atom. Resolves raw `<p>`, `.lede`, and inline muted `<p style>` patterns into one component with orthogonal `size` and `tone` axes.
- `size` (default `"body"`): `"body"` (`--fs-body` + `--lh-body`), `"lede"` (`--fs-body` + `--lh-body-relaxed`, capped at `max-inline-size: 36rem`), `"caption"` (`--fs-meta` + `--lh-meta`), `"micro"` (`--fs-micro` + `--lh-meta`). `"micro"` is NOT uppercase ŌĆö use `<Eyebrow>` for uppercase micro-labels.
- `tone` (default `"default"`): `"default"` (`--fg`), `"muted"` (`--fg-muted`), `"on-warm"` (`--fg-on-warm`), `"on-warm-muted"` (`--fg-on-warm-muted`). The two `on-warm-*` tones are exclusively for the warm editorial band.
- `as` (default `"p"`) ŌĆö closed union of `"p" | "span" | "div" | "dt" | "dd" | "li"`. No heading elements: use `<Heading>` or a raw `<h*>` for those.
- Owns no margin. Consumer owns spacing.
- Do NOT use Text for headings, eyebrows, or status ŌĆö those have their own atoms.

### Prose
- Typographic context wrapper for long-form HTML. Drop arbitrary `<h1>`ŌĆō`<h6>`, `<p>`, `<ul>`, `<ol>`, `<blockquote>`, `<code>`, `<pre>`, `<hr>`, `<figure>`, `<table>` markup inside and the DS type contract applies via low-specificity `:where(...)` descendant rules. Consumer overrides always win.
- `width` (default `"full"`): `"full"` inherits the column from the parent layout (no max-width). `"reading"` caps the column at `64ch` and centers horizontally ŌĆö canonical editorial body for standalone article pages.
- `asChild` (default `false`) ŌĆö when `true`, renders a Radix `<Slot>` and merges all props onto the single child (e.g. `<article>`). Consumer is responsible for passing exactly one child.
- Use Prose when content is authored as raw HTML (CMS output, MDX, markdown render) ŌĆö NOT for hand-composed atom layouts. Hand-composed pages compose `<Heading>` + `<Text>` directly.
- Do NOT nest Prose inside Prose. Do NOT use Prose to style standalone atoms ŌĆö it is a long-form HTML context, not a typography utility.

### Code
- Inline code chip ŌĆö communicates that the wrapped content is a literal token (variable name, CSS custom property, HTML element, shell command, config key).
- Root is always `<code>` (non-polymorphic). Non-interactive ŌĆö no hover, focus, or active states.
- Font size is relative (`0.9em`) so the chip tracks the surrounding text register on any surface.
- `children` is the literal content. Typically a plain string; ReactNode accepted for parity with the rest of the atomic system.
- Do NOT use Code for multi-line code blocks ŌĆö use a `<pre><code>` composition (or future `CodeBlock` primitive). Code is inline-only.
- Do NOT use Code for keyboard keys ŌĆö use `<Kbd>`. Code is for code literals; Kbd is for physical keys.

### Kbd
- Keyboard-key glyph ŌĆö a compact chip that represents a physical key (`Ōīś`, `K`, `Enter`, `Esc`). Used inline in onboarding copy, command-menu shortcut hints, help text, and menu rows.
- Root is always `<kbd>` (non-polymorphic). Non-interactive ŌĆö no hover, focus, or active states.
- `children` is the key content. Typically a single character (`"K"`), a symbol (`"Ōīś"`), or a short token (`"Enter"`, `"Esc"`).
- For symbol keys whose glyph does not announce well in screen readers, pass a spelled-out name via `aria-label` (e.g. `<Kbd aria-label="Command">Ōīś</Kbd>`).
- Multi-key combinations are composed by the consumer as side-by-side `<Kbd>` instances (e.g. `<Kbd>Ōīś</Kbd> <Kbd>K</Kbd>`).
- Do NOT use Kbd for code literals ŌĆö use `<Code>`.

### SkipLink
- Keyboard skip-to-content anchor. Visually hidden at rest (canonical sr-only clip pattern), becomes a fixed-position high-contrast pill when keyboard-focused. Required first focusable element in any layout shell.
- Root is always `<a>`, non-polymorphic. No `as` prop ŌĆö fragment navigation is anchor semantics, full stop. Composes neither `<Link>` nor `<VisuallyHidden>` ŌĆö owns its own clip + focus CSS in one module so the `:focus-visible` un-clip rule does not fight cross-module specificity.
- `href` (required string) ŌĆö the fragment target (e.g. `#main`, `#docs-content`). Consumer supplies; the DS does not compute or default. The target element must exist in the DOM with the matching `id`.
- `children` (optional ReactNode, default `"Skip to content"`) ŌĆö override for layouts with multiple navigable regions (e.g. `<SkipLink href="#docs-content">Skip to documentation</SkipLink>`).
- Focused pill geometry: `position: fixed`, `top: var(--space-2)`, `left: var(--space-2)`, `z-index: 9999` (above Dialog 100/101 and Toast 200 ŌĆö skip link must never be occluded). `background: var(--fg)`, `color: var(--bg)` (DS-wide max-contrast inversion pair, ~16:1, AAA in light and dark mode). `padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-4)`, `border-radius: var(--radius-2)`, `font-size: var(--fs-meta)`, `font-weight: 500`, `box-shadow: 0 4px 12px rgb(0 0 0 / 0.08)` (same elevated-chip shadow as Toast), `outline: none` (the pill IS the focus indicator ŌĆö documented deviation from the global `a:focus-visible` accent ring).
- No motion. No transition. No `:hover` style (skip link is keyboard-only by design ŌĆö mouse users never see it). Reduced-motion: nothing to suppress.
- a11y: zero axe violations rest + focused. No `aria-*` required on root. Must be the first focusable element when placed at the top of a layout shell (before nav, header, logo, or any interactive chrome).
- Usage: place as first child of the layout shell. Note: SiteShell consumer migration is a follow-up audit PR ŌĆö this atom ships standalone.
- `forwardRef<HTMLAnchorElement, SkipLinkProps>`, `displayName="SkipLink"`. Remaining `AnchorHTMLAttributes<HTMLAnchorElement>` spread onto the root.

### Mark
- Editorial highlight chip ŌĆö an inline `<mark>` that flags a run of prose as the part the reader should notice. Tints the background with `--accent-glow` (the same value the system uses for `::selection`) so the highlight reads as kindred to selected prose rather than as a foreign decoration.
- Root is always `<mark>` (non-polymorphic). Non-interactive ŌĆö no hover, focus, or active states.
- Inherits `font-family`, `font-size`, `font-weight`, `line-height`, and `color` from the surrounding context, so the chip scales with parent text on every surface (body copy, pull-quote, field note, Prose, warm editorial band).
- `children` is the highlighted content. Typically a short prose fragment; ReactNode accepted for parity with the rest of the atomic system.
- `box-decoration-break: clone` (plus the WebKit prefix) ensures the highlight tiles cleanly across line wraps ŌĆö each line-fragment gets its own padding and rounded corners.
- Token contract: `--accent-glow` (background) + `--space-1` (inline padding) + `--radius-1` (2px corner). No new tokens.
- Do NOT use Mark for inline code literals ŌĆö use `<Code>`. Do NOT use Mark for keyboard keys ŌĆö use `<Kbd>`. Do NOT use Mark for status indication (error/warning/success) ŌĆö use `<Banner>` or `<StatusBadge>`.
- Do NOT nest interactive elements inside Mark (no `<button>`, `<a>`, `<input>` children). If the highlighted run must be clickable, the consumer wraps Mark with an anchor that owns the interaction.

### Spacer
- Explicit-gap atom for contexts where flex/grid `gap` cannot reach. NOT a general spacing utility ŌĆö use `gap` or section padding tokens by default.
- `size` (required, closed union `"1" | "2" | "3" | "4" | "6" | "8" | "10"`) maps to `--space-1` ŌĆ” `--space-10`. Layout-tier values (`--space-12`+) are intentionally excluded ŌĆö those belong to template-level rhythm, not an inline atom.
- `axis="block"` (default): `display: block; height: var(--space-N); width: 100%`. `axis="inline"`: `display: inline-block; width: var(--space-N); height: 1em`.
- `as="div"` (default) or `as="span"`. Use `as="span"` with `axis="inline"` inside text flow.
- Always renders `aria-hidden="true"` ŌĆö decorative, ignored by AT. No background, no border, no content.
- Canonical use: between two `<Prose>` siblings where the parent owns no layout; between conditionally-rendered siblings; inside legacy block containers where `gap` is unavailable.
- Do NOT use between flex/grid siblings (use `gap`), for section-tier rhythm (use Section padding tokens), as a vertical typography hack (fix the type ramp), or as a margin replacement on components that own their margin.

### RadioGroup
- Container for a mutually-exclusive radio selection. Wraps `@radix-ui/react-radio-group` Root. Radix provides `role="radiogroup"`, keyboard navigation (roving tabindex), and ARIA propagation at zero cost.
- `value` / `onValueChange` for controlled usage. `defaultValue` for uncontrolled. `name` for native form submission.
- `orientation` (`"horizontal"` | `"vertical"`, default `"vertical"`) ŌĆö vertical: `flex-direction: column`, gap `--space-2`; horizontal: `flex-direction: row`, gap `--space-4`.
- **A11y:** pass either `aria-label` (when no visible heading exists) or `aria-labelledby` (when a visible heading labels the group). In development, a `console.warn` fires when both are absent. Pair with a visible error message linked via `aria-describedby` when in an error state.
- `disabled` disables all items at the group level. `required` marks the group required for form validation.
- `forwardRef` to the root `<div>`. `...rest` spread. `displayName = "RadioGroup"`.
- Tokens consumed: `--space-2`, `--space-4`. No new tokens.
- Do NOT export as `RadioGroup.Root` ŌĆö the public API is a flat `<RadioGroup>` named export. No namespace object.
- Do NOT override Radix keyboard navigation with custom `onKeyDown` handlers ŌĆö arrow-key routing is Radix-native.

### Radio
- Individual radio control inside a `RadioGroup`. Wraps `@radix-ui/react-radio-group` Item. Radix provides `role="radio"`, `aria-checked`, and keyboard routing (roving tabindex) within the group.
- `value` is required ŌĆö binds this item's checked state to the group's selected value.
- Checked indicator: a CSS `::after` pseudo on the Radix `Indicator` slot. 8├Ś8 px circle, `background: var(--fg)`. No Lucide, no SVG, no `<Icon>` component.
- Circle geometry: 16├Ś16 px, `border-radius: 50%`, `border: var(--hairline-w) solid var(--hairline)`, `background: var(--bg)`.
- States: hover (unselected, enabled) ŌåÆ `border-color: var(--fg-muted)`; checked ŌåÆ Radix mounts Indicator, `::after` dot appears; focus-visible ŌåÆ `outline: 2px solid var(--accent); outline-offset: 2px; border-radius: var(--radius-1)`; disabled ŌåÆ `opacity: 0.5; cursor: not-allowed; pointer-events: none`; `aria-invalid="true"` ŌåÆ `border-color: var(--danger)`.
- Label pairing is always the consumer's responsibility ŌĆö `Radio` renders no label text. Wrap with `<label>` or pair via `htmlFor`/`id`.
- `forwardRef` to the root `<button>`. `...rest` spread. `displayName = "Radio"`.
- Tokens consumed: `--bg`, `--fg`, `--fg-muted`, `--hairline`, `--hairline-w`, `--accent`, `--danger`, `--radius-1`, `--dur-fast`, `--easing`. No new tokens.
- Do NOT export as `RadioGroup.Item` ŌĆö the public API is a flat `<Radio>` named export.
- Do NOT add a custom dot SVG, Lucide icon, or `<Icon>` component ŌĆö the CSS `::after` pseudo is the spec-mandated approach.
- Do NOT add `onKeyDown` handlers for arrow keys ŌĆö Radix handles keyboard navigation natively.

### Stat
- Requires a caption ŌĆö numeral alone is not self-explanatory.
- value must be a numeral or numeral+unit string. source should be citation only.
- Maximum 4 Stats per section. Maximum 1 size="lg" per section.

### NumberFormat
- Pure presentational formatter backed by native `Intl.NumberFormat`. Renders a `number` as a locale-correct string and nothing else. NEVER use as a layout or visual primitive ŌĆö it owns no CSS.
- Required `value: number`. Optional `notation`: `"standard"` (default ŌĆö grouped digits), `"compact"` (`1.2M`), `"currency"` (requires `currency`), `"percent"` (pass the decimal fraction, e.g. `0.42` ŌåÆ `42%`).
- `currency` is an ISO 4217 code (`"USD"`, `"EUR"`, `"JPY"`). REQUIRED whenever `notation="currency"` ŌĆö Intl throws a `RangeError` otherwise. Ignored for all other notations.
- `locale` is BCP-47 (`"en-US"`, `"de-DE"`). Omit only for app-default locale; pass explicitly in any i18n-aware surface to guarantee server/client parity. NEVER read `navigator.language` from a consumer ŌĆö pass the resolved locale in.
- `minimumFractionDigits` and `maximumFractionDigits` pass through to Intl unchanged.
- Polymorphic `as`: `"span"` (default) | `"div"` | `"dd"`. Closed union ŌĆö no heading or interactive elements. Use `as="dd"` inside a `<dl>` for definition-list pairings (`<dt>Total users</dt><NumberFormat as="dd" value={48921} />`).
- No `children` prop. Value is the rendered content; `children` is TypeScript-disallowed.
- Edge values: `NaN` ŌåÆ "NaN", `Infinity` ŌåÆ "Ōł×", `-Infinity` ŌåÆ "-Ōł×" (whatever the Intl runtime emits ŌĆö do NOT assert against literals; assert against `Intl.NumberFormat(locale, options).format(value)` for cross-engine stability).
- Accessibility: emits plain text. No ARIA needed. For screen-reader expansion of compact/currency output, wrap with `<VisuallyHidden>` (`<><NumberFormat value={1_200_000} notation="compact" /> <VisuallyHidden>(1.2 million)</VisuallyHidden></>`).
- Non-goals: no date/time formatting, no signed display (no `signDisplay` prop), no scientific/engineering notation, no unit formatting, no animated counters, no copy-to-clipboard. Date formatting is a separate atom.
- Stat migration to use NumberFormat is a follow-up PR ŌĆö NOT shipped with this atom.

### Time
- Semantic `<time datetime>` atom. Non-polymorphic ŌĆö root is always `<time>`. Downstream consumers: `Byline`, `ArticleHeader`, `TimelineItem`.
- `dateTime` prop accepts `string | Date`. String values pass verbatim to the `datetime` attribute (date-only strings such as `"2026-05-21"` are NOT round-tripped through `new Date()`). `Date` objects serialize via `.toISOString()`.
- Four format modes (native `Intl` only ŌĆö no date-fns, dayjs, luxon):
  - `absolute` (default): `Intl.DateTimeFormat` with `{ year: "numeric", month: "short", day: "numeric" }` ŌåÆ `May 21, 2026`.
  - `relative`: `Intl.RelativeTimeFormat` with `{ numeric: "auto" }`. Threshold table (descending, by |diff|): `< 60s` ŌåÆ `"just now"` (English literal); `Ōēź 345d` ŌåÆ year; `Ōēź 28d` ŌåÆ month; `Ōēź 7d` ŌåÆ week; `Ōēź 1d` ŌåÆ day; `Ōēź 1h` ŌåÆ hour; `Ōēź 1min` ŌåÆ minute. Signed value preserves past/future direction.
  - `long`: `Intl.DateTimeFormat` with `{ year: "numeric", month: "long" }` ŌåÆ `May 2026`.
  - `time-only`: `Intl.DateTimeFormat` with `{ hour: "2-digit", minute: "2-digit" }` ŌåÆ `02:30 PM` (en-US) / `14:30` (fr-FR).
- `children` override: when `children` is provided, it replaces the computed label entirely; `format` and `locale` are ignored. The `datetime` attribute still emits the ISO string.
- SSR locale resolution: `locale` prop ŌåÆ `navigator.language` (browser) ŌåÆ `"en-US"` (server). `format="relative"` is NOT guaranteed stable across SSR/hydration ŌĆö use `children` or `format="absolute"` for consistent SSR output.
- Guardrails: no `aria-label` injection, no motion, no color variants, no new tokens. Inherits `color: inherit`. No CSS module.

### Hero
- One per page. Do NOT nest Hero inside another Hero.
- status slot: StatusBadge or equivalent only (default variant only; not available in no-title variant).
- lede: 1-3 sentences at most.
- align="center": standalone landing contexts only.
- size="display" (default): title uses `--fs-tagline` (36-68px). Default for all product, marketing, and feature pages.
- size="intimate": title uses `--fs-tagline-intimate` (32-52px). Opt-in for low-density doorways (homepages, principle lists) where a quieter register suits the context. All other Hero rhythm (gaps, lede, CTA, color, font family, em accent) is unchanged.
- variant="no-title": renders eyebrow + lede only ŌĆö no heading element of any level. Use only when the page's `<h1>` lives in body content. The molecule does not emit an `<h1>`; the consumer must. Eyebrow is rendered at `--fs-micro` (12px), uppercase, `letter-spacing: 0.04em`, `--fg-muted`. The `title` and `status` props are excluded from this variant's type signature. `align`, `entrance`, and `cta` remain available.
- illustration: optional ReactNode slot (added 0.15.0). When provided, Hero switches to a two-column layout at `--bp-md` (768px) and above (text left, illustration right); the illustration column is hidden below 768px (stacked layout: illustration above the title block). Max width of the illustration column is controlled by `--hero-illustration-max` (25rem). Slot is content-agnostic ŌĆö accepts SVG, `<img>`, `<Portrait>`, or any ReactNode. When `entrance="stagger"` is active, the illustration is the fifth stagger slot (index 4, 600ms delay). If decorative, the consumer must pass `aria-hidden="true"` or use `alt=""` on inner imagery. The Hero molecule does not enforce decorative-vs-meaningful semantics on the slot ŌĆö the consumer owns that decision.

### RoleCard
- Eyebrow format: "Role 01", "Role 02" ŌĆö Title Case "Role", zero-padded two-digit numeral.
- hiredBy footer: names separated by " ┬Ę " (middle dot with spaces).
- Min 2 cards per use; max 6 per grid.

### Principle
- Numeral convention: lowercase Roman numerals with trailing period ("i.", "ii.", "iii.").
- Numeral is aria-hidden. Min 2 Principles per use.
- Distinct from `FailureMode`: `Principle.numeral` uses Roman numerals (i, ii, iii) for editorial / philosophical sequencing. The two components encode different semantic registers ŌĆö do not conflate or rename either prop to match the other.

### FailureMode
- index is typed number, auto-formats as zero-padded ("01"). Do NOT pass as string.
- Index span is aria-hidden. Titles should be declarative noun phrases.
- Distinct from `Principle`: `FailureMode.index` + `indexLabel` use Arabic-numeral integer indices with optional formatted labels (e.g. "FM-01") for enumerated failure cases. The two components encode different semantic registers ŌĆö do not conflate or rename either prop to match the other.

### PrincipleList
- Canonical organism for presenting a sequenced set of editorial principles on a page.
- Composes `Section` (molecule) as root + `<ol>` + `<li>`-wrapped `Principle` molecules.
- Props: `heading` (ReactNode, Section title), `eyebrow` (string | ReactNode), `lede` (ReactNode), `size` ("default" | "tight", default "default"), `children` (required ŌĆö two or more `Principle` instances).
- Dividers between items are always present (`border-top: var(--hairline-w) solid var(--hairline)`). No `dividers` toggle prop.
- `<ol>` preserves semantic sequencing ŌĆö screen readers announce item count and position. Do NOT replace with `<ul>`.
- Minimum two `Principle` children. A single-item list has no sequential meaning.
- Static organism. No hover, focus, or dismiss states at this layer.
- Tokens: `--space-8` (li padding-top mobile), `--space-12` (li padding-top desktop), `--hairline`, `--hairline-w`, `--bg` (inherited, no surface override).
- Do NOT use for `FailureMode` sets ŌĆö use `FailureModeList`. Do NOT use for unordered feature grids ŌĆö use `FeatureGrid`.
- `forwardRef<HTMLElement, PrincipleListProps>`, `displayName="PrincipleList"`. Props spread to `Section` root.

### Statement
- Editorial italic-serif block at `--fs-statement` (28-44px), `line-height: 1.2`, `letter-spacing: -0.005em`, `text-wrap: balance`. Not a heading element; emits no `<h1>`ŌĆō`<h6>`.
- `statement` prop is required ReactNode, rendered inside a `<p>`. `supporting` is an optional second `<p>` at `--fs-body` in `--fg-muted`.
- `as="p"` (default) renders the root as a `<div>` wrapping the two paragraphs. `as="blockquote"` renders the root as `<blockquote>` ŌĆö use this only when quoting an attributed external source.
- `hairline={true}` adds a decorative 1px top rule plus `--space-12` padding-top. Default `false`.
- Use sparingly: an editorial statement, not a tagline replacement. The page `<h1>` still lives in `<Hero>`.

### Portrait
- Editorial photography primitive. Renders `<picture>` with AVIF > WebP > JPEG fallback chain and srcset at 4 widths derived from the canonical `width`.
- `alt` is required and must be non-empty. Dev-mode throws on empty alt ŌĆö portraits are never decorative (WCAG 1.1.1). Do NOT pass `alt=""`.
- `aspect` (`1:1` | `3:4` | `4:3` | `16:9` | `9:16`) drives the CSS `aspect-ratio` and the computed `height` attribute. Locked at render time to prevent CLS.
- `width` is the canonical source-image width in px ŌĆö drives the srcset divisors (1, 1.5, 2.34375, 3.75) and the `width`/`height` HTML attrs. Pass the full source resolution, not the display size.
- `loading` defaults to `"lazy"`; use `"eager"` for above-the-fold portraits. `fetchPriority` defaults to `"auto"`; pair `loading="eager"` with `fetchPriority="high"` for the LCP image. `sizes` defaults to `"100vw"`. `objectPosition` defaults to `"center"`.
- `src` accepts either a string URL or an object shaped like Astro's `ImageMetadata` (`.src` is extracted). Do NOT pass `import.meta` imports without unwrapping.

### Image
- Token-aware plain `<img>` atom for logos, screenshots, illustrations, and decorative photography. Non-polymorphic ŌĆö root is always `<img>`. No `as` prop, no wrapper.
- Distinct from `<Portrait>`. Image = single URL, known dimensions, static asset. Portrait = editorial photography with AVIF/WebP/JPEG srcset. Do NOT reach for Image when the asset needs srcset, `<picture>`, or format negotiation ŌĆö that belongs to Portrait.
- `src` (required string) ŌĆö image URL passed directly to the `<img>` src attribute.
- `alt` (required string) ŌĆö required at the type level. Pass a descriptive string for meaningful images (`alt="Poukai logo"`); pass an empty string for decorative images (`alt=""`). NEVER omit ŌĆö the type is `string`, not `string | undefined`.
- `width` (required number) + `height` (required number) ŌĆö the asset's intrinsic pixel dimensions from the source file. Both are required: they drive (1) the HTML `width`/`height` attributes for the browser's pre-CSS layout hint, and (2) the inline `aspect-ratio` style for CLS-safe slot reservation before the image bytes arrive.
- `loading` (`"lazy"` | `"eager"`, default `"lazy"`) ŌĆö controls the browser's native lazy-loading behavior. Use `"eager"` for above-the-fold / LCP images.
- `decoding` (`"async"` | `"sync"` | `"auto"`, default `"async"`) ŌĆö image decoding hint. `"async"` is correct for almost every case; allows off-main-thread decoding without blocking rendering.
- `fit` (`"cover"` | `"contain"` | `"fill"` | `"none"` | `"scale-down"`, optional) ŌĆö maps to `object-fit`. NO DS default ŌĆö when omitted, no `object-fit` style is written and the browser's CSS default (`fill`) applies. Set explicitly when the image occupies a fixed-dimension slot and needs to fill or contain without distortion.
- `radius` (`"none"` | `"sm"` | `"md"` | `"lg"`, default `"none"`) ŌĆö maps to `border-radius`. `"none"` ŌåÆ `0`, `"sm"` ŌåÆ `var(--radius-1)` (2px), `"md"` ŌåÆ `var(--radius-2)` (4px), `"lg"` ŌåÆ `var(--radius-3)` (8px). No new tokens introduced.
- Inline-style baseline (always applied): `max-width: 100%`, `height: auto`, `aspect-ratio: ${width} / ${height}`. The aspect-ratio reservation is the CLS prevention mechanism. Consumer `style` is merged on top ŌĆö caller can override `maxWidth`/`height` if needed.
- `forwardRef<HTMLImageElement, ImageProps>`, `displayName="Image"`. Remaining props spread onto the `<img>` element (`className`, `style`, `data-*`, `aria-*`, `fetchPriority`, etc.).
- Loading state = blank reserved space (from `aspect-ratio`). No skeleton shimmer, no placeholder color. Error state = browser's native broken-image indicator. No `onError`, no fallback URL ŌĆö consumers who need error handling manage it in their own wrapper.
- Composition: wrap in `<a>` for linked images, wrap in `<figure>` + `<figcaption>` for captioned images. Image never emits the wrapper. For person attribution disks (24ŌĆō40px, circular), use `<Avatar>`, not Image.
- Do NOT add `srcset`, `sizes`, or `<picture>` ŌĆö that is Portrait's domain.
- Do NOT add motion, hover effects, or fade-in transitions ŌĆö Image is a static presentational atom.
- Do NOT make `alt` optional. Use `alt=""` for decorative images instead.
- Do NOT use Image for editorial photography with multiple format variants ŌĆö use `<Portrait>`.

### Eyebrow
- Canonical micro-label atom. Uppercase, tracked (`--tracking-eyebrow: 0.06em`), `--font-sans`, `--fs-meta` (14px), weight 500, `--lh-meta` (1.2).
- `variant="muted"` (default) ŌĆö color `--fg-muted`. Standard section/category/role label register.
- `variant="solid"` ŌĆö color `--fg`. Full contrast when the label needs more weight.
- `variant="numbered"` ŌĆö color `--fg-muted` + activates the inline numeral slot (inline-flex, baseline-aligned, `--space-2` gap). Use with `numeral` prop.
- `numeral` prop (string, optional) ŌĆö rendered in a leading `<span>` with `font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums`. e.g. `numeral="FM-03"`, `numeral="01 ┬Ę"`.
- `as` prop ŌĆö polymorphic root element, defaults to `<span>`. Valid values: `span`, `p`, `div`, `dt`, `h2`, `h3`, `h4`, `h5`, `h6`, `li`. Use `as="h2"` when heading semantics are required at eyebrow scale.
- `margin: 0` ŌĆö Eyebrow owns no margin. The consuming molecule owns spacing between Eyebrow and its sibling title.
- Resolves the drift between `RoleCard.eyebrow` (mono, 12px, 0.06em), `FailureMode.index` (sans, 14px, 0.08em), and `.micro` utility (sans, 12px, 0.04em). Migration to consuming molecules is a follow-up PR.
- Do NOT use `--accent` on Eyebrow ŌĆö it is a label, not a link or status indicator.
- Do NOT add truncation or overflow clipping ŌĆö the DS does not manage Eyebrow overflow.

### Label
- Canonical form-label atom. Renders a `<label>` element bound to its control via `htmlFor`. Typed, token-aligned wrapper so every form surface speaks the same typographic and semantic contract.
- Root element: `<label>`. Non-polymorphic ŌĆö no `as` prop. `<label>` is the only semantically correct root for a form label; swapping it would break the `htmlFor` association contract.
- `htmlFor` (required, string) ŌĆö The `id` of the bound control. Type-level required; no default. A `<Label>` without `htmlFor` is a broken label ŌĆö TypeScript enforces this at authoring time.
- `children` (required, ReactNode) ŌĆö Label text. Typically a short string; may include inline `<strong>` or similar emphasis.
- `required` (optional, boolean, default `false`) ŌĆö When `true`, renders `<span aria-hidden="true">*</span>` in `var(--danger)` after the label text. Visual only; the ARIA required contract (`aria-required="true"` / `required`) lives on the bound control, not on this element.
- `tone` (`"default"` | `"muted"`, default `"default"`) ŌĆö text color only. `"default"` ŌåÆ `var(--fg)`. `"muted"` ŌåÆ `var(--fg-muted)`. Required indicator is always `var(--danger)` regardless of tone.
- Typography: `--font-sans`, `--fs-meta` (14px), `font-weight: 500`, `--lh-meta` (1.2), `letter-spacing: 0.01em` (raw value ŌĆö neither `--tracking-eyebrow` nor `--tracking-numeric` fits mixed-case 14px; see spec ┬¦3 rationale).
- Layout: `display: inline-block`, `margin: 0`. Consumer and molecule own all spacing.
- Tokens consumed: `--font-sans`, `--fs-meta`, `--lh-meta`, `--fg`, `--fg-muted`, `--danger`. No new tokens introduced.
- Standalone usage: `<Label htmlFor="email">Email address</Label>` / `<input id="email" type="email" />`. Consumer owns spacing between label and control.
- With `<Field>` molecule: `<Field>` composes `<Label>` internally. Do not pass `<Label>` as a child of `<Field>`.
- Contrast: `--fg` (#1D1D1F) on `--bg` (#FBFBFD) Ōēł 16.1:1 (AAA). `--fg-muted` (#6E6E73) on `--bg` Ōēł 4.9:1 (AA ŌĆö passes WCAG 4.5:1 at 14px/500). `--danger` (#B3261E) on `--bg` Ōēł 7.2:1 (AAA).
- Do NOT default or omit `htmlFor` ŌĆö it is type-level required. A label without `htmlFor` provides no programmatic association.
- Do NOT add an `as` prop. Root is always `<label>`.
- Do NOT add `(optional)` text marker, tooltip slot, icon slot, or inline-with-input layout ŌĆö all out of scope per spec ┬¦10.
- Do NOT add `aria-required` to the `<label>` element ŌĆö it belongs on the bound control only.
- Do NOT use floating-label animation ŌĆö not brand-aligned, excluded permanently.

### EmailLink
- Canonical `mailto:` affordance atom. Not a button ŌĆö it opens a mail client, it does not commit to an outcome. Choose `<EmailLink>` over `<Button asChild><a href="mailto:ŌĆ”">` when the contact register is "reach out" rather than "commit to action."
- `email` (required string) ŌĆö The email address. Component always computes `href="mailto:${email}"`. Consumers NEVER pass `href` directly ŌĆö it is excluded from the prop interface via `Omit<AnchorHTMLAttributes, 'href'>`.
- `label` (optional string) ŌĆö Visible link text. Defaults to the `email` string when omitted. When provided, `label` is the accessible name; the email is the href target only.
- `icon` (optional ReactNode) ŌĆö Leading icon slot. When present, root shifts to `inline-flex` with `align-items: center` and `gap: --space-2`. When absent, root is a plain inline anchor. Use 14px icons in `--fs-meta` contexts, 16px in `--fs-body` contexts. Pass `aria-hidden="true"` on decorative icons.
- `qualifier` (optional string) ŌĆö Trailing role qualifier rendered as ` (qualifier)` in a muted `<span>` inside the anchor. Pass the bare name/role without parentheses. e.g. `qualifier="Arian"` renders "founder@pouk.ai (Arian)".
- `variant="default"` (default) ŌĆö `color: --fg`, hover ŌåÆ `--accent`. Use on `--bg` or `--surface` contexts.
- `variant="muted"` ŌĆö `color: --fg-muted`, hover ŌåÆ `--fg`. For footer, caption, secondary-copy contexts. Matches existing `.muted-link` treatment in `SiteShell`.
- Underline inherits the global `a` two-layer underline rule in `tokens.css`. At rest: a 1px `--hairline` underline is always visible (resting discoverability signal). On hover: a 1px `--accent` underline grows left-to-right over the hairline layer via `background-size` transition (`--dur-mid`, `--easing-link`). `<EmailLink>` is a link ŌĆö same affordance as every other navigational anchor. No underline override.
- Focus ring: `outline: 2px solid --accent`, `outline-offset: 4px` (link convention ŌĆö matches global `a:focus-visible`, NOT Button's 2px offset). `border-radius: --radius-1`.
- `:visited` ŌĆö no distinct treatment (brand decision; visited state creates false scent on contact links).
- `:active` ŌĆö no `translateY` (link, not button).
- No margin on root ŌĆö consumer owns spacing.
- Do NOT use `<EmailLink>` for `tel:` or `sms:` protocols ŌĆö those get their own atoms.
- Do NOT compose `<EmailLink>` inside `<Button>` ŌĆö they are sibling affordances with different registers.
- Do NOT pass `href` ŌĆö it is always computed from `email`. The prop is excluded at the TypeScript level.

### Tag
- Inline categorical pill atom. Communicates type, category, topic, or metadata classification of adjacent content. Answers "what kind of thing is this?" inline with content flow: inside a card, in a topic list, beside a title, or inside a sentence.
- Root element: `<span>`. Non-polymorphic ŌĆö no `as` prop. Always `<span>`. Tags are inline categorical labels; they do not need heading, list-item, or block semantics.
- `children` (required ReactNode) ŌĆö the visible label text. Typically a plain string (one to three words). ReactNode accepted for rare inline `<strong>` emphasis, but a plain string is the idiomatic usage.
- `tone` (`"default"` | `"muted"`, default `"default"`) ŌĆö visual tone. Two tones only; no colored semantic tones.
  - `"default"` ŌĆö `background-color: var(--surface)` (#F5F5F7), `color: var(--fg)` (#1D1D1F), no border. Primary categorical register. Use in cards and topic lists.
  - `"muted"` ŌĆö `background-color: transparent`, `color: var(--fg-muted)` (#6E6E73), `border: var(--hairline-w) solid var(--hairline)` (1px #D2D2D7). Secondary or receding register. Use for draft labels, optional tags, or where the tag must not compete with primary content.
- `icon` (optional ReactNode) ŌĆö optional leading icon slot. When present, root shifts from `display: inline` to `display: inline-flex` with `align-items: center` and `gap: var(--space-2)` (8px). When absent, root is plain `display: inline`. Recommended icon size: 12px (proportional inside a 14px-text pill). Pass `aria-hidden="true"` on all decorative icons ŌĆö the DS does not enforce this at the type level, but it is the required consumer contract.
- Typography: `--font-sans` (Geist), `--fs-meta` (14px fixed), `font-weight: 400`, `line-height: var(--lh-meta)` (1.2), `letter-spacing: normal` (no tracking ŌĆö Tags are mixed-case; tracking is for uppercase labels like Eyebrow).
- Geometry: `border-radius: 999px` (pill ŌĆö a geometric constant, not a brand token; documented in Tag.module.css). `padding-block: var(--space-1)` (4px). `padding-inline: var(--space-2)` (8px). `box-sizing: border-box` ŌĆö ensures muted tone's 1px border is absorbed inside the padding box; pill dimensions are identical across both tones.
- `white-space: nowrap` ŌĆö Tags never wrap internally. If a list of Tags wraps at narrow viewports, the parent flex container owns that.
- No states, no motion, no transitions. Tag is a non-interactive static label. No `:hover`, `:focus`, `:active`, or `[disabled]` rules.
- Tokens consumed: `--fs-meta`, `--font-sans`, `--fg`, `--fg-muted`, `--surface`, `--hairline`, `--hairline-w`, `--space-1`, `--space-2`, `--lh-meta`. No new tokens introduced.
- Contrast: `"default"` tone `--fg` on `--surface` = 15.46:1 (AAA). `"muted"` tone `--fg-muted` on `--bg` = 4.91:1 (AA normal at 14px ŌĆö passes WCAG 2.1 4.5:1 threshold). Muted border is decorative; no contrast requirement under ┬¦1.4.11.
- Composition: use in any inline or flex context. Multiple Tags in a row: parent flex container with `gap: var(--space-2)` and `flex-wrap: wrap`. Inside prose `<p>`: natural inline flow. No self-margin ŌĆö consumer owns spacing.
- Tag is NOT `<Eyebrow>` (Eyebrow is block-level, uppercase, tracked, always above content). Tag is NOT `<StatusBadge>` (StatusBadge encodes liveness state with a colored dot). Tag is NOT a button (no onClick, no hover/focus states authored by the DS).
- Do NOT add colored semantic tones (`"success"`, `"warning"`, `"danger"`, `"accent"`). Any future colored tone requires a brand-level decision-log entry and Arian's explicit sign-off.
- Do NOT add a close button, `onRemove`, or `onClick` ŌĆö that is a future `TagButton` primitive with its own spec.
- Do NOT make Tag polymorphic. The root is always `<span>`.
- Do NOT use Tag to signal liveness ŌĆö use `<StatusBadge>`.
- Do NOT use Tag as a heading or section label ŌĆö use `<Eyebrow>`.
- Do NOT add `text-overflow: ellipsis` or truncation. Tags are always short strings by design.

### TagList
- Canonical wrapper molecule for a collection of `<Tag>` atoms. Owns the flex-wrap layout and gap rhythm between Tags, and enforces a consistent overflow strategy via the optional `max` prop.
- Root element: `<div>`. Non-interactive ŌĆö no hover, click, focus, or drag semantics. No landmark role; correct for a presentational flex row of inline labels.
- `children` (required `ReactNode`) ŌĆö one or more `<Tag>` atoms. TagList does not clone or inspect children; it wraps whatever is passed. Expected child is `<Tag>`; passing other element types breaks the visual contract and the `max` overflow logic.
- `max` (optional `number`) ŌĆö when set, renders only the first `max` children; surplus Tags collapse into a single `<Tag tone="muted">+{N}</Tag>` overflow pill. Hidden Tags are sliced from the DOM entirely (not CSS-hidden). Omit to show all Tags.
- `gap` (`"sm" | "md"`, default `"md"`) ŌĆö gap between Tag pills on both axes. `"md"` = `--space-2` (8px), the canonical editorial tag-row gap. `"sm"` = `--space-1` (4px) for dense surfaces (table cells, compact card footers).
- `className` (optional `string`) ŌĆö layout overrides by consumer (margin, alignment). TagList has no self-margin.
- Tokens consumed: `--space-1`, `--space-2`. No new tokens introduced.
- Layout: `display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; align-items: center; gap: var(--space-2)` (or `--space-1` for `gap="sm"`).
- Overflow pill: `<Tag tone="muted">+{N}</Tag>` where N = total child count minus `max`. The pill's visual treatment (transparent bg, hairline border, muted fg) is inherited from `Tag tone="muted"` ŌĆö TagList owns no overflow styles of its own.
- Do NOT use TagList to group `<StatusBadge>` elements ŌĆö StatusBadge encodes liveness state; Tag encodes category. Distinct semantic registers.
- Do NOT use TagList as a navigation row ŌĆö use `<nav>` with anchor children.
- Do NOT use TagList for a single Tag ŌĆö a lone Tag does not need a wrapper.
- Do NOT use `max` as a content strategy ŌĆö prefer curating the tag list to ~5 items.

### Section
- Canonical page-section wrapper molecule. Owns the vertical rhythm around a section's header block (eyebrow + title + lede) and exposes a children slot for section body content.
- No visual surface ŌĆö no background, border, or shadow in default configuration. Structural scaffolding only.
- `as` prop (default `"section"`) ŌĆö polymorphic root element. Valid values: `"section"` | `"article"` | `"aside"` | `"div"`. `"section"` is a region landmark when titled; `"article"` for independently distributable content; `"aside"` for tangential content; `"div"` when a landmark would be noise.
- `eyebrow` (optional `string | ReactNode`) ŌĆö String values are automatically wrapped in `<Eyebrow>` with default props (`variant="muted"`). ReactNode values are rendered as-is. Never wraps a ReactNode in a second Eyebrow. Always routes through the `<Eyebrow>` atom for consistency.
- `title` (optional `ReactNode`) ŌĆö rendered as the element named by `titleAs`. When present, wires `aria-labelledby` on the root for landmark elements.
- `titleAs` (optional `"h1" | "h2" | "h3"`, default `"h2"`) ŌĆö heading level for the title element. Default is `h2` (page subdivisions below a Hero which owns the h1). Pass `"h1"` when Section is at the top of a page without a Hero; `"h3"` for nested subdivision contexts.
- `lede` (optional `ReactNode`) ŌĆö supporting copy below the title. String values are wrapped in `<p className="lede">` (applies `color: var(--fg-muted)` and `max-width: 36rem` via the existing `.lede` class in `tokens.css`). ReactNode values are rendered as-is.
- `size` (`"default"` | `"tight"`, default `"default"`) ŌĆö block padding variant. `"default"` = `--space-16` (64px) top + bottom; `"tight"` = `--space-12` (48px) top + bottom.
- `children` ŌĆö section body content. Section applies no styling to children ŌĆö no max-width, no typography. The body is the consumer's space.
- **Empty header guard**: if `eyebrow`, `title`, and `lede` are all absent, the header wrapper div is not rendered.
- **`aria-labelledby` wiring**: when `title` is present and `as` is a landmark element (`"section"` / `"article"` / `"aside"`), the title element receives an auto-generated `id` (via `useId()`) and the root receives `aria-labelledby`. Skipped for `as="div"`.
- Header block max-width: `var(--hero-max)` (38rem). Children slot has no max-width.
- Header gaps: eyebrowŌåÆtitle `--space-2` (8px); titleŌåÆlede `--space-4` (16px). HeaderŌåÆchildren gap: `--space-12` (48px).
- Global `h2` bottom margin is zeroed in Section's CSS scope (`margin: 0` on `.title`) ŌĆö Section's flex layout controls spacing instead.
- No `bleed` prop. No `align` prop. Both excluded from spec ŌĆö structural wrapper, not visual band.
- Do NOT nest Section inside Section. Would double block padding and break vertical rhythm.
- Do NOT use Section as a card, callout, or decorative container. It carries no visual surface.
- Do NOT add `aria-labelledby` manually when a title is provided ŌĆö Section wires it automatically for landmark elements.

### Pull
- Inline editorial pull-quote molecule. Lives inside long-form prose between paragraphs. NOT a page-level molecule (unlike Hero or Statement).
- Root element: `<blockquote>` by default (correct HTML for quoted material). Use `as="aside"` only when Pull is an extracted editorial accent that is not a literal quotation from an external source.
- `variant="serif"` (default) ŌĆö Instrument Serif italic at `--fs-pull` (20ŌĆō26px fluid). Use for editorial long-form content, article pages, customer stories.
- `variant="sans"` ŌĆö Geist roman at `--fs-pull`. Use for technical or operator-grade content where Instrument Serif feels mismatched.
- `--fs-pull: clamp(1.25rem, 1rem + 1vw, 1.625rem)` (20ŌĆō26px) ŌĆö the only scale. No `size` prop. A `large` variant was explicitly rejected; the page-level editorial scale is `<Statement>`.
- Left rule: `border-inline-start: 3px solid var(--hairline)`. 3px is a brand-locked constant (not `--hairline-w`). Decorative typographic punctuation, not a layout divider.
- `padding-inline-start: var(--space-4)` (16px) ŌĆö distance from rule to body text.
- `margin-block: var(--space-8)` (32px) ŌĆö breathing room above + below. Adjacent `<p>` margin-bottom adds 48px total gap (non-collapsing). This is correct editorial rhythm at --fs-pull scale.
- `max-width: var(--hero-max)` (38rem) ŌĆö matches the article text column. Pull never exceeds prose measure.
- `cite` prop ŌĆö URL mapped to HTML `cite` attribute on `<blockquote>`. Machine-readable only. SILENTLY IGNORED when `as="aside"` (not a valid attribute on aside).
- `attribution` prop (optional ReactNode) ŌĆö visible trailing attribution line. Renders as `<footer>` inside `<blockquote>` (per HTML5 spec; scoped, not contentinfo landmark). Renders as `<p>` inside `<aside>`. Always `--fs-meta`, `--fg-muted`, italic regardless of variant.
- No decorative `"` quote glyph ŌĆö the left rule is the typographic punctuation.
- Do NOT place Pull inside a `<Hero>`. Do NOT stack Pull with `<Statement>` on the same surface. Do NOT use Pull for person identity attribution ŌĆö use `<Quote>` (once shipped).
- Multiple Pulls per page are permitted. More than 3 is an editorial judgment, not a DS constraint.
- Screen reader: `<blockquote>` is announced as a quotation; `<aside>` adds a complementary landmark ŌĆö avoid `as="aside"` in articles with multiple Pulls (landmark noise).

### LinkCard
- Canonical interactive card primitive. The entire card surface is a single click target (`<a>` root). Use for navigational index pages (`/work`, `/posts`, `/case-studies`) where each grid item routes to a destination.
- Root element: `<a>` by default. When `asChild={true}`, root becomes a Radix `Slot` ŌĆö pass a framework router `<Link>` as the child. Identical to the `Button` `asChild` pattern.
- `href` is required when `asChild` is false. A runtime `console.error` fires in development if `href` is absent and `asChild` is false.
- Do NOT pass both `href` and `asChild={true}`. When `asChild` is true the child element owns the href.
- `variant="default"` (default) ŌĆö `--surface` background, `1px solid --hairline` border, `--radius-3` (8px) corners, `--space-8` (32px) inset padding all sides. Use in grid contexts.
- `variant="quiet"` ŌĆö `--bg` background (no fill), hairline rule top only (`border-top: 1px solid --hairline`), no radius, padding-block `--space-4`, padding-inline `0`. Use in dense vertical list contexts.
- Hover: `border-color` shifts to `--accent`. Transition: `border-color var(--dur-mid) var(--easing-link)`. For quiet variant, `border-top-color` shifts.
- `:focus-visible`: `outline: 2px solid var(--accent); outline-offset: 4px`. Default variant adds `border-radius: --radius-3` on the outline to match card shape. Quiet variant: `border-radius: 0`.
- `:active`: `transform: translateY(1px)` at `--dur-press` (80ms). Both variants. Tactile press response matching `<Button>`.
- `eyebrow` (optional `string | ReactNode`) ŌĆö String ŌåÆ auto-wrapped in `<Eyebrow variant="muted">`. ReactNode ŌåÆ pass-through (consumer owns configuration). Same discriminant as `<Section>`: `typeof eyebrow === 'string'`.
- `title` (required `ReactNode`) ŌĆö primary content and accessible name anchor. Renders as `titleAs` element. Typography: `--fs-card-title` (24ŌĆō32px fluid), `--font-sans`, weight 500, `line-height: 1.3`, `letter-spacing: -0.005em`. Margin zeroed inside LinkCard flex scope (overrides global h3 margin).
- `titleAs` (`"h2" | "h3" | "h4"`, default `"h3"`) ŌĆö heading element. Default `h3` is correct when page has `<h1>` (Hero) and `<h2>` (Section above the grid). Pass `"h2"` when no preceding h2 exists.
- `body` (optional `ReactNode`) ŌĆö string ŌåÆ wrapped in `<p>`; ReactNode ŌåÆ pass-through. `--fs-body`, `--fg-muted`, `line-height: 1.55`.
- `footer` (optional `ReactNode`) ŌĆö pushed to card bottom via flex spacer (`flex: 1`). Defaults to `--fg-muted` color, `--fs-meta` font size. ReactNode pass-through; consumer content can override.
- `icon` (optional `ReactNode`) ŌĆö absolutely positioned at `top: --space-8; right: --space-8` within card padding box. `pointer-events: none`. Consumer-supplied. Conventional: `<ArrowUpRight size={16} />` from `lucide-react` for external links.
- `external` ŌĆö when true: adds `target="_blank"`, `rel="noopener noreferrer"`, and appends a visually-hidden `<span>(opens in new tab)</span>` for screen readers. Does NOT auto-inject an icon ŌĆö pass via `icon` slot.
- Global `<a>` underline animation (`background-image` grow) is suppressed on the card root via `background-image: none; padding-bottom: 0`. The card's sole hover signal is the border-color shift.
- **No nested interactive elements.** A `<button>` or `<a>` inside a LinkCard is a WCAG failure (nested interactivity). Footer tags and inline labels must be non-interactive (`<span>`, plain text). This is documented as a hard constraint, not a component-level enforcement.
- Accessible name is derived from text content ŌĆö eyebrow + title + body all contribute. Body copy should be meaningful, not boilerplate ("Click to learn more" produces a poor accessible name).
- In a CSS Grid context: set `align-items: stretch` on the grid container and `height: 100%` on the LinkCard root so all cards in a row share the same height. The footer flex spacer then aligns footers to the same baseline.
- Do NOT mix `variant="default"` and `variant="quiet"` in the same grid ŌĆö incoherent visual rhythm.
- Do NOT put `<Button>` or any interactive element inside a LinkCard.
- Do NOT use LinkCard for non-navigational content. Use `RoleCard` (structural, no click target) for role descriptions.

### TeamCard
- Canonical person tile molecule. Renders a single team member as a brand object: portrait + name + role + optional bio + optional contact affordance. Operator-grade register ŌĆö practitioner credential, not a social media card.
- Root element: `<article>` by default (correct HTML for a self-contained, independently distributable content item). Polymorphic via `as` prop: `"article"` | `"section"` | `"div"`.
- `aria-labelledby` is wired automatically from the root landmark element to the name heading's `id` (via `useId()`). Skipped when `as="div"` ŌĆö a `<div>` carries no landmark role.
- `portrait` (required `ReactNode`) ŌĆö pass a fully-configured `<Portrait>` instance. TeamCard does NOT accept `portraitSrc` ŌĆö Portrait's API is authoritative for `src`, `alt`, `aspect`, `width`, and `sizes`. Never pass a raw `<img>`.
- `name` (required `ReactNode`) ŌĆö person's name. Rendered as the element specified by `nameAs`. Default `nameAs="h3"` is correct inside a `<Section>` (h2) on a page with a Hero (h1). Override to `"h2"` for standalone person pages; `"h4"` for deeply nested contexts.
- `role` (required `ReactNode`) ŌĆö short role/title descriptor. Rendered as `<p>` at `--fs-meta` (14px), `color: var(--fg-muted)`. No uppercase treatment ŌĆö role is read at prose speed.
- `bio` (optional `ReactNode`) ŌĆö supporting prose paragraph. Rendered as `<p>` at `--fs-body`, `color: var(--fg)`, `line-height: 1.55`. When absent, no empty element is emitted.
- `contact` (optional `ReactNode`) ŌĆö any contact affordance. Standard usage: `<EmailLink email="ŌĆ”" variant="muted" />`. Slot is untyped ŌĆö LinkedIn links, GitHub handles, etc. work without spec changes. TeamCard provides no interaction states; the inner component owns them.
- `eyebrow` (optional `string | ReactNode`) ŌĆö string values are automatically wrapped in `<Eyebrow variant="muted">`. ReactNode values are rendered as-is (escape hatch). Never double-wraps a ReactNode. In grids, prefer placing the eyebrow in the Section header rather than repeating it on every card.
- `layout` (`"stacked"` | `"horizontal"`, default `"stacked"`) ŌĆö portrait-text spatial relationship. `"stacked"`: portrait above, text below (grid-context default). `"horizontal"`: portrait left at fixed `5rem` width, text block right with `--space-6` gap; collapses to stacked below 768px.
- **No padding, no border, no background.** TeamCard is a content tile, not a chrome tile. The consumer's grid owns the column gutter. If a surface variant is needed, use `className` override at the consumer layer ŌĆö it is not a DS variant.
- Gap stack (stacked layout): eyebrowŌåÆportrait `--space-3` (12px), portraitŌåÆname `--space-4` (16px), nameŌåÆrole `--space-2` (8px), roleŌåÆbio `--space-4` (16px), bioŌåÆcontact `--space-8` (32px), roleŌåÆcontact (no bio) `--space-8` (32px).
- Contrast: name heading `--fg` on `--bg` = 16.29:1 (AAA). Role `--fg-muted` on `--bg` = 4.91:1 (AA normal at 14px). Bio `--fg` on `--bg` = 16.29:1 (AAA).
- Do NOT pass `portraitSrc` ŌĆö the prop does not exist. Portrait's API is authoritative.
- Do NOT make TeamCard interactive (`href`, `onClick`). It is not a link. Consumer wraps in `<a>` or `<Link>` if needed and owns that interaction pattern.
- Do NOT nest TeamCard inside Hero. Hero is the page h1 moment; heading-level conflict results.
- Do NOT compose TeamCard with RoleCard. They are sibling molecules with distinct semantics (person vs. function).

### FeatureCard
- Canonical structural feature-grid tile. Use for capability, service, or feature grids where items are peers (not a numbered sequence) and none of the tiles are navigational destinations.
- `as` prop (default `"article"`) ŌĆö polymorphic root element. Valid values: `"article"` | `"section"` | `"div"` | `"li"`. Use `"li"` inside a `<ul>` feature list; `"section"` when region landmark semantics are required; `"div"` when landmark semantics would be noise. Consumer is responsible for wrapping `as="li"` FeatureCards in a `<ul>`.
- `icon` (optional ReactNode) ŌĆö decorative icon. FeatureCard wraps it in `<span aria-hidden="true">`. Sets `color: currentColor` on the wrapper so the icon inherits `--fg`. Recommended icon sizes: `size={24}` or `size={32}` from lucide-react. Consumer still passes `aria-hidden="true"` on the icon node for clarity, but the wrapper guarantees screen readers skip it regardless.
- `eyebrow` (optional `string | ReactNode`) ŌĆö String ŌåÆ auto-wrapped in `<Eyebrow variant="muted">`. ReactNode ŌåÆ rendered as-is (no double-wrap). Same string-convention as Section, LinkCard, and TeamCard.
- `title` (required ReactNode) ŌĆö feature name. Rendered as the element named by `titleAs`. `"h1"` is intentionally excluded ŌĆö a feature tile is never a page heading.
- `titleAs` (optional `"h2" | "h3" | "h4"`, default `"h3"`) ŌĆö heading level. `"h3"` is correct for a grid of tiles under a `<Section>` (h2) on a page with a `<Hero>` (h1).
- `body` (required ReactNode) ŌĆö feature description. String ŌåÆ wrapped in `<p style="margin:0">` (suppresses global `p` bottom-margin double-stack inside flex column). ReactNode ŌåÆ rendered as-is.
- `footer` (optional ReactNode) ŌĆö rendered inside a wrapper div with default `color: var(--fg-muted)` and `font-size: var(--fs-meta)`. When absent, no element is emitted and no gap is created.
- `variant` (`"default"` | `"bordered"`, default `"default"`) ŌĆö `"default"` is transparent, no border, no radius. `"bordered"` adds `--surface` background, `1px solid --hairline` border, `--radius-3` corners.
- Padding: `--space-6` (24px) on all four sides in both variants ŌĆö single value prevents layout shift when switching variants at runtime.
- Gap stack: iconŌåÆeyebrow/title `--space-6` (24px); eyebrowŌåÆtitle `--space-2` (8px); titleŌåÆbody `--space-3` (12px); bodyŌåÆfooter `--space-6` (24px).
- `aria-labelledby` wired on `article` and `section` roots to the title's auto-generated id (via `useId()`). Omitted for `div` and `li` ŌĆö neither carries a landmark role.
- No internal flex spacer. Footer alignment in a grid requires composition: `align-items: stretch` on the grid, `height: 100%` on the card root, `margin-top: auto` on the footer wrapper in consumer surface CSS.
- No states, no hover, no interactivity. FeatureCard is structural only. For an interactive tile, use `<LinkCard>`.
- Do NOT mix `default` and `bordered` variants in the same grid ŌĆö incoherent visual rhythm.
- Do NOT use FeatureCard for navigational tiles ŌĆö use `<LinkCard>`.
- Do NOT use FeatureCard for sequenced/numbered items ŌĆö use `<Principle>` or `<FailureMode>`.
- Do NOT pass `titleAs="h1"` ŌĆö a feature tile is never a page heading. The prop type excludes it.
- Do NOT compose FeatureCard with RoleCard in the same grid. RoleCard carries sequencing semantics that conflict with FeatureCard's peer-item register.

### FieldNote
- Inline technical-aside molecule. A short parenthetical callout ŌĆö a sentence or two ŌĆö that sits inline with body copy and provides a factual clarification, caveat, or data footnote without interrupting reading flow.
- Root element: `<aside>`. Not polymorphic ŌĆö there is no `as` prop. `<aside>` is the semantically correct element for tangentially related content (HTML spec). When nested inside `<article>` or `<section>`, `<aside>` degrades from a `complementary` landmark to a generic container ŌĆö this is the correct behavior for multiple per-page inline asides (avoids landmark pollution).
- `label` (optional string) ŌĆö renders a single `<p>` above the body text in the Eyebrow typographic register (`--fs-meta` 14px, `--fg-muted`, `text-transform: uppercase`, `--tracking-eyebrow` 0.06em letter-spacing, `--lh-meta` 1.2 line-height). When absent, no label element is rendered. Default: `undefined`. Example values: `"Note"`, `"Caveat"`, `"Aside"`, `"Data point"`. Do NOT default to `"Note"` ŌĆö the left rule is the signal; the label is for when type of aside is load-bearing information.
- `children` (required ReactNode) ŌĆö the body text. Rendered inside a `<p className="body">`. Accepts inline marks only: `<strong>`, `<em>`, `<a>`. No block-level children ŌĆö no `<h2>`, `<ul>`, `<p>`, `<div>`, `<table>`. Editorial constraint; not type-enforced.
- Left rule: `border-inline-start: var(--hairline-w) solid var(--hairline)`. Always present. 1px ŌĆö quieter than Pull's 3px editorial accent. Decorative; no contrast requirement applies.
- `padding-inline-start: var(--space-3)` (12px) ŌĆö tighter than Pull's `--space-4` (16px). Reinforces FieldNote's subsidiary character.
