- `margin-block: var(--space-6)` (24px) ŌĆö less than Pull's `--space-8` (32px). FieldNote is a minor inline pause; Pull is a major editorial pause.
- `max-width: var(--hero-max)` (38rem / 608px) ŌĆö matches the prose column cap used by Pull, Hero, and Section header blocks.
- Body typography: `--fs-body` (17ŌĆō19px fluid), `--font-sans` (Geist), `font-style: normal`, `line-height: 1.55` (global body value, inline ŌĆö BACKLOG migration target when `--lh-body` is tokenized), `color: var(--fg)`. FieldNote body is real content, not a caption ŌĆö full-weight color, not muted.
- Inline links inside `children` inherit the global `<a>` two-layer underline rule from `tokens.css`. At rest: 1px `--hairline` underline. On hover: 1px `--accent` underline grows over it via `background-size` transition (`--dur-mid`, `--easing-link`). On `:focus-visible`: `outline: 2px solid var(--accent)`, `outline-offset: 4px`, `border-radius: var(--radius-1)`. No override in FieldNote's CSS scope ŌĆö global rule is correct and must NOT be suppressed.
- No states, no motion, no entrance animation. Static typography.
- No color variants (`variant="warning"` etc.) ŌĆö excluded at the brand level. Colored callouts are a different component. This exclusion requires explicit founder sign-off to change.
- No icon slot. Icons imply status severity; FieldNote is plain text only.
- No multi-paragraph body. Single-paragraph aside. For multiple paragraphs, use Section with `size="tight"`.
- Tokens consumed: `--hairline-w`, `--hairline`, `--space-3`, `--space-1`, `--space-6`, `--fs-body`, `--fs-meta`, `--fg`, `--fg-muted`, `--font-sans`, `--tracking-eyebrow`, `--lh-meta`, `--hero-max`. No new tokens introduced.
- Contrast: body `--fg` (#1D1D1F) on `--bg` (#FBFBFD) = 16.29:1 (AAA). Label `--fg-muted` (#6E6E73) on `--bg` (#FBFBFD) = 4.91:1 (AA normal Ō£ō at 14px).
- Do NOT use FieldNote for content that has a title, an index, or more than two sentences of structured prose ŌĆö use `<FailureMode>` or `<Section>`.
- Do NOT nest FieldNote inside `<Pull>`. They operate at different typographic scales.
- Do NOT place FieldNote inside `<Hero>`. Hero owns the page display moment.
- Do NOT pass block-level elements as `children`. No `<h2>`, `<ul>`, `<table>`, `<div>`. Inline marks only.
- Do NOT add an `as` prop. Root is always `<aside>`.
- Do NOT add color variants. Tonally neutral by brand decision.

```tsx
// Plain body ŌĆö no label (left rule is the signal):
<FieldNote>
  In 2024 we ran the same prompt across three model families and saw consistent
  degradation after context exceeded 16k tokens.
</FieldNote>

// With label ŌĆö when type of aside is load-bearing:
<FieldNote label="Caveat">
  These numbers reflect Q4 2023 benchmarks. The 2024 evals are in progress
  and results may differ.
</FieldNote>

// With inline link in body:
<FieldNote label="Note">
  The latency figures come from our{" "}
  <a href="/methodology">public methodology doc</a>, updated monthly.
</FieldNote>
```

### Alert
- Inline semantic banner molecule. Five variants: `info`, `success`, `warn`, `error`, `note`. Communicates product feedback with a defined emotional register. Distinct from `FieldNote` (no live-region semantics) and `Banner` (full-width). Alert is inline, persistent, block-level.
- Root element: `<div>` with explicit `role` per variant. Not polymorphic. Always `<div>`.
- `variant` (optional): `"info" | "success" | "warn" | "error" | "note"`. Default: `"info"`. Maps to surface color, icon, and ARIA role.
- `title` (optional string): Scannable label above body. Rendered as `<strong>` ŌĆö NOT a heading. No heading levels inside inline banners.
- `children` (required ReactNode): Body copy. Rendered inside `<p>`.
- `icon` (optional ReactNode | null): Override default icon. Pass `null` to suppress entirely. Default auto-selects a lucide-react icon per variant.
- ARIA roles: `info/success/warn` ŌåÆ `role="status"` (polite). `error` ŌåÆ `role="alert"` (assertive). `note` ŌåÆ `role="note"` (static landmark). Never use `role="alert"` for non-urgent messages.
- Variant surfaces: `info/note` ŌåÆ `--surface`. `success` ŌåÆ `--bg-success` / `--fg-on-success`. `warn` ŌåÆ `--bg-warning` / `--fg-on-warning`. `error` ŌåÆ `--bg-danger` / `--fg-on-danger`. `note` uses `--fg-muted` text.
- Icon colors: `info` ŌåÆ `--accent`. `success` ŌåÆ `--success`. `warn` ŌåÆ `--warning`. `error` ŌåÆ `--danger`. `note` ŌåÆ `--fg-muted`.
- Leading icon carries `aria-hidden="true"` ŌĆö decorative. Variant conveys semantic register.
- Tokens consumed: `--surface`, `--bg-success`, `--fg-on-success`, `--bg-danger`, `--fg-on-danger`, `--bg-warning`, `--fg-on-warning`, `--fg`, `--fg-muted`, `--accent`, `--success`, `--danger`, `--warning`, `--hairline`, `--hairline-w`, `--font-sans`, `--fs-meta`, `--lh-body`, `--space-1`, `--space-2`, `--space-3`, `--space-4`, `--radius-2`, `--icon-sm`. No new tokens introduced.
- Do NOT use Alert for toast/transient notifications. Use `<Toast>`. Do NOT use Alert to replace `<FieldNote>` ŌĆö FieldNote has no live-region semantics. Do NOT nest Alert inside `<p>`. Do NOT use `note` variant for urgent feedback.

```tsx
<Alert variant="error" title="Submission failed">
  Please fix the highlighted fields and try again.
</Alert>

<Alert variant="success">Profile updated successfully.</Alert>

<Alert variant="info" icon={null}>Icon suppressed.</Alert>
```

### Quote
- Attributed customer testimonial molecule. A short prose block (1ŌĆō4 sentences) from a named person, with optional role/title and optional avatar slot. Primary surface: customer story pages and social-proof sections.
- Root element: `<figure>`. Not polymorphic ŌĆö `<figure>` is the single correct semantic root for attributed quotations per HTML5. Inside: `<blockquote>` for the quoted body, `<figcaption>` for the attribution row. This structure correctly associates the attribution with the quote at the HTML semantic level ŌĆö no extra ARIA needed.
- Distinct from `<Pull>` (unattributed editorial pull-quote, Instrument Serif italic, left rule) and `<Statement>` (brand assertion, once per page, no attribution). Quote is sans-serif roman ŌĆö identifiable at a glance from Pull/Statement without reading the attribution.
- `quote` (required ReactNode) ŌĆö the quoted body text. Accepts inline marks (`<em>`, `<strong>`) for emphasis within the passage. No block-level children ŌĆö no headings, lists, nested paragraphs. Rendered as direct child of `<blockquote>`.
- `name` (required string) ŌĆö the attributed person's name. Plain string; `font-weight: 500`, `--fg`, `--fs-meta` (14px).
- `role` (optional string) ŌĆö the person's role or title. Plain string. Example: `"VP Engineering, Acme Corp"`. `font-weight: 400`, `--fg-muted`, `--fs-meta`. When omitted, no role element is rendered.
- `avatar` (optional ReactNode) ŌĆö avatar slot. Accepts any ReactNode: `<img>`, a third-party Avatar, a `<div>` with initials. The DS does not ship an Avatar atom. When present, rendered as the leftmost element of the attribution row; when absent, the name/role column sits flush. Convention: size to 40├Ś40px and clip to circle (`border-radius: 50%`). DS does not enforce sizing or shape. If passing `<img>`, set `alt=""` (decorative) ŌĆö the `name` is the text alternative.
- Layout: stacked. Body above, attribution row below. No sidebar layout. Stacked degrades cleanly when avatar is absent.
- `<blockquote>` body typography: `--fs-pull` (20ŌĆō26px fluid), `--font-sans` (Geist), `font-weight: 400`, `font-style: normal`, `line-height: 1.45`, `color: var(--fg)`. `--fs-pull` reuses Pull's scale ŌĆö both are quoted-content primitives at elevated size. The typographic differentiator is register (sans roman vs. serif italic), not size.
- `<figcaption>` layout: `display: flex`, `align-items: center`, `gap: var(--space-3)` (12px avatar-to-name column). `margin-top: var(--space-4)` (16px) from blockquote. `padding-top: var(--space-4)` (16px) above content. `border-top: var(--hairline-w) solid var(--hairline)` ŌĆö always present; use `className` to suppress if needed.
- Name/role column: `display: flex`, `flex-direction: column`, `gap: var(--space-1)` (4px).
- `max-width: var(--hero-max)` (38rem / 608px) ŌĆö matches the prose column cap used by Pull, Hero, Section, and FieldNote.
- `margin-block: var(--space-8)` (32px) ŌĆö matches Pull's block margin. Remove default UA `margin` on `<figure>`.
- `className` merges via `clsx`. `forwardRef` to the `<figure>` root. `displayName = "Quote"`. Full `ComponentPropsWithoutRef<"figure">` spread (id, data-*, aria-*, event handlers).
- No cite prop in v1 ŌĆö customer story quotes rarely have a URL source; deferred to later revision.
- No showRule prop ŌĆö hairline rule is always on. Use className override to suppress.
- No avatar shape wrapper ŌĆö DS does not enforce sizing; consumer responsibility.
- No variant, no size, no as prop.
- Tokens consumed: `--fs-pull`, `--fs-meta`, `--font-sans`, `--fg`, `--fg-muted`, `--hairline`, `--hairline-w`, `--space-1`, `--space-3`, `--space-4`, `--space-8`, `--lh-meta`, `--hero-max`. No new tokens introduced.
- Contrast: body/name `--fg` (#1D1D1F) on `--bg` (#FBFBFD) = 16.29:1 (AAA). Role `--fg-muted` (#6E6E73) on `--bg` (#FBFBFD) = 4.91:1 (AA normal Ō£ō at 14px). Hairline is decorative.
- Do NOT use Quote for unattributed text ŌĆö use `<Pull>` if there is no named person.
- Do NOT place Quote inside `<Hero>`. Hero owns the page display moment.
- Do NOT use both Pull and Quote for the same person's words on the same page ŌĆö pick the register.
- Do NOT render Quote on a `--bg-warm-accent` band ŌĆö palette interaction is untested.
- Do NOT add a `cite` prop, `showRule` prop, avatar container wrapper, or `as` polymorphism without founder sign-off.
- Do NOT pass block-level elements as `quote`. Inline marks only.

```tsx
// Full Quote ŌĆö avatar, name, role:
<Quote
  quote="We went from weeks to hours. The tooling handled what we used to staff an entire team for."
  name="Sarah Chen"
  role="VP Engineering, Meridian Labs"
  avatar={
    <img
      src="https://cdn.example.com/avatars/sarah-chen.jpg"
      alt=""
      width={40}
      height={40}
      style={{ borderRadius: "50%", display: "block" }}
    />
  }
/>

// Without avatar:
<Quote
  quote="The accuracy improvements were immediate and measurable."
  name="James Okonkwo"
  role="Head of Data, Fieldwork AI"
/>

// Name only ŌĆö no role, no avatar:
<Quote quote="Exactly what we needed. Nothing we didn't." name="Priya Mehta" />

// Inline emphasis in quote body:
<Quote
  quote={<>We went from <em>weeks</em> to hours.</>}
  name="Sarah Chen"
  role="VP Engineering, Meridian Labs"
/>
```

### Avatar
- Atom for person/entity identity display. Three modes via discriminated union on `mode` prop.
- `mode="image"` ŌĆö renders `<img loading="lazy">` with `src` (required) and optional `alt`. `loading` is hardcoded to `"lazy"` ŌĆö no escape hatch. No `onError` fallback ŌĆö broken image falls back to browser default; consumers who need a fallback wrap in their own state.
- `mode="initials"` ŌĆö renders 1ŌĆō2 character text label inside the avatar shape. Consumer is responsible for the initials string ŌĆö Avatar does NOT derive initials from a name.
- `mode="empty"` (default when `mode` is omitted) ŌĆö blank placeholder, no inner content.
- `size` (`"sm"` | `"md"` | `"lg"`, default `"md"`) ŌĆö fixed pixel dimensions: sm=24px, md=32px, lg=40px. These are inline px values in the CSS module (no tokens). Initials font scales proportionally: sm=10px, md=12px, lg=14px.
- `shape` (`"circle"` | `"square"`, default `"circle"`) ŌĆö circle uses `border-radius: 50%`; square uses `--radius-2` (4px). Inline `border-radius: 50%` in CSS module (no token).
- Hairline ring: `box-shadow: 0 0 0 1px var(--hairline)` ŌĆö avoids layout shift versus a CSS `border`. Uses `--hairline` token.
- Background: `--surface`. Text color: `--fg-muted`. Font: `--font-sans`, weight 500.
- `name` (optional string) ŌĆö accessible name for the avatar as a whole. Required in initials and empty modes for a11y. In image mode: required when `alt` is omitted. Produces `role="img"` + `aria-label` on the root `<span>`.
- In image mode with `alt` provided, the `<img>` carries the label and the root span does NOT receive `role="img"` (avoids double-labelling).
- Root is always `<span>`. Non-polymorphic. Ref forwarded to root span. `...rest` spread to root element.
- Tokens consumed: `--surface` (background), `--fg-muted` (text + initials color), `--hairline` (ring), `--font-sans` (font family), `--radius-2` (square shape).
- Do NOT add `onError` fallback ŌĆö stateless atom. Consumers own error handling.
- Do NOT pass `loading` ŌĆö hardcoded `"lazy"` on internal `<img>`. No prop escape hatch.
- Do NOT derive initials from a name string ŌĆö pass initials explicitly via `initials` prop.
- Do NOT add group/stack composition at this layer ŌĆö consumer-side concern.
- Do NOT make Avatar polymorphic ŌĆö root is always `<span>`.
- Do NOT use Avatar for decorative brand imagery ŌĆö use `<Portrait>` for editorial photography.

### Skeleton
- Atom for async content placeholders. Single element ŌĆö no children, no inner structure.
- Root element is `<div>` by default; `as="span"` renders `<span>` with `display: inline-block` for inline contexts.
- `as` prop is a closed union: only `"div"` and `"span"`. No arbitrary elements.
- `width` / `height` (string | number, optional): bare numbers coerce to px (e.g. `120` ŌåÆ `120px`); strings pass through verbatim (`"100%"`, `"12rem"`). Applied as inline `style`. No intrinsic size ŌĆö omitting both collapses the element to zero height (silent failure, easy to debug).
- `radius` (`"sm"` | `"md"` | `"lg"` | `"circle"`, default `"md"`): sm ŌåÆ `--radius-1` (2px), md ŌåÆ `--radius-2` (4px), lg ŌåÆ `--radius-3` (8px), circle ŌåÆ `50%` (geometric instruction, not a token).
- Animation: opacity pulse only ŌĆö `poukai-skeleton-pulse` keyframe, `ease-in-out`, `var(--dur-pulse)` (1800ms), infinite. 0%/100% opacity 1 ŌåÆ 50% opacity 0.4. NO gradient shimmer, NO background-position animation, NO moving gradient of any kind. The banlist is explicit: "Skeleton shimmer that's prettier than the loaded state."
- Reduced-motion: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` sets `animation: none; opacity: 0.6`. The global `tokens.css` block collapses duration to 0.01ms but does NOT set the correct resting opacity ŌĆö the component must declare this explicitly.
- `aria-hidden="true"` by default ŌĆö decorative placeholder, communicates nothing to the a11y tree. Consumer can override via `...rest`.
- `aria-busy` is NOT the Skeleton's concern ŌĆö the consumer container owns the `aria-busy="true"` / `aria-busy="false"` lifecycle.
- Tokens consumed: `--surface` (background fill), `--radius-1`, `--radius-2`, `--radius-3` (corner radii), `--dur-pulse` (animation duration). No new tokens introduced.
- `display: block` on root div; `display: inline-block` when `as="span"`.
- Do NOT add children ŌĆö Skeleton has no inner structure.
- Do NOT implement shimmer / sweep gradient / background-position animation at any point.
- Do NOT add a `tone` prop ŌĆö fill is always `--surface`. No exceptions.
- Do NOT add default width/height ŌĆö zero is the deliberate collapsed state.
- Do NOT set `aria-busy` on the Skeleton itself.
- Do NOT open the `as` union beyond `"div"` and `"span"`.

```tsx
// Block placeholder (text lines)
<div aria-busy="true">
  <Skeleton width="100%" height={24} radius="sm" />
  <Skeleton width="60%" height={24} radius="sm" />
</div>

// Avatar placeholder
<Skeleton width={40} height={40} radius="circle" />

// Card placeholder
<Skeleton width="100%" height={220} radius="lg" />

// Inline text placeholder
<p>Posted by <Skeleton as="span" width={80} height={14} radius="sm" /></p>
```

### Divider
- Canonical hairline separator atom. Renders a 1px rule ŌĆö horizontal (`<hr>`) or vertical (`<div role="separator">`).
- `orientation` (`"horizontal"` | `"vertical"`, default `"horizontal"`) ŌĆö horizontal is a full-width top-border rule; vertical is a 1px column rule that requires a flex/grid parent to have visible height.
- `tone` (`"default"` | `"muted"`, default `"default"`) ŌĆö `"default"` uses `--hairline`; `"muted"` uses `--hairline-soft` for quieter decorative contexts.
- `as` (`"hr"` | `"div"`) ŌĆö closed polymorphic. Horizontal defaults to `<hr>` (implicit `role="separator"`); vertical defaults to `<div>` with explicit `role="separator"` and `aria-orientation="vertical"`.
- Zero self-margin by design. Parent layout (flex `gap`, padding) controls spacing around the rule.
- Do NOT use as a background fill or container. Do NOT render thicker than `--hairline-w` (1px). Do NOT apply `--hairline-soft` for structural separators.

### Spinner
- Indeterminate loading indicator. Signals that an async operation is in progress when the duration is unknown.
- Root element: `<span role="status" aria-live="polite" aria-label>`. SVG arc inside is `aria-hidden="true"`.
- `size` (`"sm"` | `"md"` | `"lg"`, default `"md"`) ŌĆö sm=16px, md=20px, lg=24px. Matches the `--icon-sm/md/lg` rung sizes.
- `label` (string, default `"Loading"`) ŌĆö accessible label announced to screen readers.
- Uses `currentColor` for the arc and track ŌĆö place inside any colored context without a separate color prop.
- Under `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce`: rotation stops; a static ellipsis (`ŌĆ”`) is revealed for reduced-motion users.
- Rotation period: `--dur-spinner` (800ms). Sub-second so the spinner reads as active, not stalled.
- Do NOT use Spinner as a page-level loading overlay ŌĆö that is a separate organism. Do NOT add a determinate progress variant here.

### VisuallyHidden
- Canonical sr-only primitive. Renders children visible to the accessibility tree but invisible to sighted users (WCAG clip pattern).
- Root element: `<span>` by default; `as="div"` for block-level contexts where `<span>` would be invalid HTML.
- No tokens, no variants, no motion. Invariant ŌĆö one shape only.
- Use `...rest` to forward `id` (for `aria-labelledby`/`aria-describedby` associations) or `aria-live` (for live-region announcements).
- Do NOT add visible styles or variants. Do NOT use for layout hiding ŌĆö use CSS `display: none` or `visibility: hidden` for that.

### Icon
- Thin rendering wrapper around any `lucide-react` icon component. Enforces three system-wide contracts at every call site: a token-aligned size scale (`xs`/`sm`/`md`/`lg`), `currentColor` as the only colour source, and decorative-by-default accessibility (`aria-hidden="true"` unless an explicit label is provided).
- Root element: the Lucide icon SVG directly. No wrapper `<span>` or `<div>` ŌĆö the SVG IS the root. Keeps the DOM flat and preserves inline-flex alignment inside button labels and icon-text rows.
- `icon` (required `LucideIcon`) ŌĆö the Lucide component reference. Consumer imports directly from `lucide-react` and passes the component. `<Icon>` is a frame, not a catalogue ŌĆö it does NOT re-export any Lucide icon.
- `size` (`"xs" | "sm" | "md" | "lg"`, default `"sm"`) ŌĆö resolves to the `--icon-*` token via a `SIZE_MAP` lookup. xs ŌåÆ 12px, sm ŌåÆ 16px, md ŌåÆ 20px, lg ŌåÆ 24px. NEVER pass raw pixel values.
- `decorative` (boolean, default `true`) ŌĆö when `true`, `aria-hidden="true"` is set and the icon is removed from the a11y tree. When `false`, `role="img"` + `aria-label` is set. A dev-mode `console.error` fires when `decorative={false}` and no `aria-label` is provided ŌĆö this is an a11y contract violation.
- `aria-label` (optional string) ŌĆö required when `decorative={false}`. Ignored when `decorative={true}`.
- `className` ŌĆö forwarded to the SVG root via `clsx`. Use sparingly; only for layout-level positioning. Never for color.
- `...rest` ŌĆö spread to the SVG element (id, data-*, event handlers).
- `focusable="false"` ŌĆö always applied (IE11/legacy-Edge SVG focus prevention). `flex-shrink: 0; pointer-events: none; color: currentColor` ŌĆö always-on in CSS module.
- No `color` prop. `currentColor` is the entire colour contract. Parent sets `color`; the icon inherits.
- No numeric `size` prop. Four named rungs are the complete v1 surface.
- `displayName = "Icon"`. `forwardRef` to the SVG element.
- Tokens consumed: `--icon-xs` (12px), `--icon-sm` (16px), `--icon-md` (20px), `--icon-lg` (24px). Size classes in `Icon.module.css` set `width`/`height` via these tokens. `currentColor` cascades from parent ŌĆö no token needed.
- Composition: inside `<Button>` ŌåÆ `size="sm"`; inside `<Tag>` icon slot ŌåÆ `size="xs"`; inline with body text ŌåÆ `size="md"`; standalone nav/feature ŌåÆ `size="lg"`.
- Do NOT add a `color` prop ŌĆö `currentColor` is the contract.
- Do NOT pass raw pixel values to `size` ŌĆö the four named rungs are the complete scale.
- Do NOT make `<Icon>` interactive ŌĆö the surrounding `<button>` or `<a>` is always the focus target.
- Do NOT nest inside `<Wordmark>` ŌĆö Wordmark geometry is inline and hand-coded.

### Link
- Canonical styled anchor atom. Named, versioned component contract replacing the leaky global `a` rule and the `.muted-link` utility class for all call sites that want an explicit DS primitive.
- Root element: `<a>` by default. When `asChild={true}`, renders a Radix `<Slot>` that merges all DS styles and event handlers onto the single child ŌĆö the canonical pattern for Next.js `<Link>` or Remix `<Link>` composition.
- `href` (required string) ŌĆö destination URL. A link without an href is not a link; the prop is non-optional at the TypeScript level.
- `variant` (`"default"` | `"quiet"` | `"muted-link"`, default `"default"`) ŌĆö visual register. Three variants only; no additional tones without a spec revision.
- `asChild` (boolean, default `false`) ŌĆö Radix Slot composition. Consumer must pass exactly one child when `asChild={true}`.
- `className` ŌĆö merged via `clsx` onto the root. Consumer owns margin; Link has none.
- `...rest` ŌĆö full `AnchorHTMLAttributes<HTMLAnchorElement>` spread: `target`, `rel`, `aria-*`, `data-*`, event handlers, etc.
- `variant="default"` ŌĆö two gradient layers: accent grows 0%ŌåÆ100% on hover (layer 1); hairline always at 100% at rest (layer 2). `color: inherit`. `padding-bottom: 2px`. Transition: `background-size var(--dur-mid) var(--easing-link)`. Hover guarded by `@media (hover: hover)`. Direct transcription of the global `a` rule in `tokens.css`.
- `variant="quiet"` ŌĆö single gradient layer (accent only, pre-declared at 0% width). No hairline at rest. Hover grow identical to `default`. Pre-declaring a 0%-wide layer is required so the browser interpolates the grow ŌĆö `none` at rest would snap instead of animate.
- `variant="muted-link"` ŌĆö `color: var(--fg-muted)`, `background-image: none`, `transition: color var(--dur-fast) ease`. On hover: `color: var(--fg)`. No underline layers. `padding-bottom: 0`. Byte-for-byte match of the global `.muted-link` class in `tokens.css` ŌĆö the migration target for `SiteShell`, `Footer`, and story fixtures.
- External-link security: when `target="_blank"` is passed and no explicit `rel` is provided, the component auto-applies `rel="noopener noreferrer"`. Consumer-supplied `rel` always wins.
- Focus ring: inherits the global `a:focus-visible` rule (`outline: 2px solid var(--accent)`, `outline-offset: 4px`, `border-radius: var(--radius-1)`). The CSS module suppresses `a:focus` outline (double-ring prevention) and re-states `a:focus-visible` for explicitness.
- No visited state, no active translateY, no color prop. Same rationale as `EmailLink`.
- Tokens consumed: `--accent`, `--hairline`, `--fg-muted`, `--fg`, `--dur-mid`, `--dur-fast`, `--easing-link`, `--radius-1`. No new tokens introduced. The `1px` underline thickness and `2px` padding-bottom are literal values matching the global `a` rule (no `--text-underline-thickness` token exists yet).
- `displayName = "Link"`. `forwardRef<HTMLAnchorElement, LinkProps>`. Named export only.
- **Migration backlog (do NOT remove the global class in this PR).** The `.muted-link` global class in `tokens.css` is used in: `SiteShell.tsx`, `Footer.tsx`, `SiteShell.stories.tsx`, `a11y.test.tsx`, `System.stories.tsx`, `SiteShellFull.tsx`, `Hero.stories.tsx`. After `<Link variant="muted-link">` ships, each site should migrate. The global class must not be removed until an audit PR confirms all consumers (including the site repo) have migrated.
- Use `<Link href="/path">` for all styled navigational anchors. Do NOT use a raw `<a>` inside prose copy.
- Use `variant="muted-link"` inside `<SiteShell>` nav and `<Footer>` nav ŌĆö direct replacement for the `"muted-link"` className.
- Use `variant="quiet"` for nav lists where link nature is implicit from layout context. Do NOT use `quiet` in body paragraphs.
- Use `variant="default"` in prose body copy where the hairline discoverability signal is required.
- Do NOT use `<Link>` as a wrapper for `<Button>` ŌĆö they are sibling atoms.
- Do NOT add a `router` prop or bundle a framework adapter ŌĆö the DS is framework-agnostic. This item is closed as Won't in the ROADMAP.
- Do NOT add a `visited` style, `active` style, or `current` prop ŌĆö out of scope per spec ┬¦10.
- Do NOT add icon slots ŌĆö inline icon composition is the consumer's responsibility at the call site.
- Do NOT pass `href` when `asChild={true}` ŌĆö the child element owns the href in that mode.

```tsx
// Default ŌĆö prose body link with hairline underline at rest:
<Link href="/work">our approach to AI adoption</Link>

// Quiet ŌĆö nav item, underline on hover only:
<Link href="/why-ai" variant="quiet">Why AI</Link>

// MutedLink ŌĆö footer / secondary nav register:
<Link href="mailto:hello@pouk.ai" variant="muted-link">hello@pouk.ai</Link>

// External ŌĆö rel auto-applied:
<Link href="https://example.com/report" target="_blank">Industry report</Link>

// asChild ŌĆö Next.js router composition:
<Link href="/about" asChild>
  <NextLink href="/about">About</NextLink>
</Link>
```
- Do NOT re-export Lucide symbols from `@poukai-inc/ui` ŌĆö the consumer manages Lucide imports.

```tsx
import { Mail, AlertCircle } from "lucide-react";
import { Icon } from "@poukai-inc/ui";

// Decorative (default) ŌĆö inside a button label:
<button style={{ display: "inline-flex", alignItems: "center", gap: "var(--space-2)" }}>
  <Icon icon={Mail} size="sm" />
  Send email
</button>

// Semantic ŌĆö icon carries meaning not expressed in adjacent text:
<Icon icon={AlertCircle} size="md" decorative={false} aria-label="Warning: action required" />

// Inheriting color from parent:
<span style={{ color: "var(--fg-muted)" }}>
  <Icon icon={Mail} size="sm" />
</span>
```

### Logo
- Partner / customer logo cell. Composes `<Image>` ŌĆö the root is always a single `<img>` with no wrapper element.
- `src` (required string) ŌĆö logo image URL passed directly to `<Image>`.
- `alt` (required string) ŌĆö the organisation's name (e.g. `alt="Stripe"`). Required at the type level. Pass `alt=""` for purely decorative logos where the name is stated in adjacent text. NEVER omit.
- `tone` (`"color"` | `"mono"` | `"muted"`, default `"mono"`) ŌĆö visual treatment via CSS `filter` and `opacity`. `"color"`: no filter, full opacity. `"mono"`: `grayscale(1) brightness(0)` (true black silhouette), full opacity, static. `"muted"`: same filter + 0.55 opacity at rest; hover/focus-visible raises opacity to 1 with `transition: opacity var(--dur-fast) var(--easing)`.
- `size` (`"sm"` | `"md"` | `"lg"`, default `"md"`) ŌĆö max-height constraint: `"sm"` ŌåÆ 24px, `"md"` ŌåÆ 32px, `"lg"` ŌåÆ 40px. Width is always `auto` ŌĆö logos scale proportionally within the height cap.
- `width` (optional number, default 200) + `height` (optional number, default 80) ŌĆö passed to `<Image>` to drive `aspect-ratio` for CLS-safe slot reservation. Override with true intrinsic pixel dimensions when known. Defaults (200 ├Ś 80, 2.5:1 ratio) are a centroid for landscape wordmark logos.
- Inline style overrides passed to `<Image>`: `maxWidth: "none"` (removes Image's `max-width: 100%` so `width: auto` + `max-height` govern dimensions), `width: "auto"`. These combine with Image's baseline `height: auto` and `aspect-ratio`.
- Hover/focus-visible contract applies only to `tone="muted"`. `tone="color"` and `tone="mono"` are static ŌĆö no hover state.
- Reduced-motion: global `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` in `tokens.css` collapses the opacity transition to 0.01ms. The opacity change is preserved; only the animation collapses. No component-level reduced-motion handling needed.
- No new tokens introduced. Reuses `--dur-fast` (180ms) and `--easing` for the muted opacity transition.
- `forwardRef<HTMLImageElement, LogoProps>`, `displayName="Logo"`. `className` merged via `clsx` onto the root `<img>`. Remaining props (`data-*`, `aria-*`, `loading`, `decoding`) spread through `<Image>` to the `<img>`.
- Composition: wrap in `<a>` for linked logos ŌĆö use `tone="muted"` when inside a link so the hover state signals interactivity. The `<a>` is the interactive element; `<Logo>` is never a button or link itself.
- Do NOT wrap in a `<figure>` ŌĆö Logo is not captioned content. Do NOT use for person avatars/headshots ŌĆö use `<Avatar>`. Do NOT use for editorial photography ŌĆö use `<Image>` or `<Portrait>`. Logo is specifically for the logo-strip use case.
- Do NOT pass conflicting inline `style` for `maxHeight`, `width`, or `filter` ŌĆö those are Logo's sizing and tone contract. If you need to override, manage it in a consumer wrapper.
- Do NOT omit `alt`. Use `alt=""` for purely decorative logos, never omit the prop.
- Do NOT use `tone="muted"` when `<Logo>` is not inside an interactive element ŌĆö the hover affordance would be misleading.

### Input
**Atomic layer: atom.** Import from `@poukai-inc/ui/atoms/Input`. Molecule path `@poukai-inc/ui/molecules/Input` is a deprecated re-export (works in 1.x, removed in 2.0).
- Single-line text input atom. Root is always `<input>`. Non-polymorphic. Ref forwarded to `HTMLInputElement`.
- `type` (`"text"` | `"email"` | `"url"` | `"tel"` | `"password"` | `"search"` | `"number"`, default `"text"`) ŌĆö constrained to single-line text-like types.
- `size` (`"sm"` | `"md"` | `"lg"`, default `"md"`) ŌĆö visual height via shared `--btn-h-*` ladder. Pairs with `<Button size="ŌĆ”">` on the same row.
- `invalid` (optional boolean) ŌĆö sets `data-invalid="true"` and `aria-invalid="true"`; border ŌåÆ `--danger`. Prefer wiring through `<Field error="ŌĆ”">`.
- `Omit<ComponentPropsWithoutRef<"input">, "size">` spread. Native HTML `size` attribute intentionally shadowed by DS `size` prop.
- `clsx` className merge via `className` prop.
- States: default (`--hairline` border, `--bg-elevated` bg), hover (border ŌåÆ `--fg-muted`, skipped on disabled/readonly), focus-visible (2px `--accent` outline + border), invalid (`--danger` border), disabled (`opacity: 0.5; cursor: not-allowed`), readonly (`cursor: default`).
- Tokens: `--bg-elevated`, `--hairline`, `--fg-muted`, `--fg`, `--accent`, `--danger`, `--font-sans`, `--fs-body`, `--lh-body`, `--space-2`, `--space-3`, `--radius-2`, `--dur-fast`, `--easing`, `--btn-h-sm`, `--btn-h-md`, `--btn-h-lg`. No new tokens.
- Idiomatic usage wraps Input in `<Field>`. Standalone usage is valid; consumer owns label association.
- Do NOT use for multi-line text ŌĆö use `<Textarea>`.
- Do NOT pass `type="checkbox"`, `type="radio"`, `type="file"`, `type="range"`, `type="date"` ŌĆö separate atoms.
- Do NOT add leading/trailing icon slots ŌĆö belongs to future `SearchField` molecule.

```tsx
// Inside Field (recommended):
<Field label="Email address" id="email" helper="We'll never share your email.">
  <Input type="email" placeholder="you@example.com" />
</Field>

// Standalone (consumer owns label):
<label htmlFor="search">Search</label>
<Input id="search" type="search" />
```

### Textarea
- **Atom layer.** Multi-line textarea primitive. Root is always `<textarea>`. Non-polymorphic. Ref forwarded to `HTMLTextAreaElement`. Label/helper/error are NOT owned by Textarea ŌĆö they live in `<Field>`.
- Import from `@poukai-inc/ui` (root) or `@poukai-inc/ui/atoms/Textarea`. The legacy `@poukai-inc/ui/molecules/Textarea` path is a `@deprecated` re-export shim and will be removed in v2.0.
- `invalid` (optional boolean) ŌĆö when true, sets `data-invalid="true"` and `aria-invalid="true"`, applies `--danger` border. Prefer wiring through `<Field error="ŌĆ”">`.
- `rows` ŌĆö default `3`. Drives the natural rendered height.
- `resize` ŌĆö `"vertical" | "none" | "both"`, default `"vertical"`. Maps directly to the CSS `resize` property on the root. `"both"` is allowed but discouraged ŌĆö horizontal drag breaks most responsive layouts.
- Standard `ComponentPropsWithoutRef<"textarea">` spread: `value`, `onChange`, `placeholder`, `disabled`, `readOnly`, `required`, `name`, `aria-*`, `data-*`, etc.
- `clsx` className merge via `className` prop.
- States: default, hover, focus-visible, readonly, invalid, disabled. Visual register mirrors the `Input` atom exactly.
- Tokens consumed: `--bg-elevated`, `--hairline`, `--fg-muted`, `--fg`, `--accent`, `--danger`, `--font-sans`, `--fs-body`, `--lh-body`, `--space-3`, `--radius-2`, `--dur-fast`, `--easing`. Strict subset of the `Input` atom's tokens. No new tokens.
- Padding contract: **symmetric `--space-3` on all four sides** (block + inline). This is the only visual delta from `Input` (which uses `--space-2` block / `--space-3` inline). Multi-line content needs equal vertical headroom so the first and last lines do not feel cramped.
- Do NOT add character count display ŌĆö consumer responsibility.
- Do NOT use Textarea for single-line input ŌĆö use `<Input>`.
- Do NOT add autosize / content-height tracking inside the atom. A future `useAutosizeTextarea` utility hook will compose on top; tracked separately.

```tsx
// Inside Field (recommended):
<Field label="Message" id="message">
  <Textarea placeholder="Tell us about your projectŌĆ”" rows={6} />
</Field>

// Resize locked (fixed-height note):
<Field label="Internal note">
  <Textarea rows={2} resize="none" placeholder="Short noteŌĆ”" />
</Field>
```

### Select
**Atomic layer: atom.** Import from `@poukai-inc/ui/atoms/Select` or `@poukai-inc/ui`.
- Native `<select>` atom. Root is always `<select>`. Non-polymorphic. Ref forwarded to `HTMLSelectElement`. Children are `<option>` / `<optgroup>` nodes ŌĆö consumer-supplied.
- `size` (`"sm"` | `"md"` | `"lg"`, default `"md"`) ŌĆö visual height via shared `--btn-h-*` ladder. Pairs with `<Input size="ŌĆ”">` and `<Button size="ŌĆ”">` on the same row.
- `invalid` (optional boolean) ŌĆö sets `data-invalid="true"` and `aria-invalid="true"`; border ŌåÆ `--danger`. Prefer wiring through `<Field error="ŌĆ”">`.
- `Omit<ComponentPropsWithoutRef<"select">, "size">` spread. Native HTML `size` attribute intentionally shadowed by DS `size` prop.
- `clsx` className merge via `className` prop.
- Trailing caret: CSS `background-image` SVG data URL (chevron-down, 16├Ś16, 1.5px round stroke). Color hardcoded per color scheme (`#1d1d1f` light, `#f5f5f7` dark) ŌĆö `currentColor` is unavailable in SVG background images. Extra inline-end padding reserves room. RTL flips caret. `[multiple]` hides caret and restores native appearance.
- States: default (`--hairline` border, `--bg-elevated` bg), hover (border ŌåÆ `--fg-muted`, gated `@media (hover: hover)`, skipped on disabled), focus-visible (2px `--accent` outline + border), invalid (`--danger` border), disabled (`opacity: 0.5; cursor: not-allowed`).
- Tokens: `--bg-elevated`, `--hairline`, `--fg-muted`, `--fg`, `--accent`, `--danger`, `--font-sans`, `--fs-body`, `--lh-body`, `--space-2`, `--space-3`, `--radius-2`, `--dur-fast`, `--easing`, `--btn-h-sm`, `--btn-h-md`, `--btn-h-lg`. No new tokens.
- Idiomatic usage wraps Select in `<Field>`. Standalone usage valid; consumer owns label association.
- Do NOT build a custom dropdown with this ŌĆö use the native platform menu. Portal listbox / combobox is a separate future atom-pair.
- Do NOT render Icon atoms or arbitrary children inside `<select>` ŌĆö the platform discards them. The caret is CSS-only.

```tsx
// Inside Field (recommended):
<Field label="Country" id="country" helper="Used for tax calculation.">
  <Select name="country" defaultValue="us">
    <option value="us">United States</option>
    <option value="ca">Canada</option>
  </Select>
</Field>

// Standalone (consumer owns label):
<label htmlFor="sort">Sort by</label>
<Select id="sort" size="sm" defaultValue="recent">
  <option value="recent">Most recent</option>
  <option value="popular">Most popular</option>
</Select>
```

### Field
- Composition wrapper that connects a visible label, optional helper text, and optional error message to a single form control (`<Input>`, `<Textarea>`, `<Select>`, or any HTML form element).
- Root element: `<div>`. Non-polymorphic. Ref forwarded to `HTMLDivElement`. `...rest` spread passes `data-*`, `aria-*`, `className`, `style`, etc.
- `label` (required string) ŌĆö visible label text. Rendered as `<label htmlFor={id}>`. Every Field must have a label.
- `id` (optional string) ŌĆö auto-generated via `useId()` when not provided. Wires `<label htmlFor>` and the child control's `id`.
- `helper` (optional string) ŌĆö helper text rendered below the control in `--fg-muted` register. Not rendered when `error` is also set.
- `error` (optional string) ŌĆö error message rendered below the control with `role="alert"`, `--fg` color, and a 2px `--accent` left-rule border. When set: replaces helper text, injects `invalid={true}` and `aria-invalid="true"` onto the child, includes error element id in `aria-describedby`.
- `required` (optional boolean) ŌĆö adds visible `*` indicator (in `<span aria-hidden="true">`) next to label, and injects `required` onto child. The `aria-hidden` hides `*` from AT; the `required` attribute conveys it semantically.
- `children` (ReactElement) ŌĆö exactly one form control. Field clones it via `React.cloneElement` to inject `id`, `aria-describedby`, `aria-invalid`, `invalid`, and `required` as needed.
- `cloneElement` injection table:
  - `id` ŌĆö always injected (wires label)
  - `aria-describedby` ŌĆö injected when helper or error is set
  - `aria-invalid="true"` ŌĆö injected when error is set
  - `invalid={true}` ŌĆö injected when error is set (for DS Input/Textarea styling)
  - `required={true}` ŌĆö injected when required is set
- `aria-describedby` points at error element id when error is present; helper element id when only helper is present.
- Error `<p>` has `role="alert"` ŌĆö announces error message to screen readers on mount.
- Layout: `display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: var(--space-1)`.
- Label typography: `--fs-meta`, `font-weight: 500`, `--fg`, `cursor: pointer`.
- Helper typography: `--fs-meta`, `--fg-muted`, `--lh-meta`, `margin-top: var(--space-1)`.
- Error typography: `--fs-meta`, `--fg`, `--lh-meta`, `margin-top: var(--space-1)`, `border-inline-start: 2px solid var(--accent)`, `padding-inline-start: var(--space-2)`.
- Tokens consumed: `--fs-meta`, `--lh-meta`, `--font-sans`, `--fg`, `--fg-muted`, `--accent`, `--space-1`, `--space-2`. No new tokens.
- Do NOT add a Form wrapper organism here ŌĆö that is a separate future organism.
- Do NOT pass multiple children ŌĆö Field supports exactly one form control.
- Do NOT add inline vs. stacked label variants in v1 ŌĆö stacked only.
- Do NOT add floating label ŌĆö not brand-aligned.

```tsx
// (a) Field + Input (email):
<Field label="Email address" id="email" helper="We'll never share your email.">
  <Input type="email" placeholder="you@example.com" />
</Field>

// (b) Field + Textarea (message):
<Field label="Message" id="message">
  <Textarea placeholder="Tell us about your projectŌĆ”" />
</Field>

// (c) Field with error:
<Field label="Email address" id="email-err" error="Please enter a valid email address.">
  <Input type="email" defaultValue="not-an-email" />
</Field>

// (d) Required field:
<Field label="Full name" id="name" required>
  <Input placeholder="Arian Zargaran" />
</Field>
```

### FormRow
- Horizontal multi-Field layout molecule. Places two or more `Field` instances side-by-side with consistent column widths and gap rhythm; collapses to single-column stack below `--bp-md` (768px).
- Root element: `<div>`. No landmark semantics ŌĆö purely a layout wrapper. `forwardRef<HTMLDivElement, FormRowProps>`. `...rest` spread passes `data-*`, `aria-*`, `className`, `style`, etc. `displayName = "FormRow"`.
- `gap` (optional `"default" | "tight"`, default `"default"`) ŌĆö column gap between Fields. `"default"` = `--space-4`; `"tight"` = `--space-2` for dense forms.
- `columns` (optional `number`) ŌĆö explicit column count. Defaults to `auto-fill` (1fr per child). Sets `--formrow-columns` CSS custom property and activates explicit `repeat(N, 1fr)` grid track.
- `children` (`ReactNode`) ŌĆö one or more `Field` molecules. 2ŌĆō4 recommended; more than 4 reads as cramped.
- CSS grid layout: `display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fill, minmax(0, 1fr))`. With explicit `columns` prop: `repeat(var(--formrow-columns), 1fr)`.
- Collapses to `grid-template-columns: 1fr` below `@media (max-width: 767px)` (just under `--bp-md`).
- Tokens consumed: `--space-2`, `--space-4`, `--space-6`, `--bp-md`. No new tokens.
- Not a fieldset replacement ŌĆö provides no semantic grouping. Wrap in `Fieldset` when `<fieldset>`/`<legend>` grouping is needed.
- Do NOT use for more than 4 columns. Do NOT use for unrelated fields. Do NOT use as a data-table row.

```tsx
// (a) First name / Last name:
<FormRow>
  <Field label="First name" id="first-name"><Input /></Field>
  <Field label="Last name" id="last-name"><Input /></Field>
</FormRow>

// (b) City / State / ZIP (tight gap, explicit 3 columns):
<FormRow gap="tight" columns={3}>
  <Field label="City" id="city"><Input /></Field>
  <Field label="State" id="state"><Input /></Field>
  <Field label="ZIP" id="zip"><Input /></Field>
</FormRow>
```

### Banner
- Persistent inline notice molecule. Sits in document flow; not a toast, not a modal, not a status indicator.
- Root element: `<div>`. `forwardRef<HTMLDivElement, BannerProps>`. `...rest` spread. `displayName = "Banner"`.
- `tone` (optional `"info" | "warning" | "danger" | "success"`, default `"info"`) ŌĆö controls color, ARIA role, and left rule.
- `icon` (optional `ReactNode`) ŌĆö leading icon slot. No auto-icon. Consumer passes decorative SVG with `aria-hidden="true"`.
- `action` (optional `ReactNode`) ŌĆö trailing action slot. Typically `<Button variant="ghost">`. Natural focus order after body.
- `children` (required `ReactNode`) ŌĆö body content.
- A11y: `role="status"` (polite live region) for `info` and `success`; `role="alert"` (assertive) for `warning` and `danger`.
- Tone color table:
  - `info`: `--bg` background, `--fg` text, `--hairline-w` / `--hairline` border all sides.
  - `warning`: `--bg-warm-accent` background, `--fg-on-warm` text, 2px `--fg-on-warm-muted` border-inline-start, no full border.
  - `danger`: `--bg-warm-accent` background, `--fg-on-warm` text, 2px `--fg-on-warm-muted` border-inline-start, no full border.
  - `success`: `--bg-elevated` background, `--fg` text, 2px `--accent` border-inline-start, `--hairline-w` / `--hairline` full border.
- Layout: flex row, `align-items: flex-start`, `flex-wrap: wrap`. Icon and action are `flex-shrink: 0`; body is `flex: 1`.
- Tokens consumed: `--bg`, `--bg-elevated`, `--bg-warm-accent`, `--fg`, `--fg-on-warm`, `--fg-on-warm-muted`, `--hairline`, `--hairline-w`, `--accent`, `--font-sans`, `--fs-body`, `--lh-body`, `--space-2`, `--space-3`, `--space-4`, `--radius-2`. Zero new tokens.
- Static and persistent. No dismissal, no animation, no timing in v1. Consumer controls mount/unmount.
- Toast (ephemeral overlay) is deferred ŌĆö requires a Provider, queue, and portal.
- Do NOT add auto-icons per tone ŌĆö icon is always a consumer-controlled slot.
- Do NOT add dismissal logic or animation in v1.
- Do NOT introduce danger/warning/success color tokens ŌĆö re-use the warm-accent tier.

```tsx
// (a) Info ŌĆö quiet on-page notice:
<Banner tone="info">Your session will expire in 15 minutes.</Banner>

// (b) Warning ŌĆö assertive, warm-accent surface:
<Banner tone="warning">API key expires in 3 days. Rotate before the deadline.</Banner>

// (c) Danger ŌĆö assertive, same warm register as warning:
<Banner tone="danger">Deployment failed. Check the logs and redeploy.</Banner>

// (d) Success ŌĆö elevated surface, accent left rule:
<Banner tone="success">Deployment complete. Your changes are live.</Banner>

// (e) With icon and action slots:
<Banner
  tone="warning"
  icon={<svg aria-hidden="true">ŌĆ”</svg>}
  action={<Button variant="ghost" size="sm">Rotate key</Button>}
>
  API key expires in 3 days.
</Banner>
```

### ContactBlock
- Organism layer. End-of-page contact moment ŌĆö email link + optional status signal + optional CTA actions.
- Composes `Section` frame + `EmailLink` atom + `StatusBadge` slot + `actions` ReactNode slot in a centered flex column.
- Props: `email` (required string), `emailLabel` (optional string ŌĆö visible link text override), `heading` (optional string ŌĆö `--fs-h2` Instrument Serif when provided), `status` (optional ReactNode ŌĆö `StatusBadge` slot), `actions` (optional ReactNode ŌĆö Button(s) slot), `as` (`"section"` | `"article"` | `"div"`, default `"section"`).
- EmailLink renders at `--fs-h3` weight (1.125rem / 18px) ŌĆö display scale, not footer scale.
- When `heading` is provided and `as` is a landmark element, root gets `aria-labelledby` pointing at the heading.
- No `tone` or `variant` prop ŌĆö one visual register only: centered, editorial, generous whitespace (`--space-16` block padding).
- A11y: `<section>` landmark named via `aria-labelledby` when heading present. EmailLink `<a href="mailto:ŌĆ”">` accessible name = visible label or email string. StatusBadge is non-interactive `<span>`. Buttons in actions slot carry their own labels.
- Keyboard order: heading (no tab stop) ŌåÆ EmailLink ŌåÆ Button(s) in DOM order.
- Do NOT use as a footer substitute ŌĆö `ContactBlock` is a content-section moment; `Footer` is persistent page chrome.
- Do NOT embed a form ŌĆö use a separate `Form` organism for multi-field contact forms.
- Import: `import { ContactBlock } from "@poukai-inc/ui"` or `"@poukai-inc/ui/organisms/ContactBlock"`.

### HeroSection
- Organism layer. Marketing-page primary hero band ŌĆö the sole `<h1>` moment on any page.
- Composes `Hero` molecule (title, status, lede, CTA, entrance stagger) inside a landmark `<section>`.
- Optional `media` slot: two-column CSS grid at `md+` (Ōēź 768px), text left (`--hero-max`), media right (`--hero-illustration-max`). Single stacked column below.
- Props: `title` (required), `status`, `lede`, `cta`, `media`, `size` (`"display"` | `"intimate"`), `entrance` (`"stagger"`), `sectionSize` (`"default"` | `"tight"`), `as` (`"section"` | `"article"` | `"div"`).
- `sectionSize` controls block padding independently of Hero's `size`. Default: `--space-16`; tight: `--space-12`.
- A11y: root `<section>` is a region landmark via `aria-labelledby` pointing at the text column (which contains the `<h1>`). One HeroSection per page only.
- Media column follows text column in DOM order ŌĆö do NOT reorder with CSS `order`.
- Do NOT nest HeroSection inside Section or another HeroSection.
- Do NOT use HeroSection when no title is needed ŌĆö use `Hero variant="no-title"` instead.
- Import: `import { HeroSection } from "@poukai-inc/ui"` or `"@poukai-inc/ui/organisms/HeroSection"`.

### StatsSection
- Organism layer. Marketing-surface band that frames a `StatList` inside a `Section`. Owns the "by the numbers" editorial moment on landing pages.
- Composes `Section` molecule (band wrapper, heading slot, block padding) + `StatList` molecule (horizontal stat row, optional dividers).
- Props: `heading` (optional string) ŌĆö renders as `<h2>` via Section; omit on pure-data surfaces. `dividers` (boolean, default `false`) ŌĆö hairline rules between stats; use when stat count Ōēź 3. `fill` (boolean, default `false`) ŌĆö applies `--surface-section` background; default is transparent (`--bg`). `children` (required ReactNode) ŌĆö one or more `Stat` atoms.
- No `centered` prop ŌĆö alignment is always left-aligned per brand register (consistent with Section and Footer conventions). StatList `align` is set to `"center"` internally for the marketing default.
- A11y: root `<section>` is a region landmark. When `heading` is provided, `aria-labelledby` is wired to the `<h2>` via Section. Each `Stat` value + label pair is adjacent block elements; no extra ARIA needed.
- Static display-only. No hover, active, or keyboard interaction on the band. Individual `Stat` atoms carry no interactive affordance.
- Tokens: `--surface-section` (fill background), `--bg` (default background), `--space-16` (block padding via Section), `--content-max` (max-width via Section), `--page-pad` (horizontal padding via Section).
- Do NOT use for interactive data, tabular data, progress indicators, chart replacements, or count-up animations.
- Do NOT nest StatsSection inside another StatsSection or HeroSection.
- Import: `import { StatsSection } from "@poukai-inc/ui"` or `"@poukai-inc/ui/organisms/StatsSection"`.

### CTASection
- Organism layer. Full-width end-of-page conversion band. Wraps `CtaBlock` in a landmark `<section>` with optional bleed-band surface.
- Root element: `<section>` (default) or `<div>`. `forwardRef<HTMLElement, CTASectionProps>`. `...rest` spread. `displayName = "CTASection"`.
- Props: `heading` (required `string`), `body` (optional `ReactNode`), `actions` (required `ReactNode`), `surface` (`"default"` | `"recessed"`, default `"default"`), `size` (`"default"` | `"tight"`, default `"default"`), `align` (`"center"` | `"start"`, default `"center"`), `headingAs` (`"h2"` | `"h3"`, default `"h2"`), `as` (`"section"` | `"div"`).
- `surface="recessed"` applies `--surface-section` background + `--hairline-w` / `--hairline` top border rule.
- `size` maps to `--space-16` (default) or `--space-12` (tight) block padding.
- `align="center"` is the brand-correct default for end-of-page CTA ŌĆö bilateral symmetry signals "this is the conclusion." `align="start"` for editorial surfaces.
- A11y: `<section aria-labelledby="{id}-title">` names the region; `CtaBlock` heading receives matching `id`. Heading level `h2` by default; override with `headingAs="h3"` for nested contexts.
- Do NOT use CTASection mid-page. For inline conversion prompts, use `CtaBlock` directly inside `Section`.
- Do NOT nest inside another `Section` ŌĆö CTASection owns its own frame and block padding.
- Do NOT use more than two actions in the `actions` slot.
- Tokens: `--bg`, `--surface-section`, `--hairline`, `--hairline-w`, `--space-16`, `--space-12`, `--content-max`, `--page-pad`. Typography tokens delegated to `CtaBlock`.
- Import: `import { CTASection } from "@poukai-inc/ui"` or `"@poukai-inc/ui/organisms/CTASection"`.

```tsx
// Default (centered, page bg):
<CTASection
  heading="Ready to start?"
  body="Spin up your first project in minutes."
  actions={<Button>Get a demo</Button>}
/>

// Recessed surface (end-of-page closing band):
<CTASection
  surface="recessed"
  heading="Ready to start?"
  actions={<><Button>Get a demo</Button><Button variant="secondary">See our work</Button></>}
/>
```

### CtaBlock
- Heading + body + action row molecule. The reusable inline conversion moment ŌĆö "Ready to start? [Get a demo]" pattern.
- Root element: `<div>`. `forwardRef<HTMLDivElement, CtaBlockProps>`. `...rest` spread. `displayName = "CtaBlock"`.
- `heading` (required `ReactNode`) ŌĆö primary CTA text. Rendered as `headingAs` element.
- `headingAs` (optional `"h1" | "h2" | "h3"`, default `"h2"`) ŌĆö semantic heading level. `h2` is correct below a Hero `h1`; override for nested contexts.
- `body` (optional `ReactNode`) ŌĆö supporting paragraph. Absent: heading jumps to actions with `--space-8` gap instead of `--space-6`.
- `actions` (required `ReactNode`) ŌĆö the CTA button(s). Consumer owns Button composition; CtaBlock provides the slot container and spacing.
- `orientation` (optional `"stacked" | "horizontal"`, default `"stacked"`) ŌĆö `stacked` stacks vertically; `horizontal` puts heading+body left, actions trailing-aligned (collapses to stacked below `--bp-md`).
- `align` (optional `"start" | "center"`, default `"start"`) ŌĆö `start` matches brand's left-aligned register; `center` for symmetrical moments (e.g. full-bleed CTASection).
- No background, no border, no padding ŌĆö NOT a card, NOT a section, NOT a Hero replacement.
- A11y: heading element determined by `headingAs`; consumer maintains correct heading hierarchy. No ARIA wrapper on root or actions slot.
- Tokens consumed: `--font-serif`, `--fs-h2`, `--font-sans`, `--fs-body`, `--lh-body`, `--fg`, `--fg-muted`, `--space-2`, `--space-6`, `--space-8`, `--bp-md`. Zero new tokens.
- For end-of-page placement, compose inside `CTASection` (organism) ŌĆö CtaBlock has no block padding of its own.

```tsx
// (a) Stacked default:
<CtaBlock
  heading="Ready to start?"
  body="We work with senior-only teams who'd rather ship than speculate."
  actions={<Button variant="primary">Get a demo</Button>}
/>

// (b) Horizontal ŌĆö heading+body left, action right:
<CtaBlock
  orientation="horizontal"
  heading="Interested in working together?"
  body="Senior-only teams. Production from day one."
  actions={<Button variant="primary">hello@pouk.ai</Button>}
/>

// (c) No body (heading jumps directly to actions with --space-8 gap):
<CtaBlock
  heading="Interested in working together?"
  actions={<Button variant="primary" asChild><a href="mailto:hello@pouk.ai">hello@pouk.ai</a></Button>}
/>
```

### Stepper
- Numbered step indicator molecule for multi-step flows and process explainers.
- Root element: `<ol>`. `forwardRef<HTMLOListElement, StepperProps>`. `...rest` spread to root.
- Props: `steps` (required `Array<{ label: string }>`), `current` (required 0-based index), `orientation` (`"horizontal" | "vertical"`, default `"horizontal"`), `size` (`"sm" | "md"`, default `"md"`), `showLabels` (boolean, default true for md / false for sm).
- State derived from `current`: index < current ŌåÆ complete (checkmark icon, visually-hidden "(complete)"); index === current ŌåÆ active (numeral, `aria-current="step"`); index > current ŌåÆ upcoming (muted numeral).
- Display-only indicator. No interactive states, no click handlers, no hover styles. Consumer owns navigation.
- Tokens: `--fg`, `--fg-muted`, `--accent`, `--surface`, `--bg-elevated`, `--hairline`, `--hairline-w`, `--font-sans`, `--fs-meta`, `--fs-micro`, `--lh-meta`, `--space-1`, `--space-2`, `--space-4`, `--space-8`, `--tracking-micro`. Marker diameter is hardcoded (28px md / 20px sm) ŌĆö no diameter token exists.
- A11y: `<ol>` root, `aria-current="step"` on active `<li>`, complete steps have visually-hidden "(complete)" suffix, checkmark icon carries `aria-hidden="true"`, connector spans carry `aria-hidden="true"`.
- Do NOT use Stepper as a navigation tab bar (use Tabs). Do NOT use for non-linear processes. Do NOT use as a progress bar (use ProgressBar).
- Import: `import { Stepper } from "@poukai-inc/ui"` or `import { Stepper } from "@poukai-inc/ui/molecules/Stepper"`.
- Usage: `<Stepper steps={[{ label: "Account" }, { label: "Profile" }, { label: "Confirm" }]} current={1} />`

### Byline
- Single-author article attribution molecule. The horizontal "by Jane Doe ┬Ę Editor ┬Ę May 21, 2026 ┬Ę 6 min read" row that appears directly below article headings or blog post titles.
- Composes Avatar, semantic text elements (name as strong, role as span), Time, and read-time text into a stable horizontal flex row. Byline owns layout and typographic register; introduces zero new tokens.
- Static presentational molecule. Not interactive. No state.
- name (required string) ŌĆö rendered inside strong. Primary text, --fg, --fs-meta, weight 500.
- avatar (optional string) ŌĆö image URL. When present, Avatar renders in mode="image" with alt="" (decorative ŌĆö name is already visible as text).
- initials (optional string) ŌĆö 1-2 char fallback. Used when avatar is absent. Renders Avatar in mode="initials". Ignored when avatar is provided.
- When neither avatar nor initials is provided, Avatar renders in mode="empty" (blank placeholder).
- role (optional string) ŌĆö author role label (e.g. "Editor"). Rendered as span in micro uppercase below name. Omitted when absent. Uses --fs-micro, --tracking-micro, text-transform: uppercase, --fg-muted.
- publishedAt (optional string | Date) ŌĆö forwarded to Time dateTime with format="absolute". Omitted when absent.
- readTime (optional string) ŌĆö consumer-formatted read-time string (e.g. "6 min read"). Rendered verbatim. Omitted when absent.
- Separator dots rendered as span[aria-hidden="true"] with padding-inline: var(--space-2). Dots appear only when trailing content (publishedAt or readTime) is present.
- Trailing content state matrix: publishedAt + readTime: "┬Ę Time ┬Ę N min read"; publishedAt only: "┬Ę Time"; readTime only: "┬Ę N min read"; neither: no dots, no trailing content.
- Root: div, forwardRef<HTMLDivElement, BylineProps>. ...rest spread to root. displayName = "Byline".
- Layout: display: flex, align-items: center, flex-wrap: nowrap, gap: var(--space-3). Color baseline: --fg-muted, --font-sans, --fs-meta, --lh-meta.
- Text column: display: flex, flex-direction: column, gap: var(--space-1), min-width: 0.
- Tokens consumed: --fs-meta, --fs-micro, --tracking-micro, --fg, --fg-muted, --font-sans, --lh-meta, --space-1, --space-2, --space-3. Zero new tokens.
- A11y: root div with no ARIA role. Avatar is decorative (aria-hidden by default). Dots have aria-hidden="true". Time renders native time[datetime]. Name in strong for semantic emphasis.
- Sits outside Prose. Parent owns spacing between h1/h2 and Byline. Single-author only.
- Do NOT pass an accessible name or alt to the Avatar inside Byline ŌĆö the author name is already visible as text.
- Do NOT use Byline for team grids or person cards ŌĆö that is TeamCard.
- Do NOT add authorHref, timeFormat prop, responsive collapse, or skeleton state ŌĆö all out of scope in v1.

### TeamGrid
- Section-framed responsive grid of `TeamCard` molecules. Presents the "about the team" surface: portrait + name + role cards with consistent column rhythm and correct semantic structure.
- Composes `Section` organism for heading, optional eyebrow, optional lede, and section background. TeamGrid passes those props straight through to Section ŌĆö the Section `<section>` element is the root.
- `heading` (required `string`) ŌĆö Section heading, e.g. `"The team"`. A nameless team grid is not a valid surface.
- `eyebrow` (optional `string`) ŌĆö micro-label above the heading, passed to Section.
- `lede` (optional `string`) ŌĆö supporting sentence below heading, passed to Section.
- `columns` (`2 | 3`, default `3`) ŌĆö maximum column count at wide viewports. Below `--bp-md` (768px): always 1 column. Between `--bp-md` and 1024px: always 2 columns regardless of this prop. At Ōēź1024px: `columns=3` ŌåÆ 3 columns; `columns=2` ŌåÆ 2 columns.
- `tone` (`"default" | "section"`, default `"default"`) ŌĆö passed to Section. `"section"` applies `--surface-section` band background.
- `children` (required `ReactNode`) ŌĆö `TeamCard` instances. TeamGrid does not validate or wrap individual cards; it positions whatever is slotted.
- Grid gap: `--space-6` (1.5rem) column and row at all breakpoints. Top margin between Section heading block and grid: `--space-16` (4rem).
- `forwardRef` forwards to the Section root (`<section>` element). `className`, `data-*`, and `aria-*` spread to Section root.
- Tokens consumed: `--space-6`, `--space-16`, `--surface-section`. No new tokens introduced.
- Do NOT use TeamGrid for non-person content ŌĆö use FeatureGrid for features, RoleGrid for roles.
- Do NOT set `columns={3}` for a 2-person team ŌĆö use `columns={2}` to prevent an orphan card.
- Do NOT exceed ~12 cards without a filtering or pagination mechanism.
- Do NOT strip the Section wrapper ŌĆö the heading/landmark contract requires it.

```tsx
<TeamGrid
  heading="The team"
  eyebrow="Who we are"
  lede="Senior practitioners only."
>
  <TeamCard portrait={<Portrait src="ŌĆ”" alt="ŌĆ”" aspect="1:1" width={800} />} name="Arian Zargaran" role="Founder, Engineering" />
  <TeamCard portrait={<Portrait src="ŌĆ”" alt="ŌĆ”" aspect="1:1" width={800} />} name="Jane Doe" role="Design Lead" />
  <TeamCard portrait={<Portrait src="ŌĆ”" alt="ŌĆ”" aspect="1:1" width={800} />} name="John Smith" role="Engineering" />
</TeamGrid>
```

### MenuItem
- Individual menu-row primitive. Composed inside DropdownMenu and ContextMenu wrappers. Not for standalone use.
- Owns visual row shape only: typography, spacing, icon alignment, shortcut placement. ARIA roles, keyboard nav, focus management, and portal behaviour are entirely delegated to the parent Radix wrapper.
- Root element: `<div>`. `forwardRef<HTMLDivElement, MenuItemProps>`. `...rest` spread. `displayName = "MenuItem"`.
- `children` (required `ReactNode`) ŌĆö visible label. Plain string expected; `ReactNode` for occasional `<strong>` emphasis.
- `icon` (optional `ReactNode`) ŌĆö leading icon slot at `--icon-sm` (16px). Consumer supplies icon with `aria-hidden="true"`. Decorative only.
- `shortcut` (optional `string`) ŌĆö rendered as `<Kbd aria-hidden="true">`. Display-only; not a functional key binding.
- `tone` (optional `"default" | "danger"`, default `"default"`) ŌĆö `"danger"` shifts label color to `--danger` for destructive rows.
- `disabled` (optional `boolean`, default `false`) ŌĆö `opacity: 0.4`, `pointer-events: none`. Row stays visible. Radix propagates `aria-disabled`.
- Hover: `background-color: var(--surface)`; transition `var(--dur-fast) var(--easing)`. Active: `opacity: 0.85` for `var(--dur-press)`.
- Tokens consumed: `--font-sans`, `--fs-meta`, `--lh-meta`, `--fg`, `--fg-muted`, `--danger`, `--surface`, `--space-2`, `--space-3`, `--radius-2`, `--icon-sm`, `--dur-fast`, `--dur-press`, `--easing`. Zero new tokens.
- A11y: no ARIA role on root ŌĆö parent Radix host carries `role="menuitem"`. Icon slot wrapped in `aria-hidden="true"` span. Kbd shortcut has `aria-hidden="true"`.
- Do NOT use MenuItem outside a Radix DropdownMenu / ContextMenu wrapper. It has no open/close logic.
- Do NOT nest `<Button>` or `<a>` inside MenuItem ŌĆö creates duplicate tab stops and ARIA violations.
- Do NOT use for navigation links in a nav bar ŌĆö that is NavLink.

```tsx
// (a) Label only:
<MenuItem>Copy</MenuItem>

// (b) With icon:
<MenuItem icon={<Copy size={16} aria-hidden="true" />}>Copy</MenuItem>

// (c) With shortcut:
<MenuItem shortcut="ŌīśC">Copy</MenuItem>

// (d) Full anatomy:
<MenuItem icon={<Copy size={16} aria-hidden="true" />} shortcut="ŌīśC">Copy</MenuItem>

// (e) Danger tone:
<MenuItem tone="danger">Delete post</MenuItem>
```

### TimelineItem
- Single entry in a sequential chronological list. Renders a date, title, and optional body copy with a left-rail marker dot and optional connector line to the next item.
- Must live inside a `<ol>` supplied by a parent Timeline organism. Do NOT use standalone.
- Composes Time atom (semantic `<time dateTime>`), Heading atom (title slot), and Text atom (body slot). Marker dot and connector rendered via CSS `::before` / `::after` pseudo-elements ŌĆö no extra DOM nodes.
- date (required string) ŌĆö ISO 8601 string; forwarded to Time dateTime. Displayed as-is in the date slot (uppercase micro register).
- title (required ReactNode) ŌĆö entry heading. Rendered via Heading atom at the level specified by headingLevel.
- body (optional ReactNode) ŌĆö supporting copy. Omitted when absent; some milestone entries are title-only.
- connector (boolean, default true) ŌĆö whether to render the downward connector stub. Pass false for the last item in the list.
- headingLevel (HeadingLevel, default "h3") ŌĆö semantic heading level for the title. Decoupled from visual size so the document outline can differ from the visual rank.
- Root: `<li>`, forwardRef<HTMLLIElement, TimelineItemProps>. ...rest spread to root. displayName = "TimelineItem".
- Date slot: `--fs-meta`, `--lh-meta`, `--tracking-micro`, text-transform uppercase, color `--fg-muted`. margin-bottom `--space-1`.
- Title slot: `--fs-h3` (via Heading atom sizeH3 class). margin-bottom `--space-2`.
- Body slot: `--fs-body`, `--lh-body`, color `--fg-muted` (via Text atom toneMuted class).
- Marker dot: 8px circle, `--accent` fill, `::before` pseudo-element ŌĆö purely decorative, no ARIA.
- Connector: `--hairline-w` wide, `--hairline` color vertical rule, `::after` pseudo-element ŌĆö present when connector=true, absent when connector=false.
- Left-rail gutter: padding-left `--space-6` (24px). Bottom margin between entries: padding-bottom `--space-8` (32px).
- Tokens consumed: `--fs-meta`, `--fs-h3`, `--fs-body`, `--font-sans`, `--fg`, `--fg-muted`, `--accent`, `--hairline`, `--hairline-w`, `--lh-body`, `--lh-meta`, `--space-1`, `--space-2`, `--space-6`, `--space-8`, `--tracking-micro`. Zero new tokens.
- A11y: outer `<li>` inside consumer `<ol>`. `<time datetime>` for machine-readable date. `<h3>` (default) matches heading hierarchy. Pseudo-elements are decorative ŌĆö no content, no ARIA.
- Do NOT use for status feeds, activity streams, or real-time logs ŌĆö static editorial primitive only.
- Do NOT use for navigation menus or step indicators ŌĆö use Stepper for interactive flows.
- Do NOT place more than one sentence in the date slot ŌĆö it is a label, not a paragraph.

### LinkList
- Canonical vertical list of styled anchor rows. Use for footer columns, sidebar nav sections, and on-this-page TOC panels. A molecule because it pairs the `Link` atom with an optional `Heading` and a semantic `<ul>` / `<li>` structure that a bare Link cannot provide.
- Compound component: `<LinkList>` (root) + `<LinkList.Item>` (each row).
- Root renders a `<div>` with an optional `Heading` atom and a `<ul>`. `forwardRef<HTMLDivElement, LinkListProps>`. `displayName = "LinkList"`.
- `heading` (optional string | ReactNode) ŌĆö column heading via `Heading` atom at `headingLevel` (default h3).
- `headingLevel` (optional 2 | 3 | 4, default 3) ŌĆö semantic heading element level.
- `size` ("sm" | "md", default "sm") ŌĆö `"sm"`: `--fs-meta` (14px) for footer/sidebar; `"md"`: `--fs-body` for TOC/reading-flow contexts.
- `divider` (boolean, default false) ŌĆö renders `--hairline-w` solid `--hairline` rule below the heading. Requires `heading` to be visible.
- `LinkList.Item` props: `href` (required), `external?` (adds `target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"` + visually-hidden "(opens in new tab)"), `current?` (sets `aria-current="page"`, renders at full `--fg`), `icon?` (trailing ReactNode icon slot). `forwardRef<HTMLLIElement, LinkListItemProps>`.
- Link color: `--fg-muted` at rest ŌåÆ `--fg` on hover. Transition: `color var(--dur-fast) var(--easing)`. No underline (`variant="muted-link"`). Current item stays at `--fg` at rest.
- Focus-visible: `2px solid var(--accent)`, `4px` offset, `var(--radius-1)` border-radius ŌĆö matches global `a:focus-visible`.
- Tokens consumed: `--font-sans`, `--fs-meta`, `--fs-body`, `--fg-muted`, `--fg`, `--accent`, `--hairline`, `--hairline-w`, `--space-1`, `--space-2`, `--space-3`, `--lh-meta`, `--lh-body`, `--dur-fast`, `--easing`, `--radius-1`.
- A11y: semantic `<ul>` / `<li>` exposes item count. `aria-current="page"` on active item (WCAG 2.4.8). External items include visually-hidden "(opens in new tab)" ŌĆö no icon-only signal. Wrap in `<nav aria-label="ŌĆ”">` at the consumer level for navigation regions; the component does not add `<nav>` automatically.
- Do NOT use LinkList for command menus, action buttons, tag clouds, or top-level primary navigation. Those are DropdownMenu, Button, Tag, and NavLink/Header respectively.
- Do NOT add onClick-only handlers ŌĆö items are `<a>` anchors, not `<button>` elements.

### SiteShell
- Do NOT nest SiteShell inside another SiteShell. It is min-height: 100dvh.
- Emits plain <a href> tags ŌĆö framework-router consumers intercept at app level.
- navLabel (default: "Primary") is the aria-label for the nav element.

### TimelineItem
- Single entry in a sequential chronological list. Renders a date, title, and optional body copy with a left-rail marker dot and optional connector line to the next item.
- Must live inside a `<ol>` supplied by a parent Timeline organism. Do NOT use standalone.
- Composes Time atom (semantic `<time dateTime>`), Heading atom (title slot), and Text atom (body slot). Marker dot and connector rendered via CSS `::before` / `::after` pseudo-elements ŌĆö no extra DOM nodes.
- date (required string) ŌĆö ISO 8601 string; forwarded to Time dateTime. Displayed as-is in the date slot (uppercase micro register).
- title (required ReactNode) ŌĆö entry heading. Rendered via Heading atom at the level specified by headingLevel.
- body (optional ReactNode) ŌĆö supporting copy. Omitted when absent; some milestone entries are title-only.
- connector (boolean, default true) ŌĆö whether to render the downward connector stub. Pass false for the last item in the list.
- headingLevel (HeadingLevel, default "h3") ŌĆö semantic heading level for the title. Decoupled from visual size so the document outline can differ from the visual rank.
- Root: `<li>`, forwardRef<HTMLLIElement, TimelineItemProps>. ...rest spread to root. displayName = "TimelineItem".
- Tokens consumed: `--fs-meta`, `--fs-h3`, `--fs-body`, `--font-sans`, `--fg`, `--fg-muted`, `--accent`, `--hairline`, `--hairline-w`, `--lh-body`, `--lh-meta`, `--space-1`, `--space-2`, `--space-6`, `--space-8`, `--tracking-micro`. Zero new tokens.
- A11y: outer `<li>` inside consumer `<ol>`. `<time datetime>` for machine-readable date. Pseudo-elements are decorative ŌĆö no content, no ARIA.
- Do NOT use for navigation menus or step indicators ŌĆö use Stepper for interactive flows.
- Import: `import { TimelineItem } from "@poukai-inc/ui"` or `import { TimelineItem } from "@poukai-inc/ui/molecules/TimelineItem"`.

### TimelineSection
- Organism layer. Section-framed vertical list of `TimelineItem` molecules. Owns the Section header block and the `<ol>` track for changelog, company-milestone, and process-explainer surfaces.
- Composes `Section` molecule (eyebrow, heading, lede, block padding) around an `<ol>` track. Each `TimelineItem` is a direct child of the `<ol>`; TimelineItem owns marker dot and connector styling.
- Props: `eyebrow` (string | ReactNode), `heading` (ReactNode), `headingAs` ("h1" | "h2" | "h3", default "h2"), `lede` (ReactNode), `size` ("default" | "tight", default "default"), `children` (required ReactNode ŌĆö one or more TimelineItem), `reversed` (boolean, default false).
- `reversed` uses HTML `<ol reversed>` ŌĆö displays list newest-first without changing source order. Screen readers receive correct reversed numbering.
- `size` delegates to Section: "default" = `--space-16` block padding; "tight" = `--space-12`.
- Track `<ol>`: `list-style: none`, `display: flex`, `flex-direction: column`, `gap: var(--space-8)`.
- A11y: `<section>` root is a region landmark via Section's `aria-labelledby` wired to heading id. `<ol>` communicates sequence to AT. Rail pseudo-elements on TimelineItem are decorative and AT-invisible. `<ol reversed>` correctly signals reversed numbering.
- Static organism ŌĆö no interactive states. Tab order follows natural DOM sequence through any interactive children inside TimelineItem bodies.
- Tokens consumed: `--space-8` (item gap), `--space-12` / `--space-16` (block padding via Section). Marker/connector tokens delegated to TimelineItem.
- Do NOT use for a single item ŌĆö use TimelineItem inside a plain Section. Do NOT use for horizontal step flows ŌĆö use StepsSection/Stepper. Do NOT put navigational links in the rail.
- Import: `import { TimelineSection } from "@poukai-inc/ui"` or `import { TimelineSection } from "@poukai-inc/ui/organisms/TimelineSection"`.
- Usage: `<TimelineSection eyebrow="History" heading="Our story"><TimelineItem date="2024-01" title="Founded" connector /><TimelineItem date="2025-03" title="Launched" connector={false} /></TimelineSection>`

### Footer
- Canonical site-footer content block. Composes copyright + email (via `<EmailLink variant="muted">`) + optional secondary link row.
- Does NOT own the hairline rule or outer padding when slotted into SiteShell ŌĆö SiteShell's `.footer` CSS provides those. When used standalone (`as="footer"`), Footer adds `border-top: var(--hairline-w) solid var(--hairline)` and `padding: var(--space-8) var(--page-pad)` via the `[data-standalone="true"]` selector.
- `as` prop (`"div"` | `"footer"`, default `"div"`) ŌĆö controls the root element. Default `"div"` is correct when Footer is the child of SiteShell's `footer` slot (SiteShell already wraps in `<footer>` ŌĆö nesting two `<footer>` elements degrades the inner one to a generic container per ARIA spec). Pass `as="footer"` for standalone surfaces without SiteShell.
- `copyright` (required string) ŌĆö full copyright string including `┬®` and year. e.g. `"┬® Pouk AI INC 2026"`. DS never auto-inserts `new Date().getFullYear()`. A dev-mode `console.error` fires on empty string.
- `email` (required string) ŌĆö email address passed to `<EmailLink variant="muted">`. Footer owns no `mailto:` logic.
- `emailLabel` (optional string) ŌĆö visible label override for EmailLink. When omitted, defaults to the raw email address as visible text (e.g. `hello@pouk.ai`). Pass `"Contact"` for space-constrained contexts.
- `links` (optional `FooterLink[]`) ŌĆö secondary nav links. Each item: `{ href: string; label: string; external?: boolean }`. When `external: true`, link gets `target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"`. When absent or empty array, no `<nav>` is emitted.
- `linksLabel` (optional string, default `"Footer"`) ŌĆö `aria-label` for the `<nav>`. Required for SR landmark disambiguation when multiple `<nav>` elements exist on the page (header nav + footer nav).
- Layout: flex row (`justify-content: space-between`) at Ōēź 768px. Below 768px: flex column, left-aligned, `--space-4` (16px) gap between rows.
- Left group: copyright `<span>` + `┬Ę` separator (`aria-hidden="true"`) + `<EmailLink variant="muted">`. Gap between items: `--space-2` (8px).
- Right group (optional): `<nav aria-label>` wrapping `<ul>` of `<li><a class="muted-link">` items. Gap between links: `--space-2` (8px). Link color: `--fg-muted` at rest, `--fg` on hover (via `.muted-link` global from `tokens.css`).
- Tokens consumed: `--fg-muted`, `--fg`, `--font-sans`, `--fs-meta`, `--lh-meta`, `--space-2`, `--space-4`, `--space-8`, `--page-pad`, `--hairline-w`, `--hairline`, `--dur-fast`, `--accent`, `--radius-1`.
- No new tokens introduced. No dark-mode per-component overrides needed ŌĆö tokens flip cleanly via the global block in `tokens.css`.
- `className` pass-through available via `React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLElement>` spread. Footer has no margin of its own.
- Do NOT nest Footer inside another Footer.
- Do NOT use `as="footer"` inside SiteShell ŌĆö this produces nested `<footer>` landmarks, which is semantically invalid. Use the default `as="div"`.
- Do NOT add a router abstraction. Footer emits plain `<a href>`.
- Do NOT pass `external: true` without providing a meaningful label that communicates external destination (e.g. `"GitHub ŌåŚ"`).
- Do NOT use Footer for multi-column layout (Products / Company / Legal grid) ŌĆö that is a separate organism pattern.
- Do NOT add the wordmark inside Footer ŌĆö use a custom composition outside the DS if needed.
- Accessible name: two `<nav>` on page (header + footer) must each have distinct `aria-label`. Default `"Footer"` satisfies this; override via `linksLabel` if convention differs.

```tsx
// Inside SiteShell (canonical):
<SiteShell
  routes={[{ href: "/why-ai", label: "Why AI" }]}
  footer={
    <Footer
      copyright="┬® Pouk AI INC 2026"
      email="hello@pouk.ai"
      links={[
        { href: "/privacy", label: "Privacy" },
        { href: "/terms", label: "Terms" },
        { href: "https://github.com/poukai-inc", label: "GitHub ŌåŚ", external: true },
      ]}
    />
  }
>
  {children}
</SiteShell>

// Standalone:
<Footer
  as="footer"
  copyright="┬® Pouk AI INC 2026"
  email="hello@pouk.ai"
  links={[{ href: "/privacy", label: "Privacy" }]}
/>
```

### Dialog

Modal overlay primitive. Always modal (`modal: true`). Wraps `@radix-ui/react-dialog` ŌĆö all focus-trap, ARIA roles, and keyboard handling are delegated to Radix. The DS adds brand styling only.

**Compound subcomponents**

| Subcomponent        | Role                                                                                              |
| ------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `Dialog.Root`       | State controller. Props: `open`, `defaultOpen`, `onOpenChange`, `modal` (always true), `children`. |
| `Dialog.Trigger`    | Element that opens the dialog. Use `asChild` to pass a `<Button>`.                               |
| `Dialog.Portal`     | Renders overlay + content outside the DOM tree (into `document.body` by default). See SSR note below. |
| `Dialog.Overlay`    | Backdrop scrim. DS-styled: `rgb(0 0 0 / 0.4)`, animates on `data-state`. Pass `forceMount` for CSS-only exit animations. |
| `Dialog.Content`    | Modal panel. Primary styled surface. Props: `forceMount`, `onOpenAutoFocus`, `onCloseAutoFocus`, `onEscapeKeyDown`, `onPointerDownOutside`, `onInteractOutside`, `className`. |
| `Dialog.Title`      | Required accessible title. Radix warns if absent. Styled at `1.125rem / weight 500` by default. |
| `Dialog.Description`| Optional accessible description. Styled at `--fs-meta` / `--fg-muted`.                          |
| `Dialog.Close`      | Button that closes the dialog. Use `asChild` to render as `<Button>` or icon button.             |

**`DialogBasic` convenience wrapper**

Covers the 90% case: single-panel dialog with title, optional description, body, and built-in X close button.

| Prop           | Type                        | Default   | Description                                                             |
| -------------- | --------------------------- | --------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `open`         | `boolean?`                  | ŌĆö         | Controlled open state. Pair with `onOpenChange`.                        |
| `onOpenChange` | `(open: boolean) => void?`  | ŌĆö         | Controlled open state handler.                                          |
| `title`        | `string`                    | required  | Dialog title. Required. Renders as `Dialog.Title`.                      |
| `description`  | `string?`                   | ŌĆö         | Optional description. When present, adds a hairline rule below title.   |
| `children`     | `ReactNode`                 | required  | Body content.                                                           |
| `footer`       | `ReactNode?`                | ŌĆö         | Optional right-aligned footer (typically Cancel + Confirm buttons).     |
| `closeLabel`   | `string?`                   | `"Close"` | Accessible label for the built-in X close button. Override for locale.  |
| `className`    | `string?`                   | ŌĆö         | Forwarded to `Dialog.Content`. Standard layout-override escape hatch.   |

**Tokens consumed**

| Token          | Role                                               |
| -------------- | -------------------------------------------------- |
| `--bg-elevated`| Content panel background (front-most elevation)    |
| `--fg`         | Title and body text                                |
