| `--fg-muted`   | Description text and close button icon             |
| `--hairline`   | Panel border + title/body divider rule             |
| `--hairline-w` | Hairline stroke weight (1px)                       |
| `--accent`     | Focus ring on close button and interactive elements|
| `--radius-3`   | Content panel corner radius (8px)                  |
| `--radius-1`   | Focus ring border-radius (2px)                     |
| `--space-2`    | Description margin-top                             |
| `--space-3`    | Footer button gap                                  |
| `--space-4`    | Body padding-top; divider margin-top               |
| `--space-6`    | Panel horizontal padding; footer margin-top        |
| `--space-8`    | Panel top padding                                  |
| `--font-sans`  | All text in the dialog                             |
| `--fs-body`    | Body content text                                  |
| `--fs-meta`    | Description text                                   |
| `--dur-fast`   | Open/close transition duration (180ms)             |
| `--easing`     | Entrance easing (expo-out)                         |
| `--page-pad`   | Panel width calculation (subtracted from 100vw)    |
| `--surface`    | Close button hover background                      |

**SSR / portal container note.** By default Radix portals content into `document.body`. Consumers using Next.js App Router or SSR strict mode who need to target a specific DOM node (e.g. `<div id="portal-root">` outside `<main>`) can pass `container` via `Dialog.Portal` props: `<Dialog.Portal container={portalRootRef.current}>`. No code-level handling is required in the DS ŌĆö the prop passes through to Radix directly.

**JSX usage examples**

```jsx
// (a) Controlled open state ŌĆö DialogBasic
import { useState } from "react";
import { DialogBasic, Button } from "@poukai-inc/ui";

function WaitlistSignup() {
  const [open, setOpen] = useState(false);
  return (
    <>
      <Button onClick={() => setOpen(true)}>Join waitlist</Button>
      <DialogBasic
        open={open}
        onOpenChange={setOpen}
        title="Join the waitlist"
        description="We'll reach out as soon as your spot is ready."
        footer={
          <>
            <Button variant="ghost" onClick={() => setOpen(false)}>Cancel</Button>
            <Button variant="primary" type="submit" form="waitlist-form">Submit</Button>
          </>
        }
      >
        <form id="waitlist-form">
          <label htmlFor="email">Email address</label>
          <input id="email" type="email" name="email" autoComplete="email" />
        </form>
      </DialogBasic>
    </>
  );
}

// (b) Uncontrolled via Dialog.Trigger ŌĆö compound API
import { Dialog, Button } from "@poukai-inc/ui";

function InfoOverlay() {
  return (
    <Dialog.Root>
      <Dialog.Trigger asChild>
        <Button variant="ghost">Learn more</Button>
      </Dialog.Trigger>
      <Dialog.Portal>
        <Dialog.Overlay />
        <Dialog.Content>
          <Dialog.Title>About pouk.ai</Dialog.Title>
          <Dialog.Description>
            pouk.ai gives operators access to enterprise-grade AI tooling.
          </Dialog.Description>
          <p>Founded in 2025, pouk.ai builds the infrastructure layer for AI operators.</p>
          <Dialog.Close asChild>
            <Button variant="ghost">Close</Button>
          </Dialog.Close>
        </Dialog.Content>
      </Dialog.Portal>
    </Dialog.Root>
  );
}

// (c) Form inside Dialog ŌĆö onOpenAutoFocus override
import { useRef } from "react";
import { Dialog, Button } from "@poukai-inc/ui";

function SettingsDialog({ open, onOpenChange }) {
  const firstFieldRef = useRef(null);
  return (
    <Dialog.Root open={open} onOpenChange={onOpenChange}>
      <Dialog.Portal>
        <Dialog.Overlay />
        <Dialog.Content
          onOpenAutoFocus={(e) => {
            e.preventDefault();
            firstFieldRef.current?.focus();
          }}
        >
          <Dialog.Title>Notification settings</Dialog.Title>
          <form>
            <input ref={firstFieldRef} id="notify-email" type="checkbox" name="notify-email" />
          </form>
          <div style={{ display: "flex", justifyContent: "flex-end", gap: "var(--space-3)", marginTop: "var(--space-6)" }}>
            <Dialog.Close asChild><Button variant="ghost">Cancel</Button></Dialog.Close>
            <Button variant="primary" type="submit">Save</Button>
          </div>
        </Dialog.Content>
      </Dialog.Portal>
    </Dialog.Root>
  );
}

// (d) Confirmation dialog ŌĆö Cancel + Confirm pattern
// Note: variant="primary" is used for the destructive action until variant="danger" ships.
import { DialogBasic, Button } from "@poukai-inc/ui";

function DeleteConfirmation({ open, onOpenChange, onConfirm }) {
  return (
    <DialogBasic
      open={open}
      onOpenChange={onOpenChange}
      title="Delete this project?"
      description="This action cannot be undone. All data will be permanently removed."
      footer={
        <>
          <Button variant="ghost" onClick={() => onOpenChange(false)}>Cancel</Button>
          <Button variant="primary" onClick={() => { onConfirm(); onOpenChange(false); }}>
            Delete project
          </Button>
        </>
      }
    >
      {null}
    </DialogBasic>
  );
}
```

- Dialog is always modal. Non-blocking overlays ŌåÆ future `Popover` primitive.
- `Dialog.Title` is required ŌĆö Radix warns if absent. Use `position: absolute; clip: rect(0,0,0,0)` visually-hidden styles for untitled designs; do NOT omit the element.
- Do NOT set `modal={false}` ŌĆö excluded from the DS. Use the raw Radix primitive if absolutely required.
- Do NOT add `--scrim` or `--shadow-dialog` tokens. The scrim value (`rgb(0 0 0 / 0.4)`) is intentionally inline (not a theme variable). No drop shadow ŌĆö `--bg-elevated` on `--bg` reads as elevated via background value difference.
- Do NOT nest a Dialog inside another Dialog.
- Do NOT add `role="alertdialog"` unless the action is truly irreversible with no non-destructive dismiss path ŌĆö a future `AlertDialog` variant will handle that register.
- `className` on `Dialog.Content` and `DialogBasic` is the sanctioned escape hatch for layout overrides (e.g. wider panels). No "wide" size variant ships in v1.

### Tabs

Tabbed interface primitive. Wraps `@radix-ui/react-tabs` ŌĆö all ARIA roles (`tablist`, `tab`, `tabpanel`), keyboard navigation (arrow keys, Home/End), focus management, and `aria-selected` / `aria-controls` / `aria-labelledby` wiring are delegated to Radix. The DS adds brand styling only.

**Compound subcomponents**

| Subcomponent    | Role                                                                                                           |
| --------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `Tabs.Root`     | State controller. Props: `value`, `defaultValue`, `onValueChange`, `orientation` (`"horizontal"` \| `"vertical"`, default `"horizontal"`), `className`, `children`. |
| `Tabs.List`     | Wrapper for triggers. Sets `role="tablist"`. DS-styled: hairline border-bottom (horizontal) or border-right (vertical), flex, overflow-x scroll. |
| `Tabs.Trigger`  | Individual tab button. Required `value` prop. DS-styled: `--fg-muted` at rest, `--fg` on hover/active, inset accent `box-shadow` on active. |
| `Tabs.Content`  | Content panel for a given tab. Required `value` prop. `padding-top: var(--space-6)` (horizontal) or `padding-left: var(--space-6)` (vertical). |

**`TabsBasic` convenience wrapper**

Data-driven API for the 90% case.

| Prop            | Type                              | Default              | Description                                                              |
| --------------- | --------------------------------- | -------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `tabs`          | `TabItem[]`                       | required             | Array of `{ value, label, content }` objects.                            |
| `defaultValue`  | `string?`                         | `tabs[0].value`      | Uncontrolled initial active tab.                                         |
| `value`         | `string?`                         | ŌĆö                    | Controlled active tab value. Pair with `onValueChange`.                  |
| `onValueChange` | `(v: string) => void?`            | ŌĆö                    | Controlled mode handler.                                                 |
| `orientation`   | `"horizontal" \| "vertical"`      | `"horizontal"`       | Tab layout orientation.                                                  |
| `className`     | `string?`                         | ŌĆö                    | Forwarded to `Tabs.Root`. Standard layout-override escape hatch.          |

**Tokens consumed**

| Token          | Role                                                                |
| -------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `--hairline`   | List border-bottom (horizontal) or border-right (vertical)          |
| `--hairline-w` | Hairline stroke weight (1px)                                        |
| `--space-1`    | Trigger horizontal padding                                          |
| `--space-2`    | Trigger vertical padding                                            |
| `--space-4`    | Gap between triggers in list                                        |
| `--space-6`    | Content padding-top (horizontal) or padding-left (vertical)         |
| `--fs-meta`    | Trigger font size (14px)                                            |
| `--lh-meta`    | Trigger line-height (1.2)                                           |
| `--font-sans`  | Trigger font family (Geist)                                         |
| `--fg`         | Trigger color on hover and active state                             |
| `--fg-muted`   | Trigger color at rest                                               |
| `--accent`     | Active trigger inset box-shadow; focus ring                         |
| `--radius-1`   | Trigger and content focus ring border-radius (2px)                  |
| `--dur-fast`   | Trigger color and box-shadow transition duration (180ms)             |
| `--easing`     | Trigger transition easing (expo-out)                                |

**JSX usage examples**

```jsx
// (a) Controlled TabsBasic with two tabs
import { useState } from "react";
import { TabsBasic } from "@poukai-inc/ui";

function ServiceSelector() {
  const [tab, setTab] = useState("build");
  return (
    <TabsBasic
      value={tab}
      onValueChange={setTab}
      tabs={[
        { value: "build", label: "Build", content: <p>Custom AI builds ŌĆö end-to-end production systems.</p> },
        { value: "automate", label: "Automate", content: <p>Workflow automation ŌĆö closed-loop inference pipelines.</p> },
      ]}
    />
  );
}

// (b) Compound API with three tabs
import { Tabs } from "@poukai-inc/ui";

function WorkPage() {
  return (
    <Tabs.Root defaultValue="principles">
      <Tabs.List>
        <Tabs.Trigger value="principles">Principles</Tabs.Trigger>
        <Tabs.Trigger value="failure-modes">Failure Modes</Tabs.Trigger>
        <Tabs.Trigger value="team">Team</Tabs.Trigger>
      </Tabs.List>
      <Tabs.Content value="principles">
        <h3>Ship the smallest real thing.</h3>
        <p>The only metric that matters is a production feedback loop closing.</p>
      </Tabs.Content>
      <Tabs.Content value="failure-modes">
        <h3>The chatbot-on-top-of-RAG plateau.</h3>
        <p>Most teams stop here. The demo dazzles; the production loop never closes.</p>
      </Tabs.Content>
      <Tabs.Content value="team">
        <h3>Senior-only.</h3>
        <p>No juniors, no coordinators. Every person ships to production.</p>
      </Tabs.Content>
    </Tabs.Root>
  );
}

// (c) Vertical orientation via compound API
import { Tabs } from "@poukai-inc/ui";

function SettingsPanel() {
  return (
    <Tabs.Root defaultValue="general" orientation="vertical">
      <Tabs.List>
        <Tabs.Trigger value="general">General</Tabs.Trigger>
        <Tabs.Trigger value="notifications">Notifications</Tabs.Trigger>
        <Tabs.Trigger value="billing">Billing</Tabs.Trigger>
      </Tabs.List>
      <Tabs.Content value="general"><p>General settings panel.</p></Tabs.Content>
      <Tabs.Content value="notifications"><p>Notification preferences panel.</p></Tabs.Content>
      <Tabs.Content value="billing"><p>Billing panel.</p></Tabs.Content>
    </Tabs.Root>
  );
}
```

- Do NOT roll your own keyboard navigation ŌĆö Radix handles arrow keys, Home, End, and focus management. Custom `onKeyDown` on triggers will break native behavior.
- Do NOT add a router abstraction ŌĆö consumers wire `value` / `onValueChange` to URL state themselves.
- Do NOT add lazy-load / suspense for `Tabs.Content` at the DS level ŌĆö wrap content in `<Suspense>` at the consumer side if needed.
- `Tabs.Trigger` value must exactly match its `Tabs.Content` value ŌĆö Radix uses these to wire `aria-controls` and `aria-labelledby`.
- Do NOT nest `Tabs.Root` inside another `Tabs.Root` ŌĆö conflicting ARIA relationships.
- Horizontal `Tabs.List` scrolls on overflow (`overflow-x: auto`, hidden scrollbar). No "More" overflow menu in v1.
- Active tab indicator is an inset `box-shadow` (not a `border`) to avoid layout shift. Horizontal: `inset 0 -2px 0 var(--accent)`. Vertical: `inset -2px 0 0 var(--accent)`.

### Form

Structured submission container ŌĆö `<form>` wrapper that composes `Field` + `Input` + `Textarea` + `Button` without owning a validation library.

- Root element: `<form>`. Not polymorphic.
- Props: `onSubmit: (data: FormData) => void | Promise<void>` (required); standard `<form>` attributes (`action`, `method`, `name`, etc.) spread via `Omit<ComponentPropsWithoutRef<"form">, "onSubmit">`.
- Behavior: prevents default browser submit, collects `new FormData(event.currentTarget)`, passes it to consumer's `onSubmit`. Handler exceptions propagate ŌĆö Form does not swallow them.
- Layout: `display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: var(--space-4); max-width: var(--hero-max);` (matches prose column).
- `forwardRef<HTMLFormElement, FormProps>`, `displayName = "Form"`, `clsx` className merge.
- A11y: native `<form>` semantics. No additional ARIA.
- Tokens consumed: `--space-4`, `--hero-max`, `--font-sans`. No new tokens.
- Validation: consumer responsibility ŌĆö pair with Zod, React Hook Form, or hand-rolled. Use `useFieldErrors` (shipped v1.1.0) for field-keyed error state management.
- Do NOT add a `<fieldset>`/`<legend>` slot ŌĆö consumer adds those via children if needed.

```tsx
<Form onSubmit={(data) => sendEmail({ email: data.get("email"), message: data.get("message") })}>
  <Field label="Email" id="email">
    <Input type="email" name="email" placeholder="you@example.com" required />
  </Field>
  <Field label="Message" id="message">
    <Textarea name="message" rows={4} required />
  </Field>
  <Button variant="primary" type="submit">Send</Button>
</Form>
```

### useFieldErrors

Validation-lib-agnostic error-state hook for Form surfaces. Shipped in v1.1.0. Co-located with `<Field>` at `src/molecules/Field/useFieldErrors.ts`.

```ts
import { useFieldErrors, type FieldErrors } from "@poukai-inc/ui";

const { errors, setErrors, setFieldError, clearAll } = useFieldErrors(initial?);
```

- `errors: FieldErrors` ŌĆö `Record<string, string | undefined>`. Keys are input `name` attributes; values are user-facing error strings (or `undefined` to clear).
- `setErrors(next)` ŌĆö replace the entire error map. Use after full-form validation runs.
- `setFieldError(name, message | undefined)` ŌĆö set or clear the error for a single field; preserves all other fields.
- `clearAll()` ŌĆö clear all errors. Call on successful submit or after server acknowledgement.
- All setters are stable across re-renders (`useCallback`). Pure state ŌĆö no tokens, no DOM, invisible to AT.
- Compatible with Zod (`flatten().fieldErrors`), React Hook Form (after one transform), Yup, and hand-rolled validators.
- Do NOT add a validation runner, schema integration, or async handling to the hook. Validation logic lives at the call site.

```tsx
// Hand-rolled validator
function ContactForm() {
  const { errors, setErrors, clearAll } = useFieldErrors();
  return (
    <Form onSubmit={(data) => {
      const email = String(data.get("email") ?? "");
      const next: FieldErrors = {};
      if (!email.includes("@")) next.email = "Enter a valid email";
      if (Object.keys(next).length === 0) { clearAll(); return submit(data); }
      setErrors(next);
    }}>
      <Field label="Email" id="email" error={errors.email}>
        <Input type="email" name="email" />
      </Field>
      <Button type="submit">Send</Button>
    </Form>
  );
}
```

### VisuallyHidden
- Canonical sr-only clip-pattern atom. Renders `children` in the accessibility tree while keeping the element invisible to sighted users. Root element is always `<span>`. Non-polymorphic.
- Use for supplementary screen-reader-only text: off-screen labels, "opens in new tab" notices, icon button labels that must not be visible.
- Implementation: `position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0,0,0,0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0`. No CSS token consumption ŌĆö geometry is fixed at the clip boundary.
- `children` (required ReactNode) ŌĆö the text or node to expose to the a11y tree.
- `forwardRef` to the root `<span>`. `...rest` spread. `displayName = "VisuallyHidden"`.
- Do NOT use `display: none` or `visibility: hidden` ŌĆö both suppress from the a11y tree entirely. `VisuallyHidden` is the only correct pattern for sr-only text in this DS.
- Do NOT substitute with `aria-label` when both a visual and a sr-only label are needed simultaneously ŌĆö use `VisuallyHidden` for the supplementary text.

### Skeleton
- Content-placeholder atom for async loading states. Renders a pulsing grey block in the shape of absent content. `currentColor`-independent ŌĆö uses `--surface` fill.
- `width` (required, `number | string`) and `height` (required, `number | string`) ŌĆö dimensions of the placeholder block. Pass numbers as px values; strings for percentages or CSS units.
- `radius` (`"none"` | `"sm"` | `"md"` | `"lg"` | `"circle"`, default `"md"`) ŌĆö border-radius variant. `"circle"` produces a pill/avatar placeholder; `"none"` for sharp-edge content like images.
- `as` (`"div"` | `"span"`, default `"div"`) ŌĆö root element. Use `"span"` for inline-in-text placeholders.
- Animation: opacity pulse at `--dur-pulse` (1800ms). Suppressed by the global `prefers-reduced-motion` block in `tokens.css` ŌĆö no per-component override needed.
- A11y: root has `aria-hidden="true"`. Wrap groups in a container with `aria-busy="true"` and `aria-label` describing what is loading.
- Tokens consumed: `--surface`, `--radius-1`, `--radius-2`, `--radius-3`, `--dur-pulse`. No new tokens.
- Do NOT use Skeleton for indeterminate process feedback ŌĆö use `<Spinner>`. Skeleton is for known-shape content placeholders only.
- Do NOT pass meaningful text children ŌĆö Skeleton is a visual shape, not a label.

### Icon
- Token-sized Lucide icon wrapper. Renders any Lucide icon at one of four named size rungs, inheriting `currentColor`.
- `icon` (required) ŌĆö a Lucide icon component (e.g. `import { Mail } from "lucide-react"`).
- `size` (`"xs"` | `"sm"` | `"md"` | `"lg"`, default `"md"`) ŌĆö maps to `--icon-xs` (12px), `--icon-sm` (16px), `--icon-md` (20px), `--icon-lg` (24px).
- `decorative` (boolean, default `true`) ŌĆö when `true`, sets `aria-hidden="true"` on the SVG (presentational). When `false`, the consumer must pass `aria-label` for the icon's semantic meaning.
- `forwardRef` to the root SVG. `...rest` spread. `displayName = "Icon"`.
- Tokens consumed: `--icon-xs`, `--icon-sm`, `--icon-md`, `--icon-lg`. Color is always `currentColor`.
- Do NOT hardcode icon pixel sizes ŌĆö always use the `size` prop and the icon size token scale.
- Do NOT pass `aria-hidden` directly when `decorative={false}` ŌĆö the `decorative` prop controls it. When `decorative={false}`, pass `aria-label` instead.

### Link
- Canonical styled anchor atom. Applies the brand two-layer underline affordance (resting `--hairline` underline; hover: `--accent` underline grows over it) and focus ring. Three variants.
- Root element: `<a>` by default. `asChild={true}` delegates rendering to a Radix `Slot` ŌĆö pass a framework `<Link>` component as the child for client-side routing.
- `variant` (`"default"` | `"quiet"` | `"muted"`, default `"default"`) ŌĆö color register:
  - `"default"` ŌĆö `color: var(--fg)`. Standard in-prose link.
  - `"quiet"` ŌĆö `color: var(--fg-muted)`. Use for secondary navigational links where reduced visual weight is appropriate.
  - `"muted"` ŌĆö applies the `.muted-link` global class pattern: `color: var(--fg-muted)` at rest, `color: var(--fg)` on hover via `transition`. No underline animation. Use for footer and caption-register links.
- `href` ŌĆö standard anchor attribute. Required when `asChild` is false.
- `forwardRef` to the root `<a>` (or Slot child). `...rest` spread. `displayName = "Link"`.
- Focus ring: `outline: 2px solid var(--accent); outline-offset: 4px; border-radius: var(--radius-1)` ŌĆö matches global `a:focus-visible` in `tokens.css`.
- Tokens consumed: `--fg`, `--fg-muted`, `--accent`, `--hairline`, `--dur-mid`, `--easing-link`, `--radius-1`. No new tokens.
- Do NOT use `<Link>` for `mailto:` ŌĆö use `<EmailLink>`. Do NOT use `<Link>` for button actions ŌĆö use `<Button>`.

### Divider
- Hairline separator rule atom. Horizontal rule (`<hr>`) or vertical rule (`<div>`) at `1px` using `--hairline` or `--hairline-soft`.
- `orientation` (`"horizontal"` | `"vertical"`, default `"horizontal"`) ŌĆö axis of the rule. Horizontal emits `<hr>`; vertical emits `<div role="separator" aria-orientation="vertical">`.
- `tone` (`"default"` | `"muted"`, default `"default"`) ŌĆö `"default"` uses `--hairline`; `"muted"` uses `--hairline-soft` (quieter decorative separator).
- Horizontal: `border: 0; border-top: var(--hairline-w) solid <tone-token>; margin: 0`. Vertical: `width: var(--hairline-w); background: <tone-token>; align-self: stretch`.
- `forwardRef` to the root element. `...rest` spread. `displayName = "Divider"`.
- Tokens consumed: `--hairline`, `--hairline-soft`, `--hairline-w`. No new tokens.
- Do NOT use `Divider` as a layout spacer ŌĆö it is a visual rule only. Use margin/gap tokens on the containing element for spacing.
- Do NOT render thicker than `--hairline-w` (1px) ŌĆö brand convention.

### Spinner
- Indeterminate loading indicator atom. An inline SVG arc that rotates continuously to signal an async operation in progress. Uses `currentColor` throughout ŌĆö adapts to any surface without a color prop.
- Root element: `<span>` with `role="status"`, `aria-live="polite"`, `aria-label`. The SVG inside is presentational (`aria-hidden="true"`). A visually-hidden `<span>` sibling contains the label text for virtual browse mode.
- `size` (`"sm"` | `"md"` | `"lg"`, default `"md"`) ŌĆö SVG dimensions: `sm`=16px (`--icon-sm`), `md`=20px (`--icon-md`), `lg`=24px (`--icon-lg`).
- `label` (optional string, default `"Loading"`) ŌĆö accessible label for `aria-label` and the visually-hidden text. Override with context-specific text: `"Submitting"`, `"Loading results"`.
- SVG geometry: `viewBox="0 0 24 24"`, `r=10`, `cx=cy=12`, `strokeWidth=2`. Track arc at `opacity: 0.2`; rotating arc at full opacity covers ~270┬░ (`stroke-dasharray Ōēł 47.12`).
- Animation: `animation: poukai-spin var(--dur-spinner) linear infinite` on the arc. `--dur-spinner: 800ms` ŌĆö sub-second so the spinner reads as actively working. Linear easing (not `--easing`) because a continuous loop must not decelerate.
- Reduced-motion fallback: explicit `animation: none` on the arc (belt-and-suspenders over the global `tokens.css` collapse) + a `ŌĆ”` (U+2026) ellipsis `<span aria-hidden="true">` revealed via CSS only under `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce`. No JS branching. DOM structure is identical across motion preferences.
- Tokens consumed: `--dur-spinner`, `--icon-sm`, `--icon-md`, `--icon-lg`. Color: `currentColor`. No color token consumed directly.
- Do NOT add a `color` prop ŌĆö `currentColor` inheritance is the contract. Control color on the parent.
- Do NOT add a `speed` or `duration` prop ŌĆö override `--dur-spinner` via a scoped CSS custom property on a parent element.
- Do NOT show `<Spinner>` for interactions that resolve in under ~300ms ŌĆö it will flash and disappear, reading as a glitch.
- Do NOT use `<Spinner>` as ambient decoration ŌĆö motion doctrine bans ambient motion without semantic meaning tied to a real operation.
- Button loading pairing: `sm`/`compact` buttons use `size="sm"`; `md`/`lg` buttons use `size="md"`. Never `size="lg"` inside a button ŌĆö 24px competes with the button label.

### ProgressBar
- Linear progress indicator atom. Determinate mode (known progress percentage) or indeterminate mode (unknown duration). Pairs with `<Spinner>` ŌĆö use `<Spinner>` when no percentage is available; use `<ProgressBar>` when a percentage or step-count can be derived.
- Root element: `<div role="progressbar">` with `aria-valuemin="0"`, `aria-valuemax="100"`. Determinate: `aria-valuenow={clamped value}`. Indeterminate: `aria-valuenow` is absent from the DOM entirely (not `undefined`, not empty ŌĆö the attribute is not rendered at all).
- Labeling: exactly one of `aria-label` or `aria-labelledby` is required. Enforced at the TypeScript type level via discriminated union ŌĆö both absent is a type error. No default label is defined; an unlabeled progress bar is inaccessible.
- `value` (`number | undefined`, default `undefined`) ŌĆö 0ŌĆō100. Omitting activates indeterminate mode. Out-of-range values clamp to `[0, 100]`.
- `size` (`"sm" | "md"`, default `"md"`) ŌĆö track height: `sm`=2px, `md`=4px. `border-radius: 999px` on track and fill at all sizes (pill shape, brand-fixed).
- `tone` (`"default" | "success" | "warning" | "danger"`, default `"default"`) ŌĆö fill color token: `default`ŌåÆ`--fg`, `success`ŌåÆ`--success`, `warning`ŌåÆ`--warning`, `danger`ŌåÆ`--danger`. Track is always `--surface` regardless of tone.
- Width: not set by the component. Consumer assigns width via `className` or containing layout. Same convention as `<Spinner>`.
- Determinate fill: `width: 100%`, `transform: scaleX(var(--progress-fraction, 0))`, `transform-origin: left center`. `--progress-fraction` set inline as a CSS custom property (`value / 100`). Transition: `transform var(--dur-mid) var(--easing)`. NEVER animate `width` ŌĆö `scaleX` only.
- Indeterminate: track contains two sibling bars (`position: absolute`, `width: 100%`) animated by `@keyframes poukai-progress-bar1` and `poukai-progress-bar2`. Both loop `var(--dur-progress-indeterminate)` (1400ms) with `--easing` (expo-out). Bar 2 starts at `translateX(-200%)` so it emerges behind bar 1 mid-cycle, producing the "two signals in sequence" sweep with no dead-track gap.
- Keyframes: `poukai-progress-bar1` (0%: -100%, 20%: -100%, 60%: 0%, 80%: 0%, 100%: +100%) and `poukai-progress-bar2` (0%: -200%, 40%: -200%, 80%: 0%, 100%: +100%). The brief 60%ŌĆō80% hold at 0% in bar1 creates a "full" pause before exit; the overlap prevents dead track.
- Reduced-motion ŌĆö indeterminate: `bar1` and `bar2` are `animation: none; display: none`. A `staticFill` element (`display: none` by default, `display: block` under reduced-motion) renders at `scaleX(0.5)` ŌĆö half-filled, communicates "in progress" without a specific value or motion. Always present in DOM; toggled CSS-only.
- Reduced-motion ŌĆö determinate: `transition: none` on the fill (snap to value immediately).
- New tokens added in this release: `--success` / `--bg-success` / `--fg-on-success` (light + dark), `--dur-progress-indeterminate: 1400ms`.
- Tokens consumed: `--surface` (track), `--fg` / `--success` / `--warning` / `--danger` (fill by tone), `--dur-mid` (determinate transition), `--easing` (both transition and animation), `--dur-progress-indeterminate` (indeterminate loop period).
- Do NOT animate `width`. The motion property contract is `transform` only. Playwright CT guards this regression.
- Do NOT add `aria-valuenow` when indeterminate ŌĆö the attribute must be absent, not set to `undefined` or `null`.
- Do NOT omit both `aria-label` and `aria-labelledby` ŌĆö TypeScript will reject the call site.
- Do NOT add a `speed` or `duration` prop ŌĆö override `--dur-progress-indeterminate` via a scoped CSS custom property on a parent element.
- Do NOT use `<ProgressBar>` inside a `<Button>` ŌĆö use `<Spinner>` for button-level loading.
- Do NOT use as ambient decoration ŌĆö every animation must communicate a real operation state.

---

## Anti-patterns

- **Do not override Wordmark colors via style= or CSS.** Set color on a parent instead.
- **Do not use --accent as a surface fill or body text color.** It is a signal color for interactivity and focus only.
- **Do not use --fg as a decorative background fill.** No dark-mode scheme is defined.
- **Do not hardcode hex values, raw pixel sizes, or magic numbers.** Every CSS value resolves to a var(--token) (ADR-0002).
- **Do not render Wordmark below height={40}.** Isotype detail collapses into illegibility.
- **Do not change the Wordmark aspect ratio.** Use only height; width is auto.
- **Do not use the stacked lockup as the default.** The horizontal lockup is canonical; stacked is for square-format surfaces only.
- **Do not nest Hero inside another Hero.** Invalid ARIA structure, duplicate h1, compounded max-width.
- **Do not use :global selectors in .module.css files.** ADR-0002 prohibits this.
- **Do not force-animate StatusBadge under prefers-reduced-motion.** The !important block in tokens.css handles it.
- **Do not import from sub-paths in tests.** Import from the component file directly.
- **Do not import from dist/ in source files.** Import from src/.
- **Do not make prop removals or renames without a major version bump.**
- **Do not ship a component without both a *.test.tsx and *.stories.tsx.**
- **Do not use `--bg-elevated` as the page background.** It is for overlays only. The page is always `--bg`.
- **Do not hardcode `#FFFFFF` for page background or content sections.** `#FFFFFF` is reserved for `--bg-elevated`. Use `--bg` (`#FBFBFD`) for the page.
- **Do not collapse the elevation rhythm.** `--surface`, `--surface-section`, `--bg`, and `--bg-elevated` are four distinct steps. Skipping or reversing them (e.g. using `--bg-elevated` where `--bg` belongs, or using `--surface` as a section divider) is a brand-level error. Use `--surface-section` for section bands; never substitute `--surface` for that role.
- **Do not use `variant="no-title"` without placing an `<h1>` in body content.** The molecule emits no heading element; the consumer is solely responsible. A page with `variant="no-title"` Hero and no `<h1>` anywhere is an accessibility violation (WCAG 2.4.6, axe-core `page-has-heading-one`).

---

## Brand voice

Precise, direct, technically confident. Never hedges, inflates, or reassures. No startup boilerplate ("unlock," "leverage," "empower," "transform").

- Eyebrows: "Role 01", "Role 02" (structural labels, not prose)
- StatusBadge: "Taking conversations for Q3." / "Not taking new work at this time." (availability facts, no exclamation marks)
- Button labels: sentence-case, max 4 words, specific verbs. "Get in touch", "Learn more", not "Click here" or "LET'S CONNECT!"
- Error states (consumer responsibility): declarative, specific, not apologetic. "Email address is required." not "Oops! Something went wrong."

### Toast
- Imperative notification organism. Wraps `@radix-ui/react-toast` ŌĆö all ARIA, swipe-to-dismiss, focus, and prefers-reduced-motion are delegated to Radix.
- Architecture: `<ToastProvider>` wraps the app root once. `useToast()` returns `{ show, dismiss }` for imperative use anywhere in the tree.
- `ToastPayload`: `{ tone?, title?, body, duration?, action? }`. `body` is the only required field.
- Four tones: `"info"` (default), `"success"`, `"warning"`, `"danger"`. Tone determines background, left-rule color, and text color ŌĆö identical palette contract to `<Banner>`.
  - `info`: `--bg-elevated` bg, hairline border all sides, `--fg` text.
  - `success`: `--bg-elevated` bg, 2px `--accent` left-rule, `--fg` text.
  - `warning`: `--bg-warning` bg, 2px `--warning` left-rule, `--fg-on-warning` text.
  - `danger`: `--bg-danger` bg, 2px `--danger` left-rule, `--fg-on-danger` text.
- `ToastProvider` props: `defaultDuration` (default 5000ms), `max` (default 4 simultaneous), `position` (`"bottom-right"` default, `"top-right"`, `"top-center"`, `"bottom-center"`), `closeLabel` (default `"Close"`).
- `position` drives the viewport's CSS class. Viewport is `position: fixed; z-index: 200` ŌĆö above Dialog overlay (100) and content (101).
- `max` enforces a queue cap: when exceeded, the oldest toast is dropped immediately.
- Per-toast `duration` overrides `defaultDuration`. Radix fires `onOpenChange(false)` when duration expires, which triggers `dismiss(id)`.
- `action`: `{ label: string; onClick: () => void }`. Rendered via Radix `<Toast.Action altText={label}>` wrapping `<Button variant="ghost" size="sm">`. `altText` is required by Radix for SR accessibility.
- Close button: always rendered. `aria-label` = `closeLabel` prop (default `"Close"`). Uses Lucide `X` at 14px.
- ARIA: Radix maps `type="foreground"` ŌåÆ `aria-live="assertive"` (warning/danger) and `type="background"` ŌåÆ `aria-live="polite"` (info/success).
- Width: `min(420px, calc(100vw - 2 * var(--page-pad)))`. Padding: `--space-3` block, `--space-4` inline. Radius: `--radius-2`. Shadow: `0 4px 12px rgb(0 0 0 / 0.08)` (inline ŌĆö no shadow token in DS vocabulary; same rationale as Dialog).
- Entrance: slide from right. Exit: fade out. Both at `--dur-fast` (180ms). Reduced motion: handled by the global `tokens.css` block ŌĆö no per-component override.
- Toast is NOT `<Banner>` (Banner is persistent, inline, document-flow). Toast is NOT `<Dialog>` (Dialog is modal and blocking). Use Toast for time-limited, non-blocking feedback only.
- Do NOT add `<ToastProvider>` to `<SiteShell>` ŌĆö that is the consuming app's concern.
- Do NOT use `useToast()` outside a `<ToastProvider>` ŌĆö it throws.
- Do NOT show more than `max` simultaneous toasts. The oldest is dropped automatically.
- Do NOT use Toast for destructive confirmations ŌĆö use `<Dialog>`.

### RoleGrid
- Section-framed organism. Arranges `RoleCard` molecules in a responsive CSS grid. Canonical usage: "who this is for" / "teams we serve" marketing pattern.
- `heading` (required string) ŌĆö Section h2. Names the audience group ("Who it's for").
- `eyebrow` (optional string) ŌĆö passed through to Section eyebrow slot.
- `surface` (`"default"` | `"section"`, default `"default"`) ŌĆö `"section"` applies `--surface-section` band background.
- `columns` (`2 | 3 | 4`, default `3`) ŌĆö max column count at `--bp-md` (768px) and above. Always 1 column below `--bp-md`.
- `children` (required `ReactNode`) ŌĆö `RoleCard` instances. Grid gap: `--space-6` mobile, `--space-8` at `--bp-md`+.
- Outer `<section>` + `aria-labelledby` from Section. Grid container is a plain `<div>` ŌĆö no ARIA role.
- `forwardRef` to the Section root (`HTMLElement`). `className` and `data-*` spread to root.
- Do NOT use RoleGrid for selectable options, team bios (use TeamGrid), or as the page's h1 source.
- Do NOT nest RoleGrid inside another Section ŌĆö double landmark nesting.
- Do NOT force non-RoleCard children into the grid ŌĆö sizing is calibrated for RoleCard proportions.


### Table
- Semantic data-display primitive. Composable sub-components: `Table.Head` (`<thead>`), `Table.Body` (`<tbody>`), `Table.Row` (`<tr>`), `Table.HeaderCell` (`<th scope="col">`), `Table.Cell` (`<td>`).
- Root element: `<table>`. `forwardRef<HTMLTableElement, TableProps>`. `...rest` spread on root. `border-collapse: collapse; width: 100%`.
- `density` (optional `"compact" | "comfortable"`, default `"comfortable"`) ŌĆö vertical cell padding. `"compact"` ŌåÆ `--space-2`; `"comfortable"` ŌåÆ `--space-3`. Horizontal padding is `--space-4` for both.
- `tone` (optional `"default" | "subtle"`, default `"default"`) ŌĆö row-hover background. `"default"` ŌåÆ `--surface`; `"subtle"` ŌåÆ `--surface-section` for tables inside section bands.
- `Table.HeaderCell` ŌĆö `<th scope="col">`. `scope="col"` is mandatory for AT column-header announcement. Typography: `--fs-meta`, `font-weight: 500`, `--lh-meta`, `--tracking-micro`, `text-transform: uppercase`, `--fg`. Bottom border: `--hairline-w solid --hairline`.
- `Table.Cell` ŌĆö `<td>`. Typography: `--fs-body`, `--lh-body`, `--fg`.
- Both `Table.HeaderCell` and `Table.Cell` accept `align` prop (`"start" | "center" | "end"`, default `"start"`). Use `"end"` for numeric columns.
- `Table.Row` hover (body context only): `background-color` transitions to `--surface` (default) or `--surface-section` (subtle) at `--dur-fast` / `--easing`. No hover on header rows.
- All sub-components forward `ref`, `className`, and `...rest` (`data-*`, `aria-*`, `style`, etc.).
- A11y: native `<table>` semantics. Pass `aria-label` or `aria-labelledby` on `<Table>` when no visible caption is present. Axe rules in play: `td-headers-attr`, `th-has-data-cells`, `scope-attr-valid`.
- No sort / filter / pagination ŌĆö those belong to the `DataTable` organism.
- Tokens consumed: `--font-sans`, `--fs-meta`, `--fs-body`, `--lh-meta`, `--lh-body`, `--tracking-micro`, `--fg`, `--fg-muted`, `--surface`, `--surface-section`, `--hairline`, `--hairline-w`, `--space-2`, `--space-3`, `--space-4`, `--dur-fast`, `--easing`.

```tsx
// Basic usage:
<Table aria-label="Team members">
  <Table.Head>
    <Table.Row>
      <Table.HeaderCell>Name</Table.HeaderCell>
      <Table.HeaderCell align="end">Joined</Table.HeaderCell>
    </Table.Row>
  </Table.Head>
  <Table.Body>
    <Table.Row>
      <Table.Cell>Arian Zargaran</Table.Cell>
      <Table.Cell align="end">2023</Table.Cell>
    </Table.Row>
  </Table.Body>
</Table>
```

---

### MetaList
- Semantic `<dl>` of label/value pairs. Canonical answer to the "key: value, stacked" pattern ŌĆö replaces ad-hoc `<p>` pairs and table-in-disguise layouts.
- Use for: article sidebars (published date, reading time, author), project spec panels (version, license, size), pricing detail blocks (billing cycle, seats, support tier).
- Root is `<dl>`; each pair is `<div><dt>ŌĆ”</dt><dd>ŌĆ”</dd></div>`. The `<div>` wrapper satisfies the HTML5 dl content model.
- Props: `items: Array<{ label: ReactNode; value: ReactNode }>` (required), `orientation: "stacked" | "horizontal"` (default `"stacked"`), `dividers: boolean` (default `false`), `labelWidth: string` (CSS value for dt column in horizontal mode, e.g. `"8rem"`).
- Typography: `--font-sans`, `--fs-meta` (14px), `--lh-meta` (1.2) on both dt and dd. Label (`dt`) uses `--fg-muted`; value (`dd`) uses `--fg`. Never bold.
- Spacing: `--space-2` row gap; `--space-3` dt-to-dd gap in horizontal mode.
- Dividers: opt-in `border-top: var(--hairline-w) solid var(--hairline)` on each row (first row has no top border).
- `margin-inline-start: 0` on `<dd>` resets browser default 40px indent without affecting semantics.
- A11y: native dl/dt/dd semantics. No ARIA additions needed. Contrast: `--fg-muted` on `--bg` = 4.56:1 (AA at 14px); `--fg` on `--bg` = 16.29:1 (AAA).
- Static/read-only. No interactive states. Interactive children (e.g. Link, StatusBadge) in value slots carry their own semantics.
- NOT for tabular data (use Table), navigation (use LinkList), forms (use Field/Fieldset), or status signals (pass StatusBadge as value ReactNode).

---

## Decision provenance

ADR-0001: Atomic design taxonomy (atoms/molecules/organisms). No fourth layer. Subpath exports are semver-governed.
ADR-0002: CSS Modules; all values via tokens. No hex, raw px, or magic numbers. tokens.css owned by poukai-design.
ADR-0003: Changesets for semver. Every PR needs a changeset. Prop removals = major. Token additions = minor.
ADR-0004: Playwright CT + axe-core. Zero a11y violations. Every component needs *.test.tsx.
ADR-0005: Ladle for showcase. Every component needs *.stories.tsx.
ADR-0006: Scope @poukai-inc/ui (GitHub Packages).
ADR-0007: vite-plugin-lib-inject-css. cssCodeSplit: true required.
ADR-0008: SiteShell Wordmark height=56. Brand floor; do not pass height < 40.
ADR-0009: Horizontal lockup is canonical. Stacked for square-format only.
ADR-0010: Proposal-loop architecture. proposal:approved label is a human gate.
ADR-0011: Wordmark gap 120 SVG units, wordtype 1.8x, isotype 1.25x. ViewBox 0 0 1184 290.
ADR-0012: MenuItem owns visual row shape only. ARIA roles, keyboard nav, portal delegated to parent Radix wrapper.


### Disclosure
- Single open/close toggle row for standalone collapsible content. Use when Accordion grouping is overkill ŌĆö "Show details" toggles, expandable card sections, optional supplementary content.
- Root element: native `<details>`. `forwardRef<HTMLDetailsElement, DisclosureProps>`. `...rest` spread. `displayName = "Disclosure"`.
- `summary` (required `string`) ŌĆö trigger label text. Rendered inside `<summary>` alongside the chevron Icon.
- `open` (optional `boolean`) ŌĆö controlled open state. Uncontrolled when omitted.
- `defaultOpen` (optional `boolean`, default `false`) ŌĆö uncontrolled initial state.
- `onOpenChange` (optional `(open: boolean) => void`) ŌĆö controlled state callback. Fires on native `<details>` toggle event.
- `divider` (optional `boolean`, default `false`) ŌĆö adds `border-top: var(--hairline-w) solid var(--hairline)` to root. For use in list/divider contexts.
- `tone` (optional `"default" | "muted"`, default `"default"`) ŌĆö `muted` applies `--fg-muted` to the summary label.
- `children` (required `ReactNode`) ŌĆö the revealed content region. Accepts any ReactNode; idiomatic usage is `<Prose>` or a plain paragraph.
- Chevron: `Icon` atom (lucide `ChevronDown`, `size="sm"`), rotates 180┬░ when open via `transition: transform var(--dur-fast) var(--easing)`. Reduced-motion handled by the global token clamp ŌĆö no per-component override.
- A11y: native `<details>` / `<summary>` pair exposes disclosure semantics. No manual ARIA needed. Focus ring on `<summary>` via `:focus-visible`.
- Tokens: `--fg`, `--fg-muted`, `--font-sans`, `--fs-body`, `--lh-body`, `--hairline`, `--hairline-w`, `--space-3`, `--space-4`, `--dur-fast`, `--easing`, `--accent`, `--radius-1`. Zero new tokens.
- Do NOT use for grouped collapsible sections ŌĆö use `Accordion`. Do NOT nest Disclosures inside Disclosures.

```tsx
// (a) Default uncontrolled:
<Disclosure summary="Show details">
  <p>Supplementary content revealed inline.</p>
</Disclosure>

// (b) Open by default:
<Disclosure summary="Advanced settings" defaultOpen>
  <p>Already expanded on mount.</p>
</Disclosure>

// (c) Divider variant in a list:
<Disclosure summary="Scope details" divider>
  <p>Week 1ŌĆō2: discovery. Week 3ŌĆō4: prototype. Week 5ŌĆō6: hand-off.</p>
</Disclosure>

// (d) Controlled:
<Disclosure summary="Configuration" open={isOpen} onOpenChange={setIsOpen}>
  <p>Controlled content.</p>
</Disclosure>
```


### NewsletterField

- Inline email + submit molecule. Native `<form>` with `Input type="email" required` and `Button type="submit"`. Supports controlled (`value` + `onValueChange`) and uncontrolled use. Optional `action` attribute for native form POST.
- Composes Input (atom), Button (atom). Root is `<form>` ŌĆö `forwardRef<HTMLFormElement, NewsletterFieldProps>`. `...rest` spread to root.
- Tokens: `--space-2`/`--space-3` for gap, `--font-sans` for type. No new tokens.
- Recommended usage: footer newsletter signup, end-of-post signup, launch waitlist. The most common inline-form pattern; ship NewsletterField before the broader form layer.
- Do NOT use as a generic form wrapper ŌĆö that is `<Form>` (organism). NewsletterField is single-purpose.

### SearchField

Molecule. Canonical table-filter and dashboard-search primitive.

**Anatomy**: `<div role="search">` root ŌåÆ `Field` wrapper (label, a11y wiring) ŌåÆ `inputWrap` flex row ŌåÆ leading `Icon` (Search, `--icon-sm`, `aria-hidden`) ŌåÆ `Input` (`type="search"`) ŌåÆ trailing `IconButton` (X, "Clear search", visible only when value non-empty).

**Props**: `value`, `defaultValue`, `onValueChange`, `placeholder` (default "SearchŌĆ”"), `size` ("sm"|"md", default "md"), `disabled`, `label` (default "Search"), `name`.

**Tokens**: `--font-sans`, `--fs-body`, `--fs-meta`, `--fg`, `--fg-muted`, `--bg`, `--surface`, `--hairline`, `--accent`, `--hairline-w`, `--radius-2`, `--icon-sm`, `--icon-xs`, `--space-2`, `--space-3`, `--btn-h-sm`, `--btn-h-md`, `--dur-fast`, `--easing`.

**A11y**: `role="search"` landmark on root; `label` always present (visually hidden via Field); leading icon `aria-hidden`; clear button `aria-label="Clear search"`; `type="search"` gives browser implicit search role; Escape key clears field.

**Anti-patterns**: Not for submit-on-enter forms (use `Input` + `<form>`). Not a Combobox. Not for multi-criteria filter state. Not a navigation search bar (use CommandPalette). Never use for non-search inputs ŌĆö `role="search"` misleads screen readers.

### NavLink

**Layer**: molecule | **File**: `src/molecules/NavLink/NavLink.tsx`

Top-nav anchor primitive. Renders a semantic `<a>` styled for primary navigation chrome. Label is `--fg-muted` at rest; shifts to `--fg` on hover with an accent underline growing via `background-size` transition (matches the global `<a>` mechanic in `tokens.css`). When `active` is true, `aria-current="page"` is set and a 2px `--accent` `border-bottom` is always visible.

**Props**: `href` (string, required) ┬Ę `active` (boolean, default false) ┬Ę `children` (ReactNode, required) ┬Ę all `AnchorHTMLAttributes<HTMLAnchorElement>` via `...rest`.

**A11y**: `aria-current="page"` when active; omitted (not `"false"`) when inactive. Parent organism owns the `<nav aria-label="Primary">` landmark ŌĆö NavLink does not.

**Tokens**: `--font-sans` `--fs-meta` `--fg` `--fg-muted` `--accent` `--hairline` `--hairline-w` `--dur-fast` `--dur-mid` `--easing` `--easing-link` `--radius-1` `--space-3`

**Do not use** for sidebar links, footer links, primary CTAs, breadcrumbs, or in-page anchors.

### StatList

Layer: molecule. Groups two or more `Stat` atoms into a shared horizontal rhythm.
Root: `<div role="list">`. Each child wrapped in `<div role="listitem">`.
Props: `dividers` (boolean, default false) ŌĆö opt-in hairline rules between items via CSS `::before` pseudo.
       `align` ("start" | "center", default "center") ŌĆö row justification within container.
       `children` (ReactNode, required) ŌĆö expects `Stat` atoms.
Tokens: `--space-8` (mobile gap), `--space-12` (desktop row gap), `--hairline`, `--hairline-w`, `--bp-md`.
Responsive: stacks column below `--bp-md` (768px); dividers become horizontal rules in stacked state.
Motion: none. Static layout shell.
A11y: `role="list"` + `role="listitem"` pair; dividers are CSS-only pseudo-elements, unreachable by AT.
Anti-patterns: single Stat, non-Stat children, nested StatLists, status indicators, form-field rows.

### Caption
- Muted micro-tracked label for supplementary attribution or contextual annotation beneath a visual or content block. Answers "what is the source or context of this artifact?" beneath figures, code blocks, audio players, and similar surfaces.
- Root element is polymorphic via `as` prop (`"figcaption"` | `"p"` | `"span"`, default `"figcaption"`). Use `"figcaption"` inside `<figure>` ŌĆö the browser natively associates it with figure content, no ARIA needed. Use `"p"` for standalone use outside a figure. Use `"span"` for inline contexts.
- `children` (required ReactNode) ŌĆö the caption text. Plain string is idiomatic. ReactNode accepted for rare inline `<a>` links; no block-level elements.
- No `tone` prop ŌĆö one visual register only. No `size` prop ŌĆö `--fs-micro` is fixed. No icon slot ŌĆö captions are text primitives.
- Typography: `--font-sans` (Geist), `--fs-micro` (12px fixed), `line-height: var(--lh-meta)` (1.2), `letter-spacing: var(--tracking-micro)` (0.04em), `color: var(--fg-muted)`.
- No padding, no margin authored by the component. Top margin (`--space-2`, 0.5rem) separating the caption from its preceding element is the consumer's responsibility.
- No states, no motion, no transitions. Caption is a non-interactive static text element. Not a tab stop.
- Contrast: `--fg-muted` on `--bg` = 4.91:1 (AA, light). `--fg-muted` on `--bg` dark = 6.10:1 (AA, dark). Passes WCAG 2.1 AA for normal text at 12px (threshold 4.5:1).
- Tokens consumed: `--font-sans`, `--fs-micro`, `--lh-meta`, `--tracking-micro`, `--fg-muted`. Zero new tokens introduced.
- Caption is NOT `<FieldNote>` (FieldNote is an editorial inline aside inside prose flow, not a below-artifact label). NOT a heading or field label. NOT for long-form body copy.

### Fieldset
- Groups related `<Field>` molecules under a shared semantic `<legend>`, enforcing consistent vertical spacing and a clear visual boundary around the group.
- Root element: `<fieldset>`. Non-polymorphic. `forwardRef<HTMLFieldSetElement, FieldsetProps>`. `...rest` spread passes `data-*`, `aria-*`, `className`, `style`, etc.
- `legend` (required string) ŌĆö group label text. Rendered as `<legend>` as first child of `<fieldset>` for correct AT announcement.
- `spacing` (optional `"default" | "spacious"`, default `"default"`) ŌĆö `"default"` ŌåÆ `--space-4` gap between fields; `"spacious"` ŌåÆ `--space-6`.
- `bordered` (optional boolean, default `false`) ŌĆö adds `--surface` background, `--hairline` border, `--radius-3` corners, `--space-8` block / `--space-6` inline padding.
- `legendTone` (optional `"default" | "muted"`, default `"default"`) ŌĆö `"muted"` renders legend in `--fg-muted` for secondary sections.
- `disabled` (optional boolean, default `false`) ŌĆö maps to native `<fieldset disabled>`, propagating disabled state to all descendant form controls automatically.
- `children` (required ReactNode) ŌĆö `<Field>` or `<FormRow>` molecules.
- A11y: native `<fieldset>` + `<legend>` provides `role="group"` with accessible name ŌĆö no `aria-labelledby` needed. `<legend>` must remain first child.
- Layout: fields container is `display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: var(--space-4)` (default) or `var(--space-6)` (spacious).
- Legend typography: `--fs-meta`, `font-weight: 500`, `--fg` (or `--fg-muted` when `legendTone="muted"`), `--lh-meta`, `margin-bottom: var(--space-1)`.
- Tokens consumed: `--font-sans`, `--fs-meta`, `--lh-meta`, `--fg`, `--fg-muted`, `--surface`, `--hairline`, `--hairline-w`, `--radius-3`, `--space-1`, `--space-4`, `--space-6`, `--space-8`.
- Do NOT use Fieldset for a single `<Field>` ŌĆö no semantic benefit.
- Do NOT use Fieldset as a general-purpose bordered box for non-form content.
- Do NOT use Fieldset as a page section (`<Section>`) or heading replacement.

```tsx
// (a) Billing address group:
<Fieldset legend="Billing address">
  <Field label="Street" id="street"><Input /></Field>
  <Field label="City" id="city"><Input /></Field>
  <Field label="Postal code" id="postal"><Input /></Field>
</Fieldset>

// (b) Bordered payment block:
<Fieldset legend="Payment details" bordered spacing="spacious">
  <Field label="Card number" id="card"><Input /></Field>
  <Field label="Expiry" id="expiry"><Input /></Field>
</Fieldset>

// (c) Disabled group (all fields disabled automatically):
<Fieldset legend="Shipping address" disabled>
  <Field label="Street" id="street"><Input /></Field>
</Fieldset>
```

### HoverCard

- Radix-wrapped hover/focus-triggered preview card. Compound API: `HoverCard.Root`, `HoverCard.Trigger` (use `asChild` to project onto Link or any anchor), `HoverCard.Content`.
- Built on `@radix-ui/react-hover-card`. Runtime-destructured Radix imports (see `Toast` for the pattern) avoid CT bundler collisions.
- Side / align driven by props (`side`, `align`); width via `--width-card-sm` / `--width-card-md` per spec.
- Open / close delays via `--dur-hover-open` / `--dur-hover-close` tokens. Enter/exit motion behind `prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference` ŌĆö reduced-motion collapses to instant show/hide.
- Content portals to `document.body`; consumers must inspect via `page.locator(...)` in tests, not the mounted root.
- Use for link-preview cards: author hover, project preview, profile mentions. Not for click-triggered panels ŌĆö that is `Popover`.
- Do NOT nest interactive controls inside `HoverCard.Content` ŌĆö touch / keyboard users cannot reach them. For interactive overlays use `Popover`.


### Popover

- Anchored, dismissible floating-content molecule. Wraps `@radix-ui/react-popover`. Use for filter pickers, inline action panels, form field helpers ŌĆö not for dialogs, tooltips, or navigation.
- Compound API: `<Popover.Root>` / `<Popover.Trigger>` / `<Popover.Content>` / `<Popover.Close>`. Content always renders in a portal (never clipped by `overflow:hidden`).
- `Popover.Root` props: `open`, `defaultOpen`, `onOpenChange`, `modal` (default `false`).
- `Popover.Content` props: `side` (`"top"|"right"|"bottom"|"left"`, default `"bottom"`), `align` (`"start"|"center"|"end"`, default `"center"`), `sideOffset` (default `8`), `arrow` (renders caret, default `false`). All `div` props (`className`, `data-*`, `aria-*`, etc.) forwarded to the panel root.
- Trigger supports `asChild` ŌĆö any DS atom (Button, IconButton, `<a>`) becomes the anchor.
- Close supports `asChild` ŌĆö pass `aria-label="Close"` when rendering an icon-only button.
- Tokens: `--bg-elevated` (panel bg), `--hairline` + `--hairline-w` (border), `--radius-3` (corners), `--space-4` (padding), `--fg` (text), `--easing` + `--dur-fast` (enter animation), ease-in + `--dur-fast` (exit). Reduced-motion handled by global `tokens.css` rule.
- A11y: Radix manages `aria-haspopup="dialog"`, `aria-expanded`, `role="dialog"` on Content. Pass `aria-label` on Content when no visible heading is present.
- Do NOT use Popover as a Dialog (blocking / backdrop ŌåÆ use `<Dialog>`). Do NOT use for hover-only reveals (`HoverCard`). Do NOT use for single-line hints (`Tooltip`).


### Tooltip

- System hover-hint primitive. Wraps `@radix-ui/react-tooltip` for portal rendering, auto-flip, delay, and keyboard semantics (focus opens, Escape closes).
- Two usage forms: (1) shorthand `<Tooltip content="label"><trigger /></Tooltip>` and (2) compound `<Tooltip.Root>` / `<Tooltip.Trigger>` / `<Tooltip.Content>`.
- `TooltipProvider` wraps the app root once; controls `delayDuration` (default 700ms) and `skipDelayDuration` (default 300ms) globally.
- `Tooltip.Trigger` is always `asChild` ŌĆö no extra DOM node; composes onto any existing element.
- `Tooltip.Content` is portalled via `<RadixTooltipPortal>` ŌĆö renders above all layers. Radix assigns `role="tooltip"` and wires `aria-describedby` automatically.
- Props: `content` (ReactNode, shorthand label), `side` (`"top"|"right"|"bottom"|"left"`, default `"top"`), `sideOffset` (number px, default `6`), `delayDuration` (number ms), `open`, `defaultOpen`, `onOpenChange`.
- A11y: `role="tooltip"` on Content, `aria-describedby` from trigger wired by Radix. Trigger must carry its own `aria-label` for icon-only buttons ŌĆö Tooltip supplements but does not replace it. Non-interactive content only (no buttons/links inside).
- Motion: fade + scale(0.96ŌåÆ1) on open; reverse on close. Duration: `--dur-fast` (180ms), easing: `--easing` (expo-out). Global `tokens.css` reduced-motion block clamps to 0.01ms ŌĆö no per-component override needed.
- Tokens: `--bg-elevated`, `--fg`, `--hairline`, `--hairline-w`, `--radius-2`, `--font-sans`, `--fs-meta`, `--lh-meta`, `--space-2`, `--space-3`, `--dur-fast`, `--easing`.
- Do NOT put interactive content (buttons, links, inputs) inside Tooltip ŌĆö use Popover. Do NOT use as the sole label for a control. Do NOT use for long-form content (>80 chars).

```tsx
// Shorthand form (common icon-button case):
<TooltipProvider>
  <Tooltip content="Settings">
    <IconButton aria-label="Settings"><Settings /></IconButton>
  </Tooltip>
</TooltipProvider>

// Compound form (full control):
<TooltipProvider>
  <Tooltip.Root delayDuration={0}>
    <Tooltip.Trigger><button>Hover me</button></Tooltip.Trigger>
    <Tooltip.Content side="bottom">Custom label</Tooltip.Content>
  </Tooltip.Root>
</TooltipProvider>
```


### Figure
- Semantic pairing molecule for an image (or Portrait) and its caption. Wraps a media slot and an optional `<figcaption>` inside a `<figure>` element.
- Root element: `<figure>`. Non-polymorphic. `forwardRef<HTMLElement, FigureProps>`. `...rest` spread passes `data-*`, `aria-*`, `className`, `style`, etc.
- `children` (required ReactNode) ŌĆö media content: `Portrait`, raw `<img>`, `CodeBlock`, or any block-level media. Figure does not constrain slot content.
- `caption` (optional ReactNode) ŌĆö shorthand caption rendered in `<figcaption>`. Omit when no caption needed.
- `align` (optional `"start" | "center"`, default `"start"`) ŌĆö caption text-alignment. `"start"` for inline editorial; `"center"` for standalone figure moments.
- `className` (optional string) ŌĆö merged onto root `<figure>`.
- Compound API: `Figure.Caption` sub-component accepts `children: ReactNode` and `...rest` forwarded to `<figcaption>`. Use when caption content is richer than a plain string.
- A11y: `<figure>` carries implicit `figure` ARIA role ŌĆö no additional `role` needed. `<figcaption>` is automatically associated with parent `<figure>` by browsers. Media slot must supply its own `alt` text; Figure does not synthesise it.
- Static. No hover, focus, or motion states.
- Tokens consumed: `--font-sans`, `--fs-micro`, `--lh-meta`, `--tracking-micro`, `--fg-muted`, `--space-2`, `--space-4`.

```tsx
// (a) Shorthand caption:
<Figure caption="Photographer: Jane Doe">
  <img src="/photo.jpg" alt="Team at offsite, March 2024" />
</Figure>

// (b) Compound Caption sub-component:
<Figure>
  <img src="/photo.jpg" alt="Team at offsite" />
  <Figure.Caption>Photo by <em>Jane Doe</em>, 2024.</Figure.Caption>
</Figure>

// (c) Centered caption for standalone figure moment:
<Figure align="center" caption="Figure 1. Deployment cycle comparison.">
  <img src="/chart.png" alt="Bar chart comparing deployment cycles" />
</Figure>
```


### CopyButton
- Copy-to-clipboard button with transient success state. Surfaces inline on product pages ŌĆö API key displays, code snippets, share URLs. Composes into `CodeBlock` as its built-in copy control.
- Root element: `<button type="button">`. Non-polymorphic. `forwardRef<HTMLButtonElement, CopyButtonProps>`. `...rest` spread passes `data-*`, `aria-*`, `className`, etc.
- State machine: idle ŌåÆ success ŌåÆ (after `timeout` ms) ŌåÆ idle. Revert is cancelled and restarted on repeated clicks during the success window.
- `value` (required string) ŌĆö the string written to the clipboard.
- `label` (optional `string | false`, default `"Copy"`) ŌĆö visible label. Pass `false` for icon-only; **must** supply `aria-label` on root when omitting label.
- `successLabel` (optional string, default `"Copied"`) ŌĆö label shown during success state.
- `timeout` (optional number, default `1500`) ŌĆö ms before reverting from success to idle.
- `size` (optional `"sm" | "md"`, default `"sm"`) ŌĆö `sm` uses `--fs-meta` + `--icon-xs`; `md` uses `--fs-body` + `--icon-sm`.
- `onCopy` (optional `(value: string) => void`) ŌĆö fired after successful clipboard write.
- `onError` (optional `(err: unknown) => void`) ŌĆö called when clipboard write rejects; component stays idle.
- Clipboard: uses `navigator.clipboard.writeText`; falls back to `document.execCommand('copy')` via temporary textarea; calls `onError` if both fail.
- A11y: root is native `<button type="button">`. Icons are `aria-hidden`. Label node uses `aria-live="polite"` so "Copied" is announced on success. Idle `--fg-muted` on `--bg`: 4.91:1 AA; success `--fg` on `--bg`: 16.29:1 AAA. Min-height: `--btn-h-sm` (32px), meets WCAG 2.5.8.
- Tokens: `--font-sans`, `--fs-meta`, `--fs-body`, `--fg-muted`, `--fg`, `--accent`, `--surface`, `--radius-2`, `--radius-1`, `--space-1`, `--space-2`, `--btn-h-sm`, `--dur-fast`, `--dur-press`, `--easing`, `--icon-xs`, `--icon-sm`, `--lh-meta`.
- Do NOT use as a primary CTA. Do NOT omit `aria-label` when `label={false}`. Do NOT use for rich content, async actions, or destructive flows.

```tsx
// (a) Default ŌĆö label + icon beside a code snippet:
<CopyButton value="npm install @poukai-inc/ui" />

// (b) Icon-only inside an API key readout (aria-label required):
<CopyButton value={apiKey} label={false} aria-label="Copy API key" size="sm" />

// (c) With callbacks for analytics + error handling:
<CopyButton
  value={shareUrl}
  label="Copy link"
  successLabel="Copied!"
  onCopy={(v) => analytics.track("copy", { value: v })}
  onError={(err) => toast.error("Could not copy")}
/>
```


### Pagination
- Stateless, controlled page-navigation control. Renders `<nav aria-label="Pagination">` with first/prev/page-numerals/next/last buttons. Returns `null` when `pageCount <= 1`.
- Root element: `<nav>`. `forwardRef<HTMLElement, PaginationProps>`. `...rest` spread passes `data-*`, `aria-*`, `className`, etc.
- `page` (required number, 1-indexed) ŌĆö current page.
- `pageCount` (required number) ŌĆö total page count.
- `onPageChange` (required `(page: number) => void`) ŌĆö controlled; component holds no state.
- `siblingCount` (optional number, default `1`) ŌĆö pages shown each side of current page.
- `boundaryCount` (optional number, default `1`) ŌĆö pages shown at each end.
- `size` (optional `"sm" | "md"`, default `"md"`) ŌĆö `"sm"` uses `--fs-micro` + `--btn-h-sm`; `"md"` uses `--fs-meta` + `--btn-h-compact`.
- `disabled` (optional boolean, default `false`) ŌĆö disables all controls (loading state).
- A11y: `<nav aria-label="Pagination">` landmark. Current page: `aria-current="page"`. Prev/Next/First/Last: `aria-label` for icon-only accessible names. Ellipsis: `aria-hidden="true"`. Disabled uses native `disabled` attribute.
- Tokens consumed: `--font-sans`, `--fs-meta`, `--fs-micro`, `--fg`, `--fg-muted`, `--surface`, `--bg-elevated`, `--hairline`, `--hairline-w`, `--accent`, `--radius-1`, `--radius-2`, `--space-1`, `--space-2`, `--btn-h-sm`, `--btn-h-compact`, `--dur-fast`, `--dur-press`, `--easing`.
- Do NOT use for in-page anchor navigation ŌĆö use `TableOfContents`.
- Do NOT use as a substitute for infinite scroll.
- Do NOT render when `pageCount <= 1` ŌĆö the component guards this internally.
- Do NOT manage page state internally ŌĆö this is a controlled component.

```tsx
// (a) Basic usage:
<Pagination page={currentPage} pageCount={10} onPageChange={setCurrentPage} />

// (b) Compact size for dense dashboard rows:
<Pagination page={page} pageCount={20} size="sm" onPageChange={setPage} />

// (c) Loading state ŌĆö all controls disabled:
<Pagination page={page} pageCount={10} disabled onPageChange={setPage} />

// (d) Wide layout ŌĆö more siblings:
<Pagination page={5} pageCount={20} siblingCount={2} onPageChange={setPage} />
```


---

### EmptyState

**Layer**: molecule | **File**: `src/molecules/EmptyState/`

Zero-data placeholder ŌĆö shown when a list, table, or content region has no records. Centered vertical stack: optional decorative icon, required title `<p>`, optional description `<p>`, optional action slot.

**Props**: `title: string` (required) ┬Ę `icon?: ReactNode` ┬Ę `description?: string | ReactNode` ┬Ę `action?: ReactNode` ┬Ę `tone?: "default" | "subtle"` (default `"default"`) ┬Ę all `<div>` HTML attributes forwarded to root.

**Tokens used**: `--fs-body` ┬Ę `--font-sans` ┬Ę `--fg` (title) ┬Ę `--fg-muted` (description, icon) ┬Ę `--lh-body` ┬Ę `--space-2` (titleŌåÆdescription gap) ┬Ę `--space-3` (iconŌåÆtitle gap) ┬Ę `--space-6` (descriptionŌåÆaction gap) ┬Ę `--space-16` (vertical padding) ┬Ę `--surface` + `--radius-3` (tone="subtle").

**A11y**: root is plain `<div>` (not `role="alert"`). Icon wrapper is `aria-hidden="true"`. Title is `<p>` ŌĆö not a heading; document outline is consumer responsibility. Inline links in description inherit global focus ring.

**Anti-patterns**: do not use for loading/error states ┬Ę do not omit `title` ┬Ę do not hardcode color on icon ┬Ę do not use as full-page first-run surface.

```tsx
// (a) Title only:
<EmptyState title="No scheduled posts" />

// (b) Full anatomy:
<EmptyState
  title="No scheduled posts"
  description="Schedule your first post to start building your content calendar."
  icon={<CalendarIcon width={24} height={24} aria-hidden="true" />}
  action={<Button variant="primary" size="md">Schedule a post</Button>}
/>

// (c) Inside a card (subtle tone):
<EmptyState tone="subtle" title="No conversations yet" description="Messages appear here." />
```

### Breadcrumb
- Hierarchical location trail molecule. Renders the route path back through nested dashboard and product pages.
- Root element: `<nav aria-label="Breadcrumb">`. `forwardRef<HTMLElement, BreadcrumbProps>`. `...rest` spread. `displayName = "Breadcrumb"`.
- Contains an `<ol>` of `<li>` items with presentational `aria-hidden="true"` separator `<li>` elements interspersed.
- Two usage patterns: compound `<Breadcrumb.Item>` children (preferred) or data-driven `items` prop array.
- `items` (optional `Array<{ href?: string; label: ReactNode; current?: boolean }>`) ŌĆö data-driven path. Mutually exclusive with compound children.
- `children` (optional `ReactNode`) ŌĆö compound `<Breadcrumb.Item>` nodes.
- `separator` (optional `ReactNode`, default `"ŌĆ║"`) ŌĆö rendered between items; each separator `<li>` carries `aria-hidden="true"`.
- `className` (optional string) ŌĆö layout override passthrough to root `<nav>`.
- `Breadcrumb.Item` sub-component: `href` (optional string), `current` (optional boolean), `children` (required ReactNode). When `current` is true: plain `<span>`, `aria-current="page"` on the `<li>`, no link.
- A11y: `<nav aria-label="Breadcrumb">` = distinct navigation landmark. `<ol>` = ordered hierarchy. `aria-current="page"` on current item. Separators `aria-hidden="true"`. Ancestor links have visible text accessible name.
- Tokens: `--font-sans`, `--fs-meta`, `--lh-meta`, `--fg-muted` (ancestor link rest), `--fg` (current item), `--accent` (hover color + focus ring), `--hairline` (separator + link underline), `--hairline-w`, `--space-2` (gap), `--dur-fast`, `--dur-mid`, `--easing`, `--easing-link`, `--radius-1`.
- Static presentational. No state. No JS-driven truncation in v1.
- Do NOT link the current (last) item ŌĆö it is always plain text.
- Do NOT use for tab navigation or wizard steps ŌĆö use `Tabs` or `Stepper`.
- Do NOT render when depth < 2 (single-item trail has no navigational value).

```tsx
// (a) Compound ŌĆö 3-level trail:
<Breadcrumb>
  <Breadcrumb.Item href="/dashboard">Dashboard</Breadcrumb.Item>
  <Breadcrumb.Item href="/dashboard/pages">Pages</Breadcrumb.Item>
  <Breadcrumb.Item current>Settings</Breadcrumb.Item>
</Breadcrumb>

// (b) Data-driven alias:
<Breadcrumb
  items={[
    { href: "/dashboard", label: "Dashboard" },
    { href: "/dashboard/pages", label: "Pages" },
    { label: "Settings", current: true },
  ]}
/>

// (c) Custom separator:
<Breadcrumb separator="/">
  <Breadcrumb.Item href="/dashboard">Dashboard</Breadcrumb.Item>
  <Breadcrumb.Item current>Settings</Breadcrumb.Item>
</Breadcrumb>
```

### ShareLinks
- Compact share-action row for editorial surfaces (articles, case studies). Horizontal flex row of `IconButton` entries for X and LinkedIn, plus a `CopyButton` for copy-URL. Replaces the entire row with a single native-share button when `navigator.share` is available.
- Root element: `<div role="group" aria-label="Share this article">`. `forwardRef<HTMLDivElement, ShareLinksProps>`. `...rest` spread. `displayName = "ShareLinks"`.
- `url` (required string) ŌĆö the URL to share or copy.
- `title` (optional string, default `""`) ŌĆö passed to `navigator.share` and pre-filled in intent URLs.
- `networks` (optional `Array<"x" | "linkedin" | "copy">`, default `["x","linkedin","copy"]`) ŌĆö which networks to show and in what order.
- `size` (optional `"sm" | "md"`, default `"md"`) ŌĆö maps to `IconButton` size prop.
- `className` (optional string) ŌĆö forwarded to root `<div>` for layout overrides.
- Native share fast-path: when `navigator.share` is defined, renders a single `IconButton` (share icon, `aria-label="Share"`) that calls `navigator.share({ url, title })`; rejection (user cancel) is silently swallowed.
- Network buttons: X ŌåÆ `window.open(xIntentUrl, "_blank", "noopener,noreferrer")`; LinkedIn ŌåÆ same with LinkedIn share URL. `aria-label` is `"Share on X"` / `"Share on LinkedIn"`.
- Copy slot: `CopyButton` with `value={url}`, `label={false}`, `aria-label="Copy link"`. Success-state owned by `CopyButton`.
- A11y: `role="group"` groups controls without adding a redundant landmark. Each button has an explicit `aria-label`. Icons are decorative (`aria-hidden`). `CopyButton` owns its own live-region announcement.
- Tokens: `--space-2` (row gap), `--fg-muted` (icon rest), `--fg` (icon hover/focus), `--accent` (focus ring), `--dur-fast`, `--easing`, `--radius-1`, `--hairline-w` ŌĆö all via `IconButton`.
- Do NOT use in site headers, sidebars, or dashboards. Do NOT use for authenticated sharing flows. Do NOT use as a replacement for a standalone `CopyButton`.

```tsx
// (a) Default ŌĆö all three networks:
<ShareLinks url="https://poukai.com/blog/post" title="My Post" />

// (b) LinkedIn + copy only, compact size:
<ShareLinks url={url} networks={["linkedin", "copy"]} size="sm" />

// (c) X only:
<ShareLinks url={url} networks={["x"]} />
```

### AnnouncementBar

- **Layer**: organism
- **File**: `src/organisms/AnnouncementBar/AnnouncementBar.tsx`
- **Purpose**: Page-top full-width dismissable banner for product announcements, maintenance notices, and time-sensitive promotions. Renders above the `Header` organism. Dismissal state persisted via `localStorage` keyed by `id`. SSR-safe: renders visible by default; hides post-hydration if already dismissed.
- **Props**: `id` (required string, localStorage key), `tone` (`"warm" | "neutral" | "success" | "danger" | "warning"`, default `"warm"`), `dismissable` (boolean, default `true`), `action` (ReactNode, optional inline link/button slot), `children` (ReactNode, required ŌĆö announcement copy), `onDismiss` (() => void, optional callback after dismissal).
- **Tokens**: `--bg-warm-accent`, `--fg-on-warm`, `--surface`, `--fg`, `--bg-success`, `--fg-on-success`, `--bg-danger`, `--fg-on-danger`, `--bg-warning`, `--fg-on-warning`, `--hairline`, `--hairline-w`, `--font-sans`, `--fs-meta`, `--lh-meta`, `--space-2`, `--space-4`, `--space-6`, `--page-pad`, `--content-max`, `--dur-fast`, `--easing`.
- A11y: `role="region" aria-label="Announcement"`. Dismiss button carries `aria-label="Dismiss announcement"`. After dismissal element is removed from DOM (not visibility-hidden). Contrast: warm tone `--fg-on-warm` on `--bg-warm-accent` Ōēź 4.5:1 AA; neutral `--fg` on `--surface` 15.46:1 AAA.
- Do NOT use for cookie consent, inline form feedback, toasts, or multiple simultaneous banners.

```tsx
// Default warm tone:
<AnnouncementBar id="launch-q2-2026" tone="warm">
  We're launching Phase 2 ŌĆö new components, new primitives.
</AnnouncementBar>

// With action slot:
<AnnouncementBar id="launch-q2-2026" tone="warm" action={<a href="/blog">Read more</a>}>
  Phase 2 is now live.
</AnnouncementBar>

// Non-dismissable mandatory notice:
<AnnouncementBar id="maintenance-2026-06" tone="danger" dismissable={false}>
  Critical maintenance in progress. Read-only mode is active.
</AnnouncementBar>
```


### TableOfContents
- Sticky anchor list molecule for long-form article right-rail navigation. Tracks scroll position via IntersectionObserver; highlights the currently visible section.
- Root element: `<nav aria-label="Table of contents">`. `forwardRef<HTMLElement, TableOfContentsProps>`. `...rest` spread. `displayName = "TableOfContents"`.
- `items` (required `Array<{ id: string; label: string; depth?: 1 | 2 }>`) ŌĆö anchor list. Each `id` must resolve to a heading element on the page.
- `heading` (optional string) ŌĆö "On this page" label above the list. Rendered as `<p>` (not a heading element ŌĆö avoids polluting the document outline). Font: `--fs-micro`, uppercase, `--fg-muted`.
- `activeId` (optional string) ŌĆö controlled active item. When provided, overrides IntersectionObserver.
- `onActiveChange` (optional `(id: string) => void`) ŌĆö escape hatch for controlled consumers.
- `offset` (optional number, default `0`) ŌĆö pixel offset for IntersectionObserver `rootMargin` top. Match sticky header height.
- `depth=2` items indent by `--space-4` and render at `--fs-micro` to signal sub-section hierarchy. Max supported depth: 2.
- Active item: `aria-current="true"` on anchor, `color: var(--accent)`, left-border pseudo-element at `opacity: 1`. Inactive: `color: var(--fg-muted)`. Transition: `color var(--dur-fast) var(--easing)`.
- Sticky positioning: `position: sticky; top: var(--toc-offset, var(--space-8))`. Consumer sets `--toc-offset` to match shell header height.
- A11y: named nav landmark, `aria-current="true"` on active anchor, native `<ul>/<li>/<a>` semantics.
- Tokens: `--fg-muted`, `--fg`, `--accent`, `--fs-meta`, `--fs-micro`, `--font-sans`, `--lh-meta`, `--space-1`, `--space-2`, `--space-3`, `--space-4`, `--space-8`, `--hairline-w`, `--dur-fast`, `--easing`, `--tracking-micro`, `--radius-1`.

```tsx
// Uncontrolled (IntersectionObserver drives active state):
<TableOfContents
  heading="On this page"
  items={[
    { id: "intro", label: "Introduction" },
    { id: "approach", label: "Approach" },
    { id: "methodology", label: "Methodology", depth: 2 },
    { id: "results", label: "Results" },
  ]}
/>

// Controlled:
<TableOfContents
  items={sections}
  activeId={currentSection}
  onActiveChange={setCurrentSection}
/>
```


### Sidebar
- Vertical navigation surface organism for docs layouts and app shells. Presents grouped hierarchical link lists in a persistent left-rail position.
- Root element: `<aside aria-label={label}>` containing `<nav aria-label={label}>`. `forwardRef<HTMLElement, SidebarProps>`. `...rest` spread. `displayName = "Sidebar"`.
- Compound API: `Sidebar` (root) + `Sidebar.Group` (repeating group unit wrapping a heading + LinkList).
- `label` (optional string, default `"Sidebar"`) ŌĆö `aria-label` for both `<aside>` and `<nav>`; override for locale or multi-sidebar pages.
- `sticky` (optional boolean, default `true`) ŌĆö enables `position: sticky; top: var(--space-12); height: fit-content; max-height: calc(100vh - var(--space-12)); overflow-y: auto`.
- `children` (ReactNode) ŌĆö one or more `Sidebar.Group` elements.
- `Sidebar.Group` sub-component: `heading` (optional string) ŌĆö renders a Heading atom at h3 rung; omit to suppress heading entirely. `collapsible` (optional boolean, default `false`) ŌĆö wraps LinkList in a Disclosure. `defaultOpen` (optional boolean, default `true`) ŌĆö initial open state when collapsible. `children` (ReactNode) ŌĆö `LinkList.Item` elements.
- A11y: `<aside aria-label>` = complementary landmark. `<nav aria-label>` = navigation landmark. Group headings are real `<h3>` elements. `aria-current="page"` on active links via LinkList.Item `current` prop. Collapsible uses native `<details>`/`<summary>` semantics.
- Tokens: `--bg`, `--fg`, `--fg-muted`, `--font-sans`, `--fs-h3`, `--fs-meta`, `--hairline`, `--hairline-w`, `--space-2`, `--space-4`, `--space-8`, `--space-12`, `--accent`, `--radius-1`, `--dur-fast`.
- Static presentational. No client state except Disclosure toggle (native details). No router awareness ŌĆö consumer passes `aria-current="page"` via LinkList.Item `current` prop.
- Do NOT use for mobile off-canvas drawers (use Sheet/Drawer). Do NOT use for primary site nav (use SiteShell header). Do NOT use for Table of Contents (use TableOfContents organism).

```tsx
// (a) Default grouped sidebar:
<Sidebar>
  <Sidebar.Group heading="Getting started">
    <LinkList.Item href="/docs/intro">Introduction</LinkList.Item>
    <LinkList.Item href="/docs/install" current>Installation</LinkList.Item>
  </Sidebar.Group>
  <Sidebar.Group heading="Components">
    <LinkList.Item href="/docs/button">Button</LinkList.Item>
  </Sidebar.Group>
</Sidebar>

// (b) Non-sticky (fixed-height scroll container):
<Sidebar sticky={false} label="App navigation">
  <Sidebar.Group heading="Settings">
    <LinkList.Item href="/settings/profile">Profile</LinkList.Item>
  </Sidebar.Group>
</Sidebar>

// (c) Collapsible groups:
<Sidebar>
  <Sidebar.Group heading="Advanced" collapsible defaultOpen={false}>
    <LinkList.Item href="/docs/theming">Theming</LinkList.Item>
  </Sidebar.Group>
</Sidebar>
```

### TimePicker
- Time-of-day input atom. Styled wrapper around native `<input type="time">`. Produces a valid `HH:mm` string. Compose with `<Field>` for label + error anatomy.
- Root element: `<input type="time">`. `forwardRef<HTMLInputElement, TimePickerProps>`. `...rest` spread. `displayName = "TimePicker"`.
- `value` (optional string) ŌĆö controlled `HH:mm` value. `defaultValue` (optional string) ŌĆö uncontrolled seed. `onValueChange` ((value: string) => void) ŌĆö fires on `change`; provides current `input.value`. `step` (optional number, default `60`) ŌĆö seconds; `900` = 15-min steps. `min` / `max` (optional string `"HH:mm"`) ŌĆö time bounds. `disabled` (optional boolean, default `false`) ŌĆö dims + blocks. `invalid` (optional boolean, default `false`) ŌĆö red border via `--danger`. `size` (`"sm" | "md" | "lg"`, default `"md"`) ŌĆö maps to `--btn-h-*` height ladder, matching Input atom.
- A11y: native `<input type="time">` semantics. `aria-invalid="true"` when `invalid`. Consumer passes `id` for `<label for="ŌĆ”">` association. `aria-describedby` forwarded via spread.
- Tokens: `--font-sans`, `--fs-body`, `--fg`, `--fg-muted`, `--bg`, `--surface`, `--hairline`, `--accent`, `--accent-glow`, `--danger`, `--radius-2`, `--space-2`, `--space-3`, `--btn-h-sm`, `--btn-h-md`, `--btn-h-lg`, `--dur-fast`, `--easing`.
- No Radix dependency. Static presentational ŌĆö no internal state.

```tsx
// (a) Controlled inside Field:
<Field label="Preferred time" id="pref-time">
  <TimePicker id="pref-time" value={time} onValueChange={setTime} />
</Field>

// (b) 15-minute steps:
<TimePicker step={900} onValueChange={setTime} aria-label="Schedule time" />

// (c) With bounds:
<TimePicker min="08:00" max="20:00" onValueChange={setTime} aria-label="Working hours" />

### BlogPostCard
- Organism layer. Single blog-post preview card ŌĆö minimal stub pending approved BlogPostCard spec.
- Root element: `<article>`. `forwardRef<HTMLElement, BlogPostCardProps>`. `...rest` spread. `displayName = "BlogPostCard"`.
- Props: `title` (required `ReactNode`), `lede` (optional `ReactNode`), `date` (optional string), `author` (optional string), `tags` (optional string), `href` (optional string).
- When `href` is provided, title is wrapped in `<a href>` for navigation. Without `href`, title is plain text.
- Meta row (`<footer>`) renders only when at least one of `date`, `author`, or `tags` is provided.
- A11y: `<article>` landmark. Title in `<h2>`. Date in `<time>`. All text inherits `--fg` / `--fg-muted` cascade.
- Tokens: `--font-sans`, `--fs-h3`, `--fs-body`, `--fs-meta`, `--lh-meta`, `--lh-body`, `--fg`, `--fg-muted`, `--space-2`.
